# Thread: Marshall's journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/marshalls-journal.20872/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### marshall mueller — 2018-02-14T08:39:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/marshalls-journal.20872/post-234715>

Hi . I'm brand new to Jack's work. Jumping in with both feet.
I'm 70 years old. 5'9" 145 lbs. light skin, red hair. I am currently residing temporarily in an apartment in Pasadena CA. My home is in Washougal Washington, but my wife and I are volunteering to be nearly full time babysitters for my 3 year old granddaughter here in Pasadena
The health issue I'm most concerned with is tinnitus and headaches. I've had tinnitus for about 30 years and the headaches for about 25 years. When the T first started I tried lots of stuff none of which worked. Acupuncture, herbs, diet, ayahuasca, meditation - - etc. etc. I finally resigned myself to it and tried just to live with it. Now both are getting worse and I've got to do something -- I'm often completely slammed by the tinnitus especially and am unable to function well.

Medications. I'm on no prescription meds. I take various supplements. vit C, B's iodine, trace minerals, coconut oil, red palm oil, C-60, magnesium citrate, ashwaganda.

Other issues. I notice I'm more forgetful than I ever was. My brain is often kind of foggy. My overall energy
level is pretty good for a 70 year old I guess, but it's slipping. Arthritis in a number of joints - - both thumbs, one big toe, a couple of fingers. Nothing really debilitating yet but not good signs.

Medical procedures. Had a hip replacement last year. completely degraded by arthritis. no complications and it's working very well. Had both eyes done for cataracts - one in 2009 and one in 2003. Unfortunately the implants are the type that filter UV. Asked my eye doctor today if they could be replaced - - heavy advice against - -(no surprise) Not sure how I will get the right lights into my body. am searching the website for some direction.

Family history . grandmothers both lived to nearly 100 and as far as I know had no big health issues. Mother died at 33 of colon cancer, and had ulcerative colitis and severe arthritis. Grandfathers both died in their mid 60s. Paternal died of heart attack, maternal of stroke. Father quite healthy for most of his life. prostate cancer in his late 70s. got radiation but it came back and he died at 83.

General life style and diet. I've done organic for 40 years. Tried vegan for a while 30 years ago but seemed to get anemic. Now I do lots of homemade probiotics including raw milk kefir, grass fed meat (not a lot) some eggs, nuts and seeds and lots of veggies. Not much carbs --occasional german rye bread toast with grass fed butter is about all I do. I'm looking into seafood. I haven't eaten it much in my life and never really loved it that much. I have been quite concerned with the toxins in it so I never felt I was missing anything. JK has changed my mind on that and I'm searching for a reliable source of clean seafood.

I should exercise more. I do a good brisk walk pretty much every day and when I'm home in Washington I walk in my neighborhood which has quite steep hills.

I'm just beginning with the work on emf's, sunlight etc. I have limited my cell phone and installed a shield on it, hard wired my computers at home, have RO water filter, wear blue blockers religiously, have been sunning lots especially in
the am.

# Thread: sun on rainy days?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-on-rainy-days.20873/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### taiyang — 2018-02-14T12:34:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-on-rainy-days.20873/post-234723>

for me health is all about setting up habits. what are everyone's habits for getting sun exposure on rainy days? do you go out with an umbrella? the older i get, the more dependent i am on getting the sun to help me sleep. other people can sleep well and not get sun, but i can't.

### Lahelada — 2018-02-14T15:09:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-on-rainy-days.20873/post-234726>

What are your glasses prescription? Mine are -4.25. I need to get strong sun without glasses on.I was perfect and losing weight when I was on the water sailing everyday.
Whenever you go out ,if you do not already do it ,take off your glasses.
Going out in the rain is great if you just go out for a walk. I never take an umbrella. I just get wet and after the initial Brr it is a very nice way of getting some mini CT in
If for any reason you cannot go out, can you sit by an open window? It is the least favourite option to be used in case of "can´t " not in case of "won´t"

### taiyang — 2018-02-15T00:31:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-on-rainy-days.20873/post-234766>

since finding this site, i always walk around without glasses on (unless safety issue or really want to see something clearly). i have had glasses since 5 years old and have extremely poor vision.

### Dean6789 — 2018-02-15T00:50:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butter-poll.20875/post-234769>

Some of the butter out there that many thought was 100% grass fed, is now being reported as not 100% grass fed... and may even have GMO's.

In that light, I'm wondering:

1\. What brand of 100% grass fed raw butter is everyone eating these days?

2\. Where/whom do you purchase it from?

3\. Why do you like this brand over others available?

Thanks for your participation!

### Allin — 2018-02-15T00:52:06+0200

Kerrygold - Pure Irish Butter

### Jonathin — 2018-02-15T02:24:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butter-poll.20875/post-234782>

Organic Valley - green label

### Brent Patrick — 2018-02-15T09:56:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butter-poll.20875/post-234799>

As important Grass fed butter is the Zip Code in which the Cream for the Butter is produced is paramount!

Interesting that Kerrygold may not be as pure as we thought.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butter-poll.20875/post-235224>

I can it locally, from a local co-op, but it comes from a farmer 3,000 mile away. Not optimal but at least it's raw and actually is 100% grass fed.

### Martha Ray — 2020-12-20T03:20:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butter-poll.20875/post-293956>

I have not yet found a butter made 100% grass fed where I am in Central VA. The Amish near here may be a source but I've not found one to buy _**yet**_.

Michael McEvoy of _Metabolic Healing_ suggested _**Organic Valley**_ **Pasture Butter, Cultured** years ago and it was a past favorite. It's organic but not sure it is 100% grass fed.

Favorite now and I really like the taste is **_Lewis Road Creamery Butter w/ Sea Salt_** [https://lewisroadcreamery.com/pages/about-our-butter](https://lewisroadcreamery.com/pages/about-our-butter) It is from New Zealand and states 100% grass fed.

I also like _**Goddess Ghee**_ their plain Ghee, [https://www.goddessghee.com/shop/](https://www.goddessghee.com/shop/)ghee out of Asheville, NC.
And their _**Brown Butter Ghee**_ but it tastes too good to be healthy [https://www.goddessghee.com/shop/brown-butter](https://www.goddessghee.com/shop/brown-butter)

Before I found Goddess Ghee _**[4th & Heart](https://www.vitacost.com/4th-heart) Grass-Fed Ghee Butter**_ Himalayan Pink Salt [https://fourthandheart.com/product/himalayan-pink-salt/](https://fourthandheart.com/product/himalayan-pink-salt/) was the best tasting and easily available--says Grass Fed but does not say 100% grass fed and I've gotten away from Himalayan Pink Salt due to possible higher fluoride content

The subject of butter always reminds me of this tongue-twister thatI learned in college speech class. [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Betty\_Botter](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Betty_Botter)

****"Betty Botter** is a** [**tongue-twister**](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tongue-twister) **written by** [**Carolyn Wells**](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Carolyn_Wells)**.**[**\[1\]**](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Betty_Botter#cite_note-:0-1)[**\[2\]**](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Betty_Botter#cite_note-2) **It was originally titled "**_**The Butter Betty Bought.**_**" By the middle of the 20th century, it had become part of the** [**Mother Goose**](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mother_Goose) **collection of nursery rhymes.**[**\[3\]**](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Betty_Botter#cite_note-3)"

"Betty Botter bought some butter,
"But," she said, "the butter's bitter;
If I put it in my batter,
It will make my batter bitter;
But a bit of better butter,
That would make my batter better."

So she bought a bit of butter,
Better than her bitter butter,
And she put it in her batter,
And the batter was not bitter;
So it was better that Betty Botter
Bought a bit of better butter."

**Here's to Better Butter!**

### Dan2 — 2020-12-20T11:03:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butter-poll.20875/post-293959>

[@Martha Ray](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/22506/)
Martha, look here
[https://www.realmilk.com/real-milk-finder/virginia/#va](https://www.realmilk.com/real-milk-finder/virginia/#va)
and if you find good cream (which might be more common than the farm making and selling butter), that can be made into good butter with a hand-crank or electric butter churn (or just put about half-full in a jar and shaken for a while). Butter made from raw cream especially is delicious, better than any stick butter I've tasted; if the cream is pasteurized before becoming butter or if the butter from raw cream is frozen it loses a taste that raw butter has that's especially good.

# Thread: Latest autism data.........
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/latest-autism-data.20876/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2018-02-15T01:21:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/latest-autism-data.20876/post-234775>

In November of 2017 the federal National Center for Health Statistics released the preliminary results of a study that showed between 2014 and 2016 the number of 3 to 17-year olds diagnosed with autism rose by 23%, yielding a new autism rate of 2.76%, or 1 in 36 children, and 3.63%, or 1 in 28 boys.

The numbers of children with developmental disabilities in general (including but not limited to ASD) is staggering at this point.

What is being done about this growing epidemic?

Why is there silence?

What will this look like in 10 years???

50 years ago the risk of autism was 1 in 10,000 in the USA and now in Orange County California, it is 1 in 37 children. "No child should feel unsafe" in schools was tweeted out today by our leaders. We hear about school shootings all the time now and the government is worried about gun violence and the use of guns to kill quickly, yet there is a serious double-standard with respect to risks in our schools:

1\. It's not okay to be damaged quickly by acute lead toxicity apparently

2\. It's okay to be damaged slowly by the cell towers on the roof and the Wifi in the rooms???

WE the people need to demand that our government uses the precautionary principle and it should be applied to any force that damages our children. Personally, my heart aches for any child damaged via any possible method. **[https://www.cdc.gov/nchs/data/databriefs/db291.pdf](https://www.cdc.gov/nchs/data/databriefs/db291.pdf)**

### Jack Kruse — 2018-02-15T01:22:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/latest-autism-data.20876/post-234776>

A new podcast is out today. If you listen at 2:48 you'll get the English version. I talk about autism in this podcast in kids. From the World War I to the present, the level of non-ionizing electromagnetic radiation in homes increased about 100 000 times. Тhe intensity of the microwaves is 1 000 000 000 000 000 000 (billion billions) higher than the intensity of the microwaves that exist in nature. That's too much, for most humans, cells, and tissue with high metabolic demands can't stand it. The prevalence of autism grows exponentially everywhere, in line with the aggressive expansion of mobile telephony and Wi-Fi technology. In my opinion, looking more carefully without industry-funded studies on non-ionizing electromagnetic radiation would be wise. For many years, there has been a fatal misconception that non-ionizing radiation is not dangerous because there is no photon energy required for the ionization of atoms and molecules. It's the same as saying that black mamba is not dangerous because it can't hit a man with a fist. I currently believe nnEMF is the primary cause of autism and not vaccines. [**https://youtu.be/9pBqdsMjtOE**](https://youtu.be/9pBqdsMjtOE)

### stacia426 — 2018-02-19T06:11:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/latest-autism-data.20876/post-235085>

Thanks for the link I will take the time to listen. The cause of autism appears to be multifactorial. For sure nnEMF is damaging children. I am trying to understand why my first child would have autism and my second does not. Since the egg that created my second child should have had more deuterium (if I remember correctly from my consult). Both are boys. I did not change my nnEMF exposure with my second pregnancy. I didn't learn about nnEMF until this past year. I did between my first and second change my diet and work my health. Did changing my diet effect my health enough for a short period of time that I had less deuterium on board? Was my worsening hyperemesis with my second pregnancy perhaps protective? My second child is completely vaccine free. I am trying to connect the dots between information I have already learned and this information I am trying to learn now. My oldest was born with torticollis and had feeding difficulties from birth with FTT. He was only breastfed for four months due to extreme food intolerances. He required hydrolyzed formula. With my second I eliminated many, many foods from my diet and was able to provide him with breast milk until 18 months of age. He did not experience FTT. How did longer breastfeeding have an impact, maybe better quality DHA for longer? My second child is not only neurotypical but actually developmentally ahead (particularly with speech and social interactions). How does deuterium play a role in why my children have had such different start to life? Would breast feeding for 18 months deplete DHA in my body? I was taking a DHA supplement but didn't know that eating seafood was far superior.

Hello,
I just wondered how your son was getting on now and if you don't mind me asking what you've been finding most helpful?

One doctor told me that he thought autism was more common in first children because the mother dumps a massive toxic load into the first child (nature guesses you might well mess things up the first time and so dumping toxins benefits your long-term fertility as it will go better for following children). # Thread: Hola de Yucatan!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hola-de-yucatan.20881/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Meli — 2018-02-15T19:49:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hola-de-yucatan.20881/post-234829>

I did make it to the Yucatan. I love it. Making lots of friends along the way. Doing the first part of my trip solo. I spent 5 nights with Guillermo a.k.a Lloyd Fisher on IslaMujeres. That was a daily adventure of quantum living with most time spent on the beach and swimming in the crystal blue calm water of the ocean. One eats well chez Guillermo with a sunrise picnic breakfast at the beach, followed by picnic lunch at the other beach that faces West staying till sunsets over the ocean. I spent 3 days in Ek Balam. I traveled there by cab with Sr. Cruz. He was delightful! I stayed at Genesis Eco Retreat in the village of Ek Balam. I borrowed a funky old bike and road out to the ruins, toured the ruins and swam in the cenote. This resort has its own cenote fed pool. Small but perfect for going in and out of all day. I loved sleeping to the sounds of the jungle and the village. It is a very simple resort. From there I went to Tulum with Carlos Lira, a cab driver referred to me by Sr. Cruz. He was great. I am staying on the beach near the bioshere. Even though it is an eco resort with salt water coming through the shower, it is luxurious to me. It is windy here all day and the waves are big and kind of wild. Yesterday I swam in cenote Car Wash. I swam to the far end and other swimmers were discussing the nearby aligator. I immediately swam away back to the dock. I am not keen on being in there with a 4 ft aligator but the locals think nothing of it. The guy at the gate told me that the aligator does not like human feet or legs. That was re-assuring! My husband comes on Saturday for our last 9 days.

We are thinking of going to Laguna Bacalar. Does anyone know if that is still in the good crater zone or would it be better to stay more north? Like staying in Tulum or Akumal. Bacalar looks like a spectacular lake.
Healthwise - I am feeling less burning in my tongue (burning mouth is my main health condition) but it is still there and still gets irritated by speaking. I am getting lots of sun here !

I am challenged to stay in the cenote water much more than 15 minutes...it is cold. I am working on building that up.

I am happy that I have about 12 more days here!

### Jack Kruse — 2018-02-15T22:55:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hola-de-yucatan.20881/post-234838>

Glad to see you made it!!!

### caroline — 2018-02-16T07:55:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hola-de-yucatan.20881/post-234873>

It all sounds wonderful ....so glad you made that happen!

### Meli — 2018-03-20T04:02:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hola-de-yucatan.20881/post-237583>

I was really sorry to miss the Q&A last night. I did not realize it was happening. I just listened to it. I agree with Jack that once you get into the sun in Mexico there is a change in perspective. I have been home for 2 weeks now and I am out in the sun much more. I don't want to be inside on the computer and I think I broke an addiction to the computer by being outside in the sun most of the day for about 3 weeks. I feel the difference the sun makes to my mood and my level of physical pain and overall sense of well-being. Last week of my trip I was very close to Chixtaclub Puerto at a very cool little air bnb place. It was an old duck hunting camp - Bunglaows Marguerita - for $42 a night. They had a great shared kitchen and dining room. We stayed in a bungalow. It was about 10 acres of ocean front with only one other couple there as guests. I noticed as soon as I stepped into the sand that I felt the calmest of the whole trip. I just dropped within. There is a nice little pool there. The ocean water was too murky for me. But the pool was great. You could go to this place with a small group of friends and have a great time. It is 40 mins from Merida. Cenote is about 30 minutes away. You can cook which I really like.

I want to share what I noticed in terms of chem trails and cell networks. I saw chem trails one day only at Isla Mujeres. Never saw any anywhere else - Playa, Tulum. Vallodolid, Progreso, Merida, etc. On my phone Isla was 4g, Tulum was 4g, Vallodolid 3g, Progreso 3g, Playa 3g. Cell phone towers and data towers are abundant. I think the concrete block construction is a Godsend for the Mexican people and tourists.

My favorite cenotes were at Tanka Pueblo near Tulum. So clean and beautiful! My least favorite was the one in the center of Vallodolid....it did not smell good. I went in and discovered the drain pipe and bad smell after the fact.

I had a skin rash that went away while in Mexico and returned upon coming home. Small bumps on upper abdomen, back and legs. Unfortunately I did not see any improvement in the burning mouth situation... and it is still problematic. This was trip number one to Mexico so maybe my expectations were high to think it would diminish. I did drop most all of my supplements while in Mexico and only take a few now. I eat lots of oysters and seafood. Still some vegetables and lots of raw A2 dairy.

I will go back at least 2 more times this year. Next trip is Jacksonville and then the following 2 trips this year to the Yucatan. We are getting to UV of 6 in Boulder now with about 6 hours of vitamin D sun. That seems decent to me. I am taking as much advantage of it as I can, while still needing to work inside a number of day time hours.

I am still not sure why Boulder is loaded with terahertz but I am planning to sell my house in the foothills. I am clear it is not a long term house with icy steep hilly road access, so now seems like a great exit point. I met with the realtor today who said we are approaching market top - good time to sell and become more flexible with 5g coming out. I am not ready to move to Mexico. I plan on renting or RVing out toward Estes Park...maybe Lyons or West. I want to see how things shake out before even considering buying again. I have a whole spiritual community here and I am committed to adjusting my life style to be able to stay. Once 5g comes I plan on staying out of Denver and whatever cities it is in. I feel happy to be selling the house and being able to move easily even within the area or out of the area for periods of time as needed.

I am doing CT with cold baths and showers. 4-5 minutes at a time tops - several times a day. It is not so easy for me, I think being thin has its challenges. I am looking forward to getting into the local rivers once it gets a little bit warmer.

I hope Jack's Farm opens up soon. I want to go.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-03-20T04:16:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hola-de-yucatan.20881/post-237585>

I was thinking about your consult when I was speaking last night but I bit my tongue. The farm is ahead of schedule. The weather has been good here. We might be done with the first phase by July. I'm glad you sensed the change in Mexico.

### Meli — 2018-03-20T16:42:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hola-de-yucatan.20881/post-237616>

I think I sensed that when you were speaking about Scott Kelly. Glad that the farm is ahead of schedule. When you next do consults I would like to talk again.
I used to spend the entire summers as a kid at the Lake in Wellesley, MA - swimming most of the day. I was reminded for the first time since then being of Mexico of what it is to be in the sun all day. It is an altered state from what life has become living indoors and on computer screens. It is important to make the shift and then it is harder to get on the computer and kind of a drag to stay on it very long as much. It really is a good change and it makes getting into the sun easy.

### Meli — 2018-03-29T05:57:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hola-de-yucatan.20881/post-238185>

Jack was right about sleep apnea. I found out today that I have mild sleep apnea after doing the sleep test. My oxygen levels are low and heart rate is high, body is not going into deep sleep. I am going to try a few nights breathing oxygen from a tank with the nasal canula and then re-test and see if that makes any difference. I am wondering if there is any connection to my burning mouth situation.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-04-11T23:52:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hola-de-yucatan.20881/post-239253>

Mel, I wish I was wrong about your case. Now here is the next thing for you to realize. The article is misleading and wrong when you have the mitochondriac perspective. Why? OSA is associated with transient hypoxia during sleep. Most cancers are associated with high oxygen tensions via the Warburg shift. This is the key. The mitochondriac knows that OSA is braking mechanism cells that are using the development of OSA at night when mitophagy should occur to LOWER oxygen tensions to avoid oncogenesis for as long as possible because a disabled autophagy/mitophagy are prerequisites for oncogenesis. When autophagy/mitophagy is broken oxygenation is low because ECT flow is low as a protection mechanism. The respiratory proteins are stretched out and this favors glycolysis and the PPP over the TCA and urea cycle when O2 is low. This metabolism is favored in obligate glycolytic tissues in humans like embryonic stem cells, RBC’s, and the retina. That is the only reason why OSA and cancer are linked. [https://well.blogs.nytimes.com/2012/05/20/sleep-apnea-tied-to-increased-cancer-risk/](https://well.blogs.nytimes.com/2012/05/20/sleep-apnea-tied-to-increased-cancer-risk/)

### Jack Kruse — 2018-04-11T23:52:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hola-de-yucatan.20881/post-239254>

^^^^This is why I told you to get out of Colorado.

### Meli — 2018-04-12T20:05:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hola-de-yucatan.20881/post-239305>

Thank you for the article Jack. Not great news, but good to know. One thing that I am doing is working on my breathing - shifting to belly diaphragmatic and trying to slow it down - so that I breath in less Oxygen. Started wearing a DD mask and am doing some breathing exercises.
Striving for less breaths per minute and being able to hold my breath longer after exhale. So that CO2 is increased and more oxygenation to tissues/organs.

So, the idea being live somewhere where the respiratory proteins are in general not as stretched -like the Yucatan. Even though I can get decent sun here 9UV right now I would need stronger UV because of this situation.
I imagine the Yucatan magnetism would be very healing as well.
I would think in the long run an Eskimo has to eventually return North and would not long term fair well in the South.

### Antonis — 2018-07-01T21:18:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hola-de-yucatan.20881/post-244705>

It sounds amazing adventure. Hope you are better now and you sleep well.

# Thread: Social Isolation or 5G: Which to Pick? (Any Alternatives?)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/social-isolation-or-5g-which-to-pick-any-alternatives.20884/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 32
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Matty_M — 2018-02-16T08:50:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/social-isolation-or-5g-which-to-pick-any-alternatives.20884/post-234876>

Hello! I'm looking for some guidance.

**In this post I will share some very insightful things that Jack has shared with me about the gravity of the 5G issue. This is for more than just advice for a teenager; it is one example of a type of "dilemma" that I think applies to everyone living in a place that will be hit with 5G... unless we create a third, less shitty option that turns it into a "tri-lemma".**

I am currently working to build up my company, Ra Optics, and attempting to decide on where I will move (away from Philadelphia) as the business enters a 3-12 month build-up phase before being fully functional so I can be location independent.

Take these things for granted:

\- In order to upscale so that it can reach its full potential; I need to partner with an optical lab.

\- There are loads of these across the USA, and any can do what I need done; the cost and quality difference is minimal among the many true professionals, which exist in every region.

\- I need this "HQ" to remain in the USA for certain types of orders and shipping purposes.

\----
I have had my business headquartered from a makeshift optical lab back where I grew up in the Philadelphia suburbs, but it is now critical to move up a notch.

I do not like being home, would absolutely not want to be home for more than two weeks; the environment is shit, and I have no friends around there anymore because everyone is at college (\* if I really had to, I could stick around for a few months, but I think I would just not be very happy. 5G antennas are everywhere)

I want to take it away from Philadelphia for this 3-12 month build-up phase.

Because all money is very important to the company at this phase, paying rent when I don't have to is a real "theft" from the company, unless I need to be in a specific place, and even then its a huge impossibility if it's anything normal. It's not like rent in Mexico in the US. It may only work if I could pay someone for a room. So, if not at my stressful and toxic house back in Philadelphia, my options for a place to stay for cheap/free in the US and build up the company are currently:

**1) Vero Beach, Florida:**

PRO: a great 35 y/o quantum friend lives here, right on the beach. Cold ocean water at the moment, UV 6-7, good food, minimal EMF for the next few months before 5G, minimal fake light; awesome all-around.

CON: it is in the middle of nowhere in a town where there's nothing but houses of retired people; no University and hardly any young people at all. Definitely no one around college-age.

**I am 18 years old and enjoy few things more talking with peers and spreading these ideas.
**
PRO: I feel so good physically there, it is quite unbelievable. I feel much better than Mexico because I have a cool ocean and no artificial light to deal with, I think.

This friend is absolutely an amazing guy and we could toss around ideas forever and never get bored.

CON: As a divorced 35 year old, he has a kid to be with half the time and a very different place in life than me. We can keep each other company for a while, but at a certain point I definitely crave being around a crowd in my age cohort for girls as well as these connections with peers in general. Everywhere I go I find myself meeting cool people and sharing this information, and really enjoying it.

I will not stop doing this, however for what could be 3-12 months, I will be mostly locked where I choose, and I want to make the best decision possible before going for it.

It's also possible that this friemd will move, but most likely after my timeframe of interest to get the business running and self-sufficient.

So, my production would end up being based in a place that I have little connection to, which matter too much, because I could move it with some trouble, and I am currently focused on just the next few years.

PRO: Although the town is 1.5 hours from the Orlando or Fort Lauderdale airport, it is still quite easy to get to healthy environment of Mexico from there.

CON: The only real con is that there is no one my age; I would have to take occasional flights to visit friends in other places

\-----
**Option 2): Fort Collins, Colorado
**
PRO: My uncle lives here, so I could live for cheap and really dig into getting things set up with a lab.

It is sunny almost every day, despite low UVB the winter, there is loads of UVA and all other frequencies of light. The people and the girls here look generally much healthier than other places in the US (however, this may change with 5G).

Key: there is a university here in Fort Collins and I have a few connections to friends.

Boulder is another university town just a 45 minute drive away, and I have some minor connections there, but more importantly the town is full of open minded people who are interested in health and would be a great market for blue blockers

Devil's advocate: just because it is a good market, it's not a reason for me to choose to produce here at a lab, and settle here for 3 to 12 months.

The real reason I would choose Colorado for the next 3 to 12 months is that there are just people my age around, so I can make some real connections with people, even if it's temporary.

CON: there is 5G in all these towns, already installed according to @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/). From the readings on my Cornet ED88T meter, a solid device, the power density in all of these towns is quite high, even though I couldn't find the 5G antennas like I could easily find in Philadelphia and all of its suburbs, brown rods on top of telephone poles or wrapped around lamp posts.

I asked Jack "would you choose social isolation over 5G every day of the week?" and he said "yep". He also told me about a member that used to live in Vero Beach moved to Mexico, and just posted today about how much better she's doing.

I absolutely plan on going to Mexico, however to have the capital, I am choosing to stay in the states just 3-12 months longer.

\---
3) Philadelphia Suburbs

PRO: If I choose to do it in Philadelphia, it might be the fastest, because I already have a connection. And, I can always just fly back from Cancun very easily as Philadelphia has a massive airport.

CON: On some level I feel like I'm setting myself up for failure by putting the production in a place that I absolutely never want to go.

PRO: I already know a decent amount of people who can carry out part-time shipping/fulfillment and things like this for me. As I am not currently in a position to pay anyone full-time, but also don't want to be tethered to the production/fulfillment myself, this is a huge advantge.

\-----

I may seem very adverse to the concept of "buckling down and building up my business; delaying gratification",

But this is simply because I would rather be smart and work on my business rather than in it.
\-----

I am just concerned that if I have my production in a place like Philadelphia, that it will not be sustainable when 5G really comes around. this is resting on the assumption of a situation that is not quite as bad as an apocalypse, in which case I would have much bigger issues than whether or not my company is still functioning, but Bad enough that it would have been smarter to invest in a place that won't be hit quite as badly.

As I write, I'm realizing that almost any area I would consider for this task will be hit pretty badly, whether it is Colorado, Philadelphia, and even the Florida Atlantic coast, according to Shrihari and Jack.

\-----
Other options:

I have thought about choosing to settle and some other open-minded place such as Santa Barbara or San Luis Obispo while building up this business.

However, they too will be hit hard by 5G, or maybe already are. I do not have any free rent situations and these places, which pushes me away, as if the toxic environment weren't already enough. I will definitely travel through these places and market products, however I cannot find any reason to settle there for 3 to 12 months while attempting to build up my production.

I am realizing that the path of least resistance will probably be my home of the Philadelphia Suburbs. The only issue is that I'm not interested in being present there any more than 1-2 weeks; but I may be able to leave and fly back as necessary, as it is always under $200 round trip to Florida or Mexico if you know how to find flights like I do.

\--------------

_**I know that whatever I choose, it is temporary**_, and probably possible to move the setup, although it may be an expense worth avoiding if possible.

If it turns out that I won't be able to just make it work by being in Philadelphia for a couple of days here and there, I am basically choosing between having people around but being in 5G Colorado for a few months, versus no one around but being in a better environment in Florida that does not yet have 5G and probably won't within the timeframe I am looking at. Granted that I will be probably south and central America and healthier places most of the time, Florida has an advantage due to its relative proximity.

If I leave the production in Philadelphia, I have friends who can drive me around and I can take Uber as necessary, as I would probably have my car somewhere down south in this case.

\------

**What would you choose? How would you go about approaching this decision?**

I know that it's very far from the end of the world; it's just the beginning for me  but, I like to make the best decision possible with the knowledge that I can obtain. It's working so far.

### Matty_M — 2018-02-20T06:05:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/social-isolation-or-5g-which-to-pick-any-alternatives.20884/post-235164>

Because of the fact that I'm flying and traveling so much, and want to dial it back to keep myself in good health, I'm trying to pick the place where I will settle down for a while.

If it turns out that I need to be around the physical business for multiple months, and can't just get it up and going from Philly, I will probably settle in Florida/Puerto Rico (PR because of US laws and good shipping and so on) to make this happen, for the next phase of life.

@[JMO](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/403/) I love being around peers but I couldn't force myself to LIVE in a 5G environment like all universities will be very soon... I would definitely visit, but my life is too short to shorten it.

I am visiting UC Berkeley at the moment and really joying meeting smart and interesting people my age, people who are very interested in the stuff that I have to talk about… This makes me really want to be in place like this. However, I'm still not willing to take a risk with my health.

I decided I can totally visit these places for even a few weeks, but it is key that I LIVE in an environment that is charging me up.

If 5G/radio frequencies weren't an issue at all, I would probably try to live with my aunt and uncle in Berkeley, make friends with loads of university students, build my business, and sell an awesome product…

But the evidence is saying the 5G is more than just a small issue; and given that I actually have the freedom to make a good decision for myself, I want to make the
most prudent one, given information I can gather. So, I've decided I can visit places like Berkeley, but it's key for me to live in a super optimal enviornment so I can get away with the other stuff.

To have the financial freedom to live in this environment, my business is my main focus at this stage in my life. My next move is to figure out how it will be to set up production and and work remotely from somewhere like Florida or Mexico, and to check out the playing field for Florida and Puerto Rico in case it becomes clear that I must be present for months.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/myopia-hacking-and-success.20889/post-234970>

Despite all the talking in podcasts and blogs, there is really not much stuff here for reversing myopia.
So I wanna know, what you do and if you have success and result to show.
Jack talks in a podcast about someone (maybe hypothetically) going from high myopia to zero. But I want to know if this is acutally real and possible.

I have little success. I just do weaker glasses for the far sight and even weaker for near work. I block all blue light without red. But I actually had not much sun in that time, that Im going to change this season. The autorefraktometer actually shows improvement, but I dont know if I was just overprescribed.

And if there is a change possible, like Jack says for himself. How is the eye actually rebuilding the architecture? Am I right that something must regulate the collagen building of the vitrious? Is that the same mechanism like making the eye growing longer?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brand-new-super-b-bulb-just-released-from-solacure.20891/post-234985>

The company is called "Solarcure", and their market is geared towards making light bulbs that help the marijuana growing and curing industry, wood curing, nail polish drying in salons, etc..

They have a brand new bulb release that seems to be a tad bit safer than their older version called the "flower power", which cuts out at around 275nm, and this new "Super B" drops out at 280nm.

"Super B" 34% UVB/66% UVA F32T12 bulb.

He is currently running a really great deal if anyone is interested, check out their Facebook page.

"Arriving today, and I'm feeling generous. Our "Super B" 34% UVB/66% UVA F32T12 bulb. These are on sale for $30 ea, but for our followers that wants to try them and give some feedback, I will make you a deal too good to pass up: a 4 pack for $80 and I will eat the shipping (normally $20-30), but only for US 48 states. Limit of 16 total bulbs per customer at this price. Msg me and I will give a special paypal account to send to. This is like buying for $15 each (half of my best sale price, 1/3 of normal), and I'm only going to do this for a couple hundreds lamps, so when they are gone, they are gone. Basically, I want the feedback and willing to put my money where my mouth is on these new lamps."

Here is the link - [http://www.solacure.com/superb.html](http://www.solacure.com/superb.html)

And a link to the spectrum chart -
[https://sep.yimg.com/ca/I/yhst-139912697762728\_2509\_7324168](https://sep.yimg.com/ca/I/yhst-139912697762728_2509_7324168)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brand-new-super-b-bulb-just-released-from-solacure.20891/post-234997>

### Hbuttel — 2018-02-18T02:49:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brand-new-super-b-bulb-just-released-from-solacure.20891/post-235035>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=234997)

Nice, I like the red sperti but in comparison it is a bit pricy for its size. It is 30%uv-b and 70%uv-a.
The total size is 25" x 14" and 100 watts.
Price 319

The solacure is 34% uv-b and 66% uv-a.
The bulbs are 4 foot long compared to 15" so you get much more coverage.

If you wanted to mount 4 of those on your wall,
That's $80 for the bulbs and $160 for 4 ballasts = $240
128watts spread over a 25" x 48" area
You would save $80, gain 33" in length, plus 28watts in power.

I thought it might have been just when taking Zinc alone as I had a few lozengers around Xmas and after 3 or 4 days I noticed the symptoms mentioned above. Today and yesterday I consumed a few fresh raw oysters but only like half a dozen and within 15 mins today I get the same feeling.

I would have thought the copper in the oysters would balance the zinc but is it possible to be very zinc sensitive. Anyone with anything similar or what to test would be great to hear back.

# Thread: Yeaterday, I decided to give myself the flu
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yeaterday-i-decided-to-give-myself-the-flu.20906/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hbuttel — 2018-02-19T16:22:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yeaterday-i-decided-to-give-myself-the-flu.20906/post-235103>

Yesterday I paid my father a visit and he had the flu really bad for 3 days, and it was so bad that he could hardly get off the couch to feed the cows.
He is a healthy 65yo man, who takes no pharmaceuticals, has no health issues, and is quite fit.
He does not ground himself often, and gets very little sun.
However he does subject himself to more of the cold outdoor weather here in the northeast than the average person, and is very active, and is a very hard worker.
His diet is fair, I've managed to get him to cut down on the morning donuts, the pasta, and pre-bedtime ice cream, but he still indulges often.

He recently caught the flu really bad, and I haven't ever seen him this sick.

I decided to do an experiment since I had been doing daily CT for nearly 3 months, grounding a lot daily, drinking spring water, getting sunshine on the skin and in eyes, and wearing blu blockers at night, along with the rest of the very sober and healthy lifestyle that I have been applying for years.

I drank a big glass of water out of the cup that he was using all of that day knowing that I would get sick, but my goal was to see just how strong these practices were actually making my immune system.

I drank the water at about 2pm yesterday afternoon.
I diddnt feel anything until about 12 hours later that night. I woke up around 2am with obvious flu like symptoms. My throat glands were swelling, I felt slightly uncomfortable, and I had a slight fever.
I tossed and turned more than usual, and was able to sleep, but it wasn't deep sleep and their was lots of dreaming that oddly was quite unpleasant.

I woke up around 5:30am and felt much better than I had at 2am.
I had my morning cup of hot cacao, cayenne, stevia, and mct oil,
And was very happy to be feeling so good.
I then took a 20 min CT at 50deg which is my usual, then finished with half body submersion meditation in the hot tub.

I feel great, a little bit less energy than I usually have but that is it.

Feeling much gratitude towards the amazing team that worked so hard within me all throughout the night to protect me.
We are all literal coaches of a team of wonderful little organisms whom dedicate their entire lives towards making sure we stay healthy.
We just need to make sure that they are supplied with everything that they need to thrive and be healthy themselves, creating an amazing microcosm that can be a real force to be reconed with.
Way to go team!

This experiment definitely helped build more self love, and a deep emotional gratitude for all of the quadrillion tiny conscious organisms within me.

### JanSz — 2018-02-19T18:36:22+0200

[https://twitter.com/nntaleb/status/965904733788688385](https://twitter.com/nntaleb/status/965904733788688385)
[https://www.amazon.com/Skin-Game-Hidden-Asymmetries-Daily/dp/042528462X](https://www.amazon.com/Skin-Game-Hidden-Asymmetries-Daily/dp/042528462X)

Jack Kruse talks a lot about skin in the game, and as it happens to be, Nassim Taleb is writing a book on skin in the game. I read some excerpts and I think the we will see a lot of parallels. Iatrogenesis is one of his subjects he talked about in previous books, that lines up perfectly with our view on human health in todays society.

### Lahelada — 2018-02-20T15:46:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-in-the-game.20911/post-235189>

I think it will be a very interesting read.
There is one thing that I want to hit upon though.I now make a distinction between medicine and doctors.Young doctors are now already born into this system into the blind belief in algorhythms and the fiction of continual upgrades meaning a better life. In times past when the doctor swore the Hippocratic oath it was responded to with a letter of protection on the part of society. I think in modern time s this protection will have to come in the form of the realisation that we aided and abetted in allowing this behemoth that is now the medical industry to grow into what it is today. We neglected our education and transferred the onus of vigilance onto the shoulder of the doctor only.

# Thread: Cynthia Holmes - My Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cynthia-holmes-my-optimal-journal.20912/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Cynthia Holmes — 2018-02-20T21:16:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cynthia-holmes-my-optimal-journal.20912/post-235212>

Basics

· Female. Married with 28 y.o. son and 23 y.o. daughter.

· I was born in February 1958 (just turned 60).

· I am the second child of three daughters.

· My mother smoked during all 3 pregnancies. She and my father smoked during the 18 years I lived at home. My mother did not breastfeed any of us. She died of COPD at age 80 after being on oxygen 24/7 for 8 years.

· My maternal grandmother lived to 104, outliving her daughter (my mother) who was her only child. Both my mother and her mother were mentally sharp until they died.

· I was raised in northern NJ (18 years), then moved to Philadelphia (6 years), then moved to San Francisco (6 years), and then to Tucson (28 years).

· I had plenty of sun exposure early in life but became neurotic about avoiding it when I moved to Tucson. Seriously! Hats, sunglasses, sun screen, long-sleeves and shade- seeking behavior for 28 years!

My health concerns (in order of importance)

· Dry macular degeneration (optometrist first noticed drusen when I was in my 30s)

· Dysthymia/depression since age 17 (on 20 mg fluoxetine for 20 years)

· HSV-2 since age 19 (on prophylactic acyclovir for 33 years)

· APOE-E 3/4 and MTHFR defects

How I found Jack

In September 2017, a google search on causes of macular degeneration brought up the link to Jack’s 2017 Nourish Vermont talk. I watched it at least 10 times because what he says about the eye clock is information I had never heard before! I transitioned quickly from watching various podcasts on YouTube to becoming a bronze then silver then gold member. Since my last membership upgrade I have been devouring the old webinars and should be current by the end of the month, at which time I will start on the blogs. I really look forward to a live event: I couldn’t do MX last year because we had family visiting and because I already had a trip to Playa De Carmen planned for this month (2/21-2/25). I will attend the member event in MX in 2018.

Prior to discovering JK

In July of 2017 I began self-paying to see an internal medicine doc who is fellowship-trained in Integrative Medicine because the PCPs on my insurance plan would not order the labs I wanted. A month before my first meeting with him I had eliminated all grains and dairy from my diet and in doing so lost some weight, which was a goal, but also had the unexpected benefit of completely eliminating my chronic neck and shoulder pain which I had treated for a long time (years) with 800 mg of ibuprofen every morning: I no longer do this. Also, on this diet, my stools went from type 2 to type 4 on the Bristol chart, albeit after some GI discomfort and diarrhea in the transition.

JK stuff I’m doing now:

· LIGHT: barefoot sungazing for about 10 minutes at dawn (not looking directly at the sun because I’m still nervous about retinal damage). No sunglasses outside ever. Have discontinued wearing contacts and take off my regular glasses as much as possible when outside. I am using daytime and nighttime blue blockers when in artificial light. I am nude sunbathing daily when D is available per D-minder app, 15 minutes each side, lying on a mat with my feet and hands in contact with the ground. I use an infrared sauna (Sunlighten) three times a week for about 45 minutes each session. I can make D in Tucson 365 days a year (32nd latitude).

· WATER: First I switched from Brita-filtered tap water to spring water and increased my consumption. I am currently drinking reverse osmosis water exclusively. Tucson water is not fluoridated.

· MAGNETISM: thinking about a Magnetico sleep pad but haven’t made that investment yet. Just trying to be barefoot outdoors more. Will spend time in a cenote in Cancun this coming week.

· CT: In Nov/Dec 2017 I did CT about a dozen times in my bathtub between 50 and 55 degrees for about 30 minutes each time. Currently, not doing it, but may start face-dunking. (Leptin on labs is currently 6).

· EMFs: After I do the EMF Boot Camp I will test my home, which is the environment I spend the most time in. I like the idea of eliminating wi-fi, hardwiring our computers and TV, and installing a kill switch for the master bedroom. In anticipation of worsening vision (but before I became aware of the negative health effects of artificial light) I spent a bundle upgrading our electric and installing a bunch more lights. Bummer!

· FOOD: I’m eating more sea food, although I think I injured my wrist shucking my own oysters to save money! My diet is ketogenic and relatively high fat. I buy fruits and veggies from the farmers’ market to ensure these items are local and seasonal. I am monitoring my blood sugar but rarely have morning fasting levels below 100. I am trying to keep my eating window as narrow as possible but still almost always have three meals a day.

· SUPPLEMENTS: My doc has me on quite a few things and I will discuss these in a later post. I started the JK practices and the supplements around the same time so of course it is impossible to know what is having what effect. The good news is my labs have improved. Since I didn’t make changes one at a time and monitor results with labs, I think I’ll try to do the reverse and start reducing or eliminating things one at a time (e.g., prescription drugs and supplements) and check labs after each step.

· EXERCISE: I should have waited to start exercising until I lost weight, but I didn’t and gave myself bone spurs on the tops of both feet. These are no longer painful and I currently walk/jog outdoors three days a week and do a Cybex circuit indoors three days a week.

Health Wins

· I’ve lost 35 pounds since June 1, 2017 (about 7 months). I started at 177 pounds and 47.2% body fat and am now at 141 lbs. and 31.4% body fat (using Bod Pod for body composition). 135 lbs is what I weighed for most of my 20s and 30s.

· Getting more REM sleep and Deep sleep as reported by Fitbit Alta HR.

· Mood has stabilized and improved above and beyond the gain I made 20 years ago with fluoxetine.

· Way less brain fog and way more energy.

· Feet don’t feel as cold as they used to and I am sometimes kicking off the covers at night.

· Labs: The attached PDF compares labs from September 2017 and January 2018 (about 5 months apart). The September labs, reported in the right-hand column of the PDF, show lots of red (high risk) and yellow (intermediate risk) results. My current labs, to the left, show a lot more green (optimal) results. A couple of highlights include the following improvements: cholesterol went down to 163 from 206, hs-CRP when down to 1.2 from 3.4, oxidized LDL went down to 49 from 74, CoQ10 went up from .69 to 1.38, and omega 3 index went up to 9.4 from 6.2. Areas of concern include possible insulin resistance, high adiponectin at 16, high ferritin at 156, high postprandial fatty acids, low free T4, and BUN: Creatinine ratio of 21.

Health Goals

· Do whatever can be done to reverse or slow the progression of my macular degeneration.

· Discontinue fluoxetine and find another way to manage depression.

· Discontinue acyclovir and find another way to prevent outbreaks of herpes.

· Optimize bioidentical hormones.

· Do whatever can be done to minimize risk of cognitive decline from APOE E 3/4 status.

I use an infrared sauna (Sunlighten) three times a week for about 45 minutes each session. I can make D in Tucson 365 days a year (32nd latitude).

# Thread: Radioactive Americium-241 in Smoke Alarms: A Concern or Not?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/radioactive-americium-241-in-smoke-alarms-a-concern-or-not.20914/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Greenie — 2018-02-21T01:10:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/radioactive-americium-241-in-smoke-alarms-a-concern-or-not.20914/post-235230>

Came across information concerning smoke alarms. Checked the two in my place; both contain Americium-241.

While the literature I have researched states the Americium is harmful only if inhaled or swallowed, I still question if this is truly the case. This radioactive material is said to ionize the air. Has anyone some insight as to whether smoke alarms containing this element are a no go, even if left encased in the alarm?

(

)

### drezy — 2018-02-21T02:36:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/radioactive-americium-241-in-smoke-alarms-a-concern-or-not.20914/post-235241>

I don't think it's anywhere near a main concern at your age.

### drezy — 2018-02-21T04:03:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/radioactive-americium-241-in-smoke-alarms-a-concern-or-not.20914/post-235251>

I'd give you a big hug if I were near you.

### Greenie — 2018-02-23T19:43:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/radioactive-americium-241-in-smoke-alarms-a-concern-or-not.20914/post-235541>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=235245)
>
> There is "science" and there is observability:
> [http://thebabushkasofchernobyl.com](http://thebabushkasofchernobyl.com)

Watched the film, thank you for the recommendation. It was fascinating and enjoyable.

Can you direct me to where I can find more about the reasons why the radiation levels did not impact the Babushkas? Was it more socio-psychological or is the impact on radiation completely overblown? Based on the conventional perspective, the women would not have survived living in those conditions for so long without cancer quickly taking hold.

The gentleman's words at the medical clinic to the Babushka being tested for cesium levels resonated: "\[These elevated levels of radiation\] don't show that radiation isn't harmful. But it does show that socio-psychological factors also greatly influence health...\[a study showed\] evacuees died younger than those who stayed. Quite simply, people die from anguish."

### drezy — 2018-02-23T21:37:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/radioactive-americium-241-in-smoke-alarms-a-concern-or-not.20914/post-235553>

I've yet to watch it. I've just caught interviews about the film and the women.

I know that in radiation younger people (more active thyroids) need more potassium iodide to keep their thyroids flushed through than old people. It's a little like testicular cancer screening. It stops around mid-life since the testicles are deemed no longer metabolically crazy active. It's almost a little insulting when you think about it. We don't need to check you because not even cancer wants your testicles, it's got better things to do.

My point was that since you're not very young it's a far cry from being close to an issue as far as I can tell.

That all explains the short to mid term for the babushkas but I don't have a great explanation.

Since the film wasn't titled "Babushkas Gone Wild: 2017 Spring Break Chernobyl Edition" I'm guessing they don't expose their whole naked bodies to the sun regularly. Did you notice their footwear (aka grounding)?

I'm thinking either something LWM is going right for them or radiation doesn't work the way I thought it does.

It's still interesting or maybe indicative that seem to be no men.

### Greenie — 2018-02-24T01:07:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/radioactive-americium-241-in-smoke-alarms-a-concern-or-not.20914/post-235568>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=235553)
>
> I've yet to watch it. I've just caught interviews about the film and the women.
>
> I know that in radiation younger people (more active thyroids) need more potassium iodide to keep their thyroids flushed through than old people. It's a little like testicular cancer screening. It stops around mid-life since the testicles are deemed no longer metabolically crazy active. It's almost a little insulting when you think about it. We don't need to check you because not even cancer wants your testicles, it's got better things to do.
>
> My point was that since you're not very young it's a far cry from being close to an issue as far as I can tell.
>
> That all explains the short to mid term for the babushkas but I don't have a great explanation.
>
> Since the film wasn't titled "Babushkas Gone Wild: 2017 Spring Break Chernobyl Edition" I'm guessing they don't expose their whole naked bodies to the sun regularly. Did you notice their footwear (aka grounding)?
>
> I'm thinking either something LWM is going right for them or radiation doesn't work the way I thought it does.
>
> It's still interesting or maybe indicative that seem to be no men.

The only mention of cancer in the film was thyroid. One of the Babushka had hers removed shortly after the meltdown. No men are featured in their area but it is not clear whether any males were still left.

Haha, no salacious scenes—family safe.  I did not catch any instances where a Babushka wasn't almost entirely covered up. Funny you mention shoes, I consciously observed their footwear. I could not make out if the soles were rubber or not. One thing I noticed was that not one of them wore glasses. They consumed food from their garden and lake/river as well as food delivered to them. One Babushka talks about harvesting potatoes, beets, and onions. Another collected herbs, berries, and mushrooms.

> I have some people asking me about sage seed oil.
>
> Now here in Israel a US company called naturasage is being promoted as the next best thing for DHA
> [http://www.naturesageomega3.com/](http://www.naturesageomega3.com/)
>
> From what I learned from Jack nothing replaces DHA from fish and seafood
> So..
> is it a deuterium boom?
> What should I answer the people that think it is the best thing on the planet...
> I kinda don't want to waste my energy on people that don't get it , but it's important to me to at lest get the right information out there
> Thanks

This is an omega 3, ALA. This is not the same as DHA. The body can turn some of it into DHA, but it isn't very good at it. On top of that, some of us have SNPs that further impair this conversion. You will encounter vegans who will argue this point, but they are wrong!. So it's not an adequate substitute for seafood. It's also really easy to get ALA with a relatively small amount of seeds or nuts that don't cost nearly as much as a month supply of this stuff. No idea how much deuterium it carries.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-02-22T03:40:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clary-sage-oil.20919/post-235344>

Not all omega-3s are created equal. In fact, not all PUFA's are equal. Most PUFA's are a real problem in a 4/5G world but DHA is not in that group. You actually need more of it the more power density you allow in your life. For example, autistic kids and adults with dementia need a ton of it to reverse their disease while limiting their environmental exposure. EPA & DHA--the 2 forms of omega-3 we use--are only found in animal foods. Conversion of ALA into EPA/DHA difficult, especially for men. Retinal/ brain structure/function heavily dependent on DHA in the SN-2 position and this only comes from the marine chain with iodine and deuterium depletion all in one package!

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### shiran — 2018-02-22T16:44:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clary-sage-oil.20919/post-235380>

Thanks for that it really helps me to put the info in Hebrew out correctly

### WalterNL — 2018-02-22T17:20:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clary-sage-oil.20919/post-235385>

There are some supplements from animal sources with DHA in SN2 position, but I suspect they are much higher in deuterium and obviously lack everything else that's in seafood. In other words; seafood is the way to go.

# Thread: Blueblockers for driving?

### LieselK — 2018-02-21T23:34:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blueblockers-for-driving.20921/post-235320>

So I have the amber glasses and I have the red ones- they fit over my prescription eyeglasses. I don't remember exactly what I purchased, just that they were recommended ones. But... I can't wear them and see to drive at night. I plan to get prescription glasses tinted through RA Optic- will those work for driving? What can I do until I can get those ordered?

### Jack Kruse — 2018-02-22T03:17:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blueblockers-for-driving.20921/post-235339>

don't drive at night

### Billybats — 2018-02-22T13:50:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blueblockers-for-driving.20921/post-235361>

More like in the morning on your way to work. These new headlights are so bright now I have to adjust my mirrors to reduce the brightness.

### kris90 — 2018-02-22T13:54:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blueblockers-for-driving.20921/post-235362>

I use blue blockers to drive to work in the winter when it's pitch black out. Not ideal, but I don't want bright headlights shining in my eye.

Soon I won't have to worry about this, because I won't be working a desk job in the city.

### LieselK — 2018-02-22T16:02:05+0200

### Sun Disciple — 2018-02-22T17:01:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blueblockers-for-driving.20921/post-235382>

I wear blue blockers after sunset on the road. It wasnt easy at first but after a year of doing it Im used to it. Then again if you have bad night vision I could imagine it might be pretty dangerous.

### WalterNL — 2018-02-22T17:10:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blueblockers-for-driving.20921/post-235383>

Preferably what Jack Kruse said, but otherwise I don't wear blue blockers while driving. They make me sleepy

### Theka — 2018-02-22T22:19:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blueblockers-for-driving.20921/post-235457>

We wear amber ones if we must drive at night......and my son wears them early bicyling to the train station. Some of the new headlights are just painful and there are no laws here about maximum brightness. We find the darker ones block too much to drive safely.

### Zeke — 2018-02-22T22:49:42+0200

### Jack Kruse — 2018-05-16T06:53:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blueblockers-for-driving.20921/post-241323>

be careful that you do not fall asleep driving!!!

### Sajid Mahmood — 2018-05-16T12:38:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blueblockers-for-driving.20921/post-241329>

Daytime yellow tints from raoptics would be okay for driving too. It might not give the full protection but at least you can see everything on road quite clearly. They also wouldn’t make you feel drowsy too

### LieselK — 2018-05-16T16:00:41+0300

# Thread: Cite for original sun/cancer study
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cite-for-original-sun-cancer-study.20926/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JoyfulJan — 2018-02-22T16:01:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cite-for-original-sun-cancer-study.20926/post-235376>

Jack mentioned that the original study that they formulated "the sun is bad for you" was performed with a skewed light source. Can someone provide me with that cite, I am writing a book (Feel Better In One Day) for the lay public and I would like to have that ammo.

### Sue-UK — 2018-02-23T13:06:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cite-for-original-sun-cancer-study.20926/post-235503>

I don't know if this would be any good for you. [http://www.ajo.com/article/0002-9394(82)90529-3/pdf](http://www.ajo.com/article/0002-9394\(82\)90529-3/pdf) I don't have access to the full paper, but in his book Light, Medicine of the Future by Jacob Liberman, he references it. Liberman thought the results were questionable, because the monkeys were tranquillized and their eyelids pried open with lid clamps, and when fully dilated, researchers beamed light into their eyes from a 2500 watt xeon lamp, containing high levels of UV i.e. they based conclusions on something that would never happen in the natural world, either to monkeys or humans.

But, unemployment stats are only for those declaring that they are looking for work.
Apparently, we have lots of folks who don't want to work or look for work......but will stand in line for food. It is amazing that the advice given about not feeding wild animals is so spot on........but the zoo humans never seem to get the true essence of the logic behind the recommendations.

They already have jobs as "professional voters for liberal politicians," who compensate them with your tax dollars to buy shit food and cigarettes. Self-sustainable political ecology 101. If you don't have enough of them, import more. Self-sustainable political ecology 201.

### NeilBB — 2018-02-24T19:09:40+0200

### JanSz — 2018-02-24T19:20:49+0200

@[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) I don't even own any land, but I'd gladly give up my voting rights for a shread of sanity.

### JanSz — 2018-02-24T19:53:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/we-dont-feed-wild-animals-but-why-do-we-feed-modern-humans.20939/post-235625>

>
> @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) I don't even own any land, but I'd gladly give up my voting rights for a shread of sanity.

I do not think voting rights should be given up, specially for a price.

Sanity is a word, describing this to one and something else to someone else.
Words must have a meaning.
I would start with restoring original meaning to words.
Tower of Babel

I would start with those who create class of perpetual victims.

...

The way it goes I see shortly possibility of great increase in number of peoples declared as insane.
We have been there before.

### NeilBB — 2018-02-24T20:03:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/we-dont-feed-wild-animals-but-why-do-we-feed-modern-humans.20939/post-235630>

Could not agree with you more about words and meanings, but we may be only 2 left that believe that, JanSz. Epistemology is everything, nobody has beaten that drum more than I have.

Voting rights should belong to productive people with a stake in the nation's future, assuming the nation and the people are rational, of course. They should not belong to professional freeloaders.

Also, the US was conceived as a nation of laws to protect individual freedoms, a Republic, not a damn Democracy as is often incorrectly stated. Democracies have long existed in human history and have always failed because essentially pure Democracy means "mob rule," until the mob destroys itself and is eager to elect a despot...well that is where we are now, not only in the US, but pretty much everywhere in the "civilized" world.

### brandie — 2018-02-24T20:17:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/we-dont-feed-wild-animals-but-why-do-we-feed-modern-humans.20939/post-235632>

Politicians have allowed people to make welfare a career, a way of life. Setting limitations on the amount of help given allows people to lift themselves up, knowing they have to take care of themselves eventually motivates them to work harder. Setting a limit on how many children you can get welfare for would stop people from having more kids just to get more money. Limited welfare is what is needed.

### NeilBB — 2018-02-24T20:56:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/we-dont-feed-wild-animals-but-why-do-we-feed-modern-humans.20939/post-235635>

Limited charity is what is needed and moral. Welfare is charity enforced at the point of a gun by self-appointed demigods, who always take a large cut. I don't support that.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-02-24T23:40:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/we-dont-feed-wild-animals-but-why-do-we-feed-modern-humans.20939/post-235647>

Welfare recipients are akin to being afflicted by Munchhausen by proxy for the left.

Voting rights should belong to productive people with a stake in the nation's future, assuming the nation and the people are rational, of course. They should not belong to professional freeloaders.

Also, the **US was conceived as a nation of laws to protect individual freedoms, a Republic, not a damn Democracy as is often incorrectly stated. Democracies have long existed in human history and have always failed because essentially pure Democracy means "mob rule," until the mob destroys itself and is eager to elect a despot...well that is where we are now, not only in the US, but pretty much everywhere in the "civilized" world.**\[/quote\]
US was conceived as a nation of laws to protect individual freedoms
\==============
@[NeilBB](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6879/)
That kind of system leads to prosperity and low birth rate.

Discriminating
immigration system should be established that select for individuals that:
#1- love and support American system and all their laws
#2- are already self sufficient prior to entering USA

..

### Billybats — 2018-02-24T21:07:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-ifs-and-why-how-to-survive.20941/post-235637>

Hello All,

What if you were stranded on a island and the omly water available is the ocean. How or can it be made into drinking water. Lol yes these are the things that I think of and it was not leaving my head until I know.. Been thinking of things of how I would survive if we come across hard times. Like going back in time. How I feel we should know how to manage for ourselves even if we were camping. Very few people left that know how to live off the land.

### Billybats — 2018-02-26T18:16:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-ifs-and-why-how-to-survive.20941/post-235778>

### Billybats — 2018-02-28T18:18:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-ifs-and-why-how-to-survive.20941/post-235960>

I would love to learn how to make a fire with supposedly stick, rock, and dry grass/leaves etc..

Does anyone have other survival methods. I see some on here that know how things work make some diy stuff. How about if became crazy and you had to survive. Make water, fire, catch fish, catch an animal, yep I have no instincts that I know of yet..lol Maybe deep down inside if I had to they may come around.

# Thread: Varicose Vein pain (especially day 1-2 of cycle)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/varicose-vein-pain-especially-day-1-2-of-cycle.20942/>
**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LieselK — 2018-02-25T01:48:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/varicose-vein-pain-especially-day-1-2-of-cycle.20942/post-235662>

Ladies, help me remember the details here... with my 3rd pregnancy (after some gut healing, detoxing and diet changes for Hashimoto's) I had a lot of problems with varicose vein pain. Mine aren't raised, they were only visible during pregnancy. It got way better after pregnancy and with weight loss, but the first 1-2 days of my period it always flares up. I'm a month in on the leptin reset and epi-paleo rx and oddly last month just a few days after changing to epi-paleo they weren't that bad, but this cycle it's horrible again. I was hoping the CT immersion I've been doing this past week and today as well would help, but it's not.

So is this just because of increased blood flow during this part of my cycle? Have y'all read anything anywhere about hormones and varicose veins? Has anyone experienced a change in their veins after a time following Jack's protocols? I'm wondering if doing CT more frequently on these days would be helpful? I just read somewhere (forum or blogs) that after following leptin reset women experienced lighter periods and shorter cycles so I'm wondering where this will go.

# Thread: New Balance Grounding Shoes?

**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions

### Greenie — 2018-02-25T03:31:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-balance-grounding-shoes.20943/post-235666>

Has anyone any familiarity with these electrostatic shoes from New Balance? [http://www.newbalance.com/pd/steel-...\_MID627-V2L\_color=Black#color=Black&size=10.5](http://www.newbalance.com/pd/steel-toe-627v2-leather/MID627-V2L.html?dwvar_MID627-V2L_color=Black#color=Black&size=10.5)

The website states:

-   **Meets ASTM F2412-11 and ASTM F2413-11 Electrostatic Dissipative performance requirements that displace charges of electricity through a walking surface thus reducing static electricity hazards**
-   Steel Toe footwear is engineered with a wide-flanged tempered steel-toe cap that provides protection in hazardous work environments (meets ASTM F 2412-11 & ASTM F 2413-11 I/75 and C/75 impact and compression safety standards)

### shiran — 2018-02-25T16:39:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-balance-grounding-shoes.20943/post-235693>

Did I understand right- the strap really grounds you?

### drezy — 2018-02-25T17:47:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-balance-grounding-shoes.20943/post-235697>

> [shiran said:](/goto/post?id=235693)
>
> Did I understand right- the strap really grounds you?

Through a resistance so charge would transfer more slowly (versus not al all with most shoes) but slower than being in direct contact with ground.

The same "slow"/resistive ground is specified by the standards mentioned above and designed into those shoes. The reason it's resistive is that in the unfortunate case a mains wire tapped you out in the big bad world instead of getting a hole burned in you by massive current flow you'd just feel slightly uncomfortable.

### JanSz — 2018-02-26T20:51:26+0200

>
> I think I read or heard Dr. K say be grounded no matter what because that was a big question in my head too, and I remember reading it or hearing it from him when I decided to order my all leather moccasins.

When you see electric wire,
most often
if not always
you assume that electric current will flow thru the wire
and with about same density across it's cross section.

That assumption is correct for DC current.
Almost correct for AC house wiring.
but
there are situations where the current will flow mostly on the surface of wire.
and
if you push it
on the surface of wires insulation

/////////////
@[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/)
details
please

...
voltage
amperage
frequency

what else?
.....

### Total EMFSolutions — 2018-02-27T18:14:32+0200

Unfortunately the EMF world is measuring what rides on the skin by holding a piece of metal using a volt meter and the ground wire as a potential.

To measure **internal voltage** you will need a good quality battery operated oscilloscope and ECG electrodes.

From the 1999 NIEHS EMF RAPID report
“Through the EMF-RAPID Program, considerable progress was made in the area of in vitro research on ELF-EMF. Many of these studies of ELF-EMF exposure focused on end-points commonly associated with cancer (e.g. cell proliferation, disruption of signal transduction pathways and inhibition of differentiation). **Convincing evidence for causing effects is only available for** magnetic flux densities greater than 100 µT _(1,000mG)_ or **internal electric field strengths greater than approximately 1 mV/m.”**
