### William Abrahamson — 2018-02-25T04:19:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-arlington-texas.20944/post-235672>

Hi 58 yo male here. My hobby is nutrition, and anti aging andcreading medical journal abstracts. I found Jack K online a few years ago. It is exciting to be reading cutting edge stuff. A tad more about me. In 2007, i wass diagnosed as type 2 diabetic. In 2013 something happened. Maybe a virus but my immune system attacked my thyroid and pancreas. I had tshirt drenching night sweats and uncontrolled shivering. Fine during the day. I lost 30 lbs in 3 months. Lots of muscle mass. I'm 5'7" lifetime high weight wise was 186. So 30 lbs was alot. Anyway... i've been on once per day insulin (lantus) and synthroid for my thyroid. Wife is a nurse practitioner at a functional medicine gyne office. She is doing keto right now. I just watched vermont 2017. Wow. My problem is discipline. I do feel i'm on the cusp of starting.

### RebbalforLight — 2018-02-25T19:26:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-had-3-minutes.20947/post-235703>

If you had 3 minutes to impress/sway someone towards light/water/magnetism, specifically a scientist who was much more familiar with neurology/biology and that terminology than you were, what would you say? What references would you want on hand as proof/support?

I am a small fish in a small pond. After years of illness, my circle of influence extends to my immediate family and a handful of co-workers. One of the co-workers is a big fish who swims in deep waters with other big fish. This person can influence the health of many in a positive way.

So, in short segments, I will approach this person with data and concerns. This thread will be about my attempts.

### RebbalforLight — 2018-02-28T14:56:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-had-3-minutes.20947/post-235930>

Blue light is the beginning. My blue blocking glasses are obvious to all at work, as I only take them off when outside or when working with elements involving blue (which disappear with my fancy specs).

Molecular Vision 2016; 22:61-72; Effects of blue light on the circadian system and eye physiology Gianluca Tosini, Ian Ferguson, Kazuo Tsubota

Colour As a Signal for Entraining the Mammalian Circadian Clock

-   [https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pbio.1002127](https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pbio.1002127)

and many others. On this topic, at least, the science world seems to be catching up to Jack.

Next, I am trying to understand the repair mechanism that Jack speaks about in Vermont 2017 around the 18-minute mark.

And I am having trouble finding journals that state that UV goes beyond the lens. I guess I need to go to older journals?

I think there is a pretty predictable sequence when first swamped with Docs info:
1.) Disbelief
2.) Initial minor attempt (like blue blockers or travel to lower latitude)
3.) Noted improvements
4.) Realization of just how much one would have to change if Doc is even 25% right
5.) Desire to fix the whole rest of the world around oneself first
6.) burnout OR capitulation to required changes OR stagnation OR run the other direction

Um, wow. Update a post, go off to work and WHAM! Two pages of comments! Thanks to everyone for chiming in.

For those suggesting to focus on me and mine first, I understand and am working to educate my immediate family first. As I learn, I inform them. They are tolerant and willing to modify some things. For further out family and friends, I answer questions as honestly and completely as possible but stop when interest wanes. As I said in the OP, my clip-ons have created quite a stir.

The Big Fish (BF for short) is someone that I greatly respect and is known for being a truth teller (like the gentleman whose name is on the top of the page). IF the BF can be swayed to look into the connections that Jack has made, the learning curve will be much shorter as all these pathways are familiar to the BF (trying really hard not to out this person). This person has "good genes" with few obvious health problems, but works with a genetically compromised population that is being harmed worse than regular folks by nnEMF and blue light toxicity. I feel for this population and their struggles. If I can point towards things that will help them, I feel I must try. And since it is not my place, I must sway the BF. As I said, the first step is easy. Blue light is out there.

If I think the BF may look into Jack's website, this thread will magically disappear. Just a warning.

Now, I'll try and go through carefully and answer specific responses.

The Benny Hill video was awesome.

### drezy — 2018-03-01T03:52:40+0200

>

Not knowing any specifics about any of your training or if we have any overlapI have to go with a weaker general analogy of what I understand.

Say you took a job as curator of a clock museum with 4 stories of floors filled with clocks from every era. Your pride and joy would be that when visitors came every clock would, within reason, be as accurate as possible.

Assuming the museum runs like most museums and closes at night, when would you start checking and adjusting all the clocks?

Would you adjust the plethora of clocks relative to your least accurate clock or your hyper accurate atomic clock?

I'm a plant person surrounded by neuro/eye people. General works. I hear a lot of their terminology, but it doesn't always click.

One would hope that the atomic clock would keep accurate time (assumption) and that would be the standard to set the other clocks. That is the SCN eye clock, right?

# Thread: My Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.20948/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 75
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jim Laird — 2018-02-25T21:26:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.20948/post-235712>

Maternal H
H1c2
Paternal H
R-L21

25.2% British and Irish
300 Neanderthal Variants

APOA2
BTBD9
CYP1A2
ACTN3
CYP1A2
Y402H
ARMS2

Bio: Grew up in Northern Canada (Edmonton) was not breast feed, due to a "allergy to breast milk" had all sorts of fun chronic ear infections growing up, all sorts of fun antibiotic exposure at a young age. Played every sport imaginable, was drawn to the outdoors spent hours playing outside, fishing and hunting and hiking. As a teenager was drawn to staying up late and watching TV, struggled with sleep. Moved to the USA in 1994 to play football, become a strength and conditioning coach, Moved back and forth between Tucson Arizona and Aspen Colorado. looking back thrived in both environments, Moved to Lexington KY in 2001 started my own business training people. Started competing in power-lifting to fill the void of football, used competing to manage anger issues from a very ruff childhood. Began taking performance enhancing drugs, also used stimulants to push performance. Worked long hours and continued to push conformance, struggled with sleep. Became more reliant on stimulants, was able to thrive in competition until 2008 when i hit a wall, had warning signs in winter would struggle but i would push through. 2008 had a break down decided to move back to Colorado, drove out there then changed my mind came back one day after getting there, upon return developed colitis which my father had. This was huge wake up call realized i had been pushing way to hard for to long. Started meditating, changed diet starting making sleep a priority. started using nicotine to help manage colitis. Also started dealing with my past and my anger forgave some people, Started a gym based on what i had learned about rest. Did a better job of self care but got sucked into business. Was helping others but started neglecting my self care again. 2012 got my first and only pedicure and got a staff infection from it, almost lost my leg. Was able to beat is with antibiotics. Over the last 6 years have battled flare ups of infection get high fevers and go septic i have ran through antibiotic options. Started to figure out that when i spent to much time inside working to much i would have flare ups. Had a bad septic flare up about two years ago and antibiotics were not working. Suggested a blood transfusion to docs and they with hesitation allowed my to try it. Was able to beat it. Started looking at Dr. Kruses' work and it reinforced what i had known all along. Had another flare up just before Christmas was able to beat is with fasting, tanning bed, red light and CT. I downsized my gym so i don't have to work so much, added some black lights to the gym and a cold tub outside. Making am light exposure a priority also taking trips to mexico to visit the Cenotes at Playa Del Carma. Switched to seasonal eating. Struggle to not fall back into old habits of stimulant use and working to much.

Father: Night shift worked got UC at 40 did traditional medical treatment ended up with colostomy bag. ended up with type 2 diabetes witch he manages with low carb diet and walking outside. Grandfather died in late 80's heart just stopped one morning looking out the window, no heart attack. Grandmother on dad side died of liver cancer in her late 80's.

Mother: Had polio was not expected to live was in an iron lung. Now has post polo syndrome is doing well by not following the conventional wisdom. Grandfather had brain injury from a car wreck building the Alaska Highway, died of a heart attack in his 70's. Grandmother died at the age of 99 of Alzheimers.

Goals: To become less of a zoo animal.
Really looking forward to the summer I fish in small cold streams (get into the water) a number of times a week that is when i thrive, looking back its when i got to busy to go fishing is when i would get into trouble.
Continue to learn more from Dr. Kruse so I can help my clients I am seeing a massive explosion of auto immune issues in the people I work with especially the youth.

Please forgive my spelling and grammar I am horrible at it.

### Jim Laird — 2018-02-25T23:58:29+0200

Today's anti Zoo Animal activities.

Reverse osmosis water.
Heavy sea food Keto diet, lots of mushrooms cooked in butter.
30 min Am walk in the rain. It is terrible here right now, i still get outside still makes me feel better even if its raining.
15 min CT in a grounded tub outside 50F
10 mins red light
before bed i will pack myself in ice for 10-15 mis

### Jim Laird — 2018-02-27T02:53:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.20948/post-235818>

Good day today. Finally a sunny day in Kentucky, I was able to get outside plenty today. My biggest struggle is finding balance between helping others and self care. Excited for the spring ! My creek fishing is right around the corner ! So excited to thrive instead of just surviving. I really think this journal is going to help keep me accountable. It's been about three weeks since I added some black bulbs to the gym, I can honeslty say i think it is making a difference. I added a magnetico sleep pad around the same time. Sleeping much better. So who knows what's helping lol I'll take it!

### Jim Laird — 2018-02-27T22:18:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.20948/post-235883>

Good day today so far, tuesday/Thursdays are my long days. Start at 6 am go till noon then I get a short break and go 3:30-7:30pm I work with 4-6 people an hour. One thing I have done that has helped is block off the 8 am hour to get outside into the am sun. Today I did 15 mins of CT in the noon sun. Another sunny day today which is rare for this time of year. Water temp was 38F emerced up to the neck, felt fantastic. I have a metal tub from tractor supply behind my gym, I provide some great entertainment for my neighbors lol

### Jim Laird — 2018-02-28T17:42:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.20948/post-235955>

> [Dylan Petkus said:](/goto/post?id=235886)
>
> Whoa, in a thread with Jim Laird. Have you shared any of these protocols with your clients yet? Anyway to track their progress after having them implement things like CT/ sun/ etc. Able to put full spectrum bulbs into the place?
>
> It's hard to help the kids. It's really the parents that needed to be helped first and then the trickle down begins... (theoretically)

Hi Dylan,

My main focus at the moment is me. My job has been killing me for years and it's my own fault. I've been recommending high quality water and getting outside since I started working with people back in 1997. I've starting sharing some info on light and CT with clients that are open minded. I have a number of kids that have auto immune issues that I have been slowly sharing info with, simple stuff. Seen some nice results. Since I'm not a medical Doc I just present simple info and let them go from there.

I've had some really nice results using the help you lose belly fat angle. I will tell a client that struggles with belly fat to put a couple ice packs on their belly before bed over a t shirt 10-15 mins i will not tell them why other then it helps with fat loss. I'll ask about it a number of weeks later I've had them report back, my sleep has improved, I'm not craving sugar and i'm down a size.

Your spot on with the kids, It's a tough call, I know in my gut that the time to play nice and be patient is coming quickly to an end.

Thanks for saying hi!

Jim

I also know the that the key to me thriving is making outdoor time my number one priority, everything else needs to take a back seat to that. Jim

### Jim Laird — 2018-03-02T02:32:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.20948/post-236115>

Today was one of those days. When you work with people as long as I have you come to the understanding everything runs in waves and seasons, everyone was in a funk today. Some sort of stress or family crisis or drama. This is a huge energy drain, it's a full moon here and the weather has been drepressing, so i don't know if that has something to do with it or not. Got my CT in today in the rain, followed it up with 10 mins of red light. I am smoked, Having a glass of Malbec and some octipus salad and sashmi and calling it a day. I am travelling tomorrow so i need to crash and get my rest. Listened to Dr. K pod cast on 5G still absorbing and thinking about my future plans in this morndern world. One a positive note planning a trip to Playa De Carmen in June so I'm excited about that. Wish I could go sooner just couldn't pull it off.

This to shall pass

### Jim Laird — 2018-03-06T03:36:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.20948/post-236390>

Had a really busy weekend, got lots of outdoor time so it was overall good. Started a three day fast today, so far so good. In the winter (which sucks here in Kentucky) I do 10mins in the tanning bad a couple times a week. Did that today. It's kinda cool it has an AC system that i turn up full blast, it's almost like doing CT. I have my yearly phyiscal on Wednesday I'm intersted to see what the fast does to my bloodwork, and I want to mess with my Dr.  I've also began to three about my game plan for the next 5 years.

### Jim Laird — 2018-03-06T21:55:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.20948/post-236498>

Crazy busy day today. Half way through my fast, I will break my fast after my physical tomorrow at 11am. Fasting is super easy for me because I eat a heavy seas food based Keto diet during the winter. The last time I did a three day fast I used a Dexcom to track my blood sugar. That was really interesting. This morning I got a brisk 30 min walk out in the am sun, I just finished 15 mins of grounded CT in the sun which felt amazing! It sure is nice to have the extra free time because I don't have to sit down and eat. Here is my latest pod cast if anyone is interested. [http://body.io/gym-laird-29-strategies-training-children-elderly-without-pain/](http://body.io/gym-laird-29-strategies-training-children-elderly-without-pain/)

Looking forward to having tomorrow am off.

### Jim Laird — 2018-03-08T03:13:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.20948/post-236650>

Spent most of my Am in a doctors office for my physical, sad being around so many sick people, especally the medical staff. My doctor is a nice guy very open minded. He is always is happy to see me. We have gotten in to many heated debates over the years. He always says we are going to have to agree to disagree but he loves that I care and take ownership of my health. Most people just don't care. Weather was crap today so I did the tanning bed. Broke my three day fast today with sardeans and bacon. It tasted like a desert, super long day tomorrow, time for bed.

### Jim Laird — 2018-03-11T23:39:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.20948/post-237009>

### Jim Laird — 2018-03-13T02:17:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.20948/post-237093>

Weird day in Kentucky today, we had 10+ inches of snow. Most of my morning was spent helping people get unstuck, growing up in Northern Canada winter driving is like second nature for me. Tanning bed this am, got a lift in, then the sun came out. I was out front of my gym in t shirt and shorts reading a book people think I nuts  Weekend was good. I have been really good about my sleep lately, need to walk more outside in the am. Putting more thought into what I want my life to look like in 5 years, all my life I have dreamed about disconnecting I am starting to think now is the time to move in that direction. Looking forward to getting in some CT tomorrow and getting the results of the blood work from my physical.

### Jim Laird — 2018-03-16T03:32:37+0200

### Jim Laird — 2018-04-12T17:40:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.20948/post-239289>

Well I went in for my physical and man Have a made some improvements. At this time I was battling reoccurring infections from my one and only pedicure. Until listening to Dr. Kruse I had not idea how chronically dehydrated i was. Last year at this time my Bun ratio was 28 this time around it was 19. So still some room for improvement but it's exciting to see such rapid changes with such simple changes. So I'm hoping that these new changes will help in keeping my lovely infection at bay.

### Jim Laird — 2018-04-17T14:04:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.20948/post-239496>

Weather here has been terrible. We had a snow storm yesterday, staying on top of CT and tanning bed, looking forward to spring and fishing. Booked a trip to Tulum in early June really.lookong forward to that. I will be making muliple trips down there this winter. Doing research for possible relocation as 5g world becomes a reality.

### Jim Laird — 2018-05-06T03:21:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.20948/post-240602>

Weather is getting much better ! I have been doing CT in the sun pretty much everyday, also doing a really goood job of getting my am light with my feet on the ground. What a difference it has made, making being a none zoo animal my number one priority!

### Jim Laird — 2018-05-16T17:32:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.20948/post-241358>

I have been doing well, integrating the new info I have learned from Dr. Kruse has been super simple and easy which is what most people need. I have started adding a few things to what I suggest to people when they reach out for help. Here is some feedback from a woman that was exercising multiple times a day and still gaining weight.

“Hi Jim, I wanted to check in with you, it’s been three weeks since we talked, I’ve been spending a lot more time outside, always getting the morning sunlight first thing. I’ve also been sitting with ice packs covering most of me almost every evening. I’ve been turning off the WiFi at night and I swear I’m sleeping better. My inflammation is much less, I’m 8 pounds lighter, and my digestion is improving. I am still doing very light exercise but overall so much is improved. I’m wondering if, once I’m ready to add more exercise in, if you’d be willing to talk again? Thank you for all of your help!”

This is really cool. I’ve been recommending people get outside and walk for years. This new info allows me to speed up the process for people that are in a bad place.

Always learning and adapting

Thanks for your help Dr. Kruse it’s so much fun to help those who are willing to try some very simple things.

Jim

### Jim Laird — 2018-05-16T22:46:03+0300

Going to Tulum first week of June, so excited !

### drezy — 2018-05-22T21:14:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.20948/post-241817>

> [Jim Laird said:](/goto/post?id=241777)
>
> Believe it or not I’m a nice person
>
> I wish I had know about the negative side effects of tatoos before I got them ... what’s done is done. Lucky very little red.

I believe it.

To not throw Vikings under the bus either the Vikings are much maligned. Human history is fascinating. The vikings had amazingly ridonuclous women's rights built right into their culture. The women ran all the business the majority of the year as the men were away and swam nude if they liked(1). The women had accessible divorce rights as well (2).

1- I love "outsiders" takes on cultures. A muslim traveller wrote about his time with Vikings and was shocked at some of the practices. His own line makes me laugh when I read it. You have to picture this guy chasing ladies around with a blanket:

"I did not cease to strive \[to induce them\] to make the women cover themselves from the men while swimming"

Now that was culture shock for old [Ahmad ibn Fadlan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ahmad_ibn_Fadlan)

2- This young lass was sick of her husband so got him drunk and had him put on a ladies shirt in public \*bam\* instant divorce and half his money (yikes) [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Laxdæla\_saga#Love\_triangle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Laxdæla_saga#Love_triangle)

Can’t decide if I’m annoyed that they hyphenated badass.

### Jim Laird — 2018-05-23T21:44:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.20948/post-241910>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=241817)
>
> I believe it.
>
> To not throw Vikings under the bus either the Vikings are much maligned. Human history is fascinating. The vikings had amazingly ridonuclous women's rights built right into their culture. The women ran all the business the majority of the year as the men were away and swam nude if they liked(1). The women had accessible divorce rights as well (2).
>
>
> 1- I love "outsiders" takes on cultures. A muslim traveller wrote about his time with Vikings and was shocked at some of the practices. His own line makes me laugh when I read it. You have to picture this guy chasing ladies around with a blanket:
>
> "I did not cease to strive \[to induce them\] to make the women cover themselves from the men while swimming"
>
> Now that was culture shock for old [Ahmad ibn Fadlan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ahmad_ibn_Fadlan)
>
>
> 2- This young lass was sick of her husband so got him drunk and had him put on a ladies shirt in public \*bam\* instant divorce and half his money (yikes) [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Laxdæla\_saga#Love\_triangle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Laxdæla_saga#Love_triangle)

### Jim Laird — 2018-05-26T02:23:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.20948/post-242062>

I’ve been working on intergrating what I’ve learned as much as possible at the gym. I’ve added black lights inside, constantly talking to people about getting outside in the am. Adding ice packs before bed. Now that the weather is better trying to get people outside as much as possible. It’s insane how many people are terrified to take off there shirt or sunglasses because they are afraid I mean terrified of the sun. I guess it’s the old totalitarian motto tell a lie long enough and loud enough ..... chipping away one day at a time.

### Jim Laird — 2018-05-28T13:19:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.20948/post-242191>

Sitting outside the gym feet on the ground getting my am sun. Its hilarious to see the reactions of the people driving by. I've already.had the police called on me twice. Its funny how normal human behavior would be considered so abnormal in our modern world. Its memorial day looking.forward to relaxing and taking care of a few ”housekeeping” items I leave for Tulum next Thursday I can't wait! So looking forward to topping off my tank I plan on going again in September Making a trip to Thailand over Christmas then back to Tulum in Jan/feb its going to be a fun year.

### Jim Laird — 2018-05-28T17:52:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.20948/post-242207>

An interesting observation, a couple months ago I got a magnetico sleep pad. I have two dogs they both have their own beds that they chill on. Ever since I have gotten that pad they have been glued to the bed. I've noticed I sleep much better, I feel more rested even on the nights I don't get as much sleep as I should. Pretty cool. Any one else have any experience with the sleep pad simalar to mine?

Jim

### Jim Laird — 2018-08-08T14:16:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.20948/post-247885>

It’s intersting I was going through some old papers (purging so to speak). I came across some old quick start fat loss guides I used to hand out to clients.

Eat protein at every meal especially at breakfast
Drink plenty of water
Get your sleep
Walk outside everyday.

Dr. Kruse has been a nice slap in the face for me to bring me back to a reality I already knew. I knew since a young boy that loved the outdoors how important it was. I allowed my job and business to consume me becoming and indoor creature so I could “help others”. While destroying myself in the process. Over the last several years I have been battling reoccurring infections that first started from my one and only pedicure. Since being snapped back to reality by Dr. Kruse I have not had a reoccurance I was experiencing 2-3 a year. I now make my am sun exposure a top priority as I wrote this I am shirtless in a park with my feet on the wet grass, I get outside as nakid as I can as much as I can. I wear long sleeves and blue blocking glasses inside. I changed the lighting in my gym, it’s nothing like the sun but it’s all I can do for now. My struggle is with the future Lexington in the next couple years will be going to 5g, I have two years remaining on my lease (gym) I know that people don’t need to spend more time inside they need to be outdoors. I have started the thought process of what to do next, where to live to give me the best opportunity to not only survive but thrive. Looking at my business and if I can even continue with it all. I’m married to it for the next two years, I am constantly talking to clients about getting outside they see me sitting outside with my shirt off all the time. I’m sure most of them think I’ve gone nuts lol (as well as my neighbors lol)

Just a random thought dump ...
I’m doing well but I have some major conflict in my mind ...

I’m excited about the member event in December flight and hotel is booked.

I struggle is real ... how to break out of the system that has made our life so easy .. with out completly destroying ones own existence while trying to help others at the same time.

Jim

### Jenelle — 2018-08-08T16:14:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.20948/post-247893>

[@Jim Laird](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20597/) ~ it is always good to see your updates. I love all the changes you're making, and that you are doing it in the public eye way more than most of us are.

Maybe some do think you're nuts ~ but I can guarantee that you are planting seeds. Funny thing about planting seeds and moving on with your life... you often don't know whether someone else will come along and water that seed. Will it have the right conditions to grow up and poke through the earth ... will it see the sunshine?

I find I can't dwell on it. Not even with the closest members of my own family. I am perfectly content with living my best life & planting seeds where I can. Under just the right conditions.

Best wishes as you move forward on your journey and work through the decisions about your gym. It's a difficult place to be ... needing to provide for your family, wanting to help others, while at the same time striving for not just self-preservation ~ but a deep internal need to \*thrive\*.

Your thoughts resonated with me this morning, as I am in a similar place. Let's keep on seeing that sunrise, man... I wholeheartedly believe it brings us clarity.

### Jim Laird — 2018-10-23T22:25:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.20948/post-254043>

I have been doing very well. Just got done with CT which is fantastic this time of year, with overnight temp approaching freezing the water is perfect right around 55f around mid day. I like to do my CT in the afternoon sun before my 3:30 PM shift. I have been doing an excellent job of keeping up to speed with my am light, it has become a none negotiational activity for me. Will be making some changes soon that will allow me to have even a better am experience. It is close to freezing in the am here now so I get even stranger looks when I'm sitting out in front of my gym with my shirt off getting my sunrise in and everyone walking by is bundled up like it's the artic. Had a great consult with Dr. Kruse on Sunday it was at 8 am in the morning it was fun to sit outside and have a good conversation with him, he gave me some great ideas for my business now and in the future. He also offered me some ideas on how to offset some to the dumbassery of my past performance driven choices. If your a member and you have never done a consult with Dr [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) I highly recommend it. I am excited for the uncoming year and some of the changes I am about to implement. My evolution over the last 25 years in this business has been an interesting one to say the least. If you would have told me 25 years ago that I would be telling people to get outside and not be a Zoo Animal I would hace said you were insaine.

The member event is coming up fast and I am really looking forward to that.

### Jim Laird — 2018-10-24T16:02:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.20948/post-254065>

One thing that has really helped me is taking monthly off the grid breaks. Air BB is great for this Kentucky is great with in an hours drive from Lexington there are areas that do not get any cell service I mean zero. I have been going to this cabin I found a great place in McKee on Airbnb. Check it out: [https://abnb.me/F4bI4q4ahR](https://abnb.me/F4bI4q4ahR) it's cheap and in the middle of no wear. It has WiFi I just unplug it. I will live the entire weekend with candles and fire spend all day outside with my dogs with as little clothes as possible. My sleep is amazing I come back to work so relaxed I almost can't function lol

I so desire simple life like this and I am in the process of putting things into pla to make it happen.

Do a search on air BB for off the grid cabins you will be pleasantly surprised. In the log book at the cabin review after review so happy no cell service ...... People are looking to dissconect. Might be a good business model for the future.

Jim

### Jim Laird — 2018-10-24T16:11:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.20948/post-254066>

I have been researching putting a shipping container under the ground as a strategy for dealing with the upcoming 5 g assult. After during some research shipping containers have a couple drawbacks, weak on the sides they are strong top to bottom desged to be stacked so they can pron to collapse on the side. Some have had harsh chemicals stored inside them. Not very corrosion proof. So looking around I found this site that has shelters designed for being underground starting as little as 20,000 looks like a good option to put beside or under and existing structure as a sleeping area or as Un Der ground strand alone home.

They also have water tight structures for areas with high water tables. I'm sure some of the tornado shelters would do the trick as well.

[https://www.atlassurvivalshelters.com](https://www.atlassurvivalshelters.com)

### Jim Laird — 2018-11-22T04:11:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.20948/post-256266>

i have so much going on right now. So many exciting things happening in January I will be putting myself first for the first time in my life. Totally revamping my buiness. The changes are going to allow me to live a more authentic human life. It's scary and exciting at the same time !
Hopefully it will encourage some of my cleints to make similar changes. Amazing day in Lexington today it was cold but finally sunny. Got an amazing grouned outdoor CT session in this afternoon, followed up by a high speed CT session on my motorcycle. Time is flyin by we are almost a month away from the member event, I am really looking forward to that. It will be a nice reset to come back to my new schedaul changes.

Happy Thanksgiving !

Jim

### Jim Laird — 2018-12-04T03:20:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.20948/post-257213>

It’s been a ruff week and it’s just started. Just let me clients know that I am radically changing my schedaul. Lots of upset clients, they understand why I am doing what I am doing but they are still upset. Have to take care of myself first. This is the first time I’ve ever put myself first. It’s excotong but still difficult. Really looking forward to the member event, winter has been shitty in Kentucky very little sun lots of Cloudy shitty days. I have a sauna at the gym now so looking forward to adding that into my routine. Mexico is going to be great to recharge my batteries to get me through the winter. I’m going to by a toy hauler rv to put on a piece of property I bought that had zero cell phone service I will be spending my weekends there starting in the new year I am really looking forward to that. Just moved out of my house, threw out tons of shit I never used feels so good to downsize. I pretty much have my personal possessions down to what I can fit in my four runner it is so refreshing. I am so excited for my new schedaul and eliminating early mornings. Sipping on Malbec looking to the future. I want to live a more authentic human life.

That’s all for now

Laird out ....

I\`m going to the member even I am so excited to g back to Mexico get recharged and come back and hit the ground running with my new schedule.

Been doing a good job getting my outdoor time my am light is none negotionble. Really looking forward to Jan when my day doesnt start untill 9am

### Jim Laird — 2018-12-21T21:25:01+0200

I am really looking forward to the member event and getting back to Mexico once again to recharge my batteries to help me get through the rest of Kentucky winter. It’s hard to believe it’s almost here. Looking forward to meeting everyone that will be there.

### CarolandNick — 2018-02-25T21:53:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beautiful-man-and-nature-in-tune-and-producing-unrefined-sea-salt.20949/post-235715>

I loved watching this, and I thought some of you might too. The way sea salt is produced in the Guerande and the Isle de Re in Brittany, France is wonderful. Man and nature in harmony. Nothing but wind and sun are used to evaporate the sea water. Nothing but wood or occasionally plastic tools are used as metal reacts with the salt. This is a totally sustainable way of making a living - how fantastic! This guy is so lucky to work in this environment, don't ya think!

### ab70bandit — 2018-02-25T23:39:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beautiful-man-and-nature-in-tune-and-producing-unrefined-sea-salt.20949/post-235716>

What a great video! I just finished reading the book the salt fix, so much great info.
This man in the video is exposed to vast amounts of salt through his skin and respiration alone without consumption and he looks extremely healthy.
I remember when my daughter was 6 or 7 we were on a low salt diet from the Dr. Because of hubby's high blood pressure, I remember walking in the kitchen and finding my daughter with the open salt shaker to her lips...I freeked...realizing now that her body new instintively that she needed more salt.

### CarolandNick — 2018-02-26T10:35:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beautiful-man-and-nature-in-tune-and-producing-unrefined-sea-salt.20949/post-235749>

I haven't yet read that book. I have read some of the French literature on salt, and have recently spoken with a friend who is a French chef/scientist about the French angle on various unrefined salts. In my daily work I am often asked about the differences between unrefined Celtic sea salt and Himalayan salt. Many people assume that Himalayan salt is better because it is from long ago and believe it to be uncontaminated. The French literature seems to suggest this is not correct, and that fresh sea salt is better because the minerals are in an "organic" state rather than fossilised by geologic pressures. They say organic minerals are bio-available - unlike rock minerals. I would love to know the thoughts of the folks here. I am not a scientist, but my instinct is that the sea salt is superior, especially when it is produced using the methods in the above film, as they are in tune with nature's cycles and the salt is not filtered or dried by artificial means - just the sun and the wind. I also read in a book years ago by Jacques De Langre that the sun has a cleaning effect on the sea water as it is carried into the evaporation ponds - I am guessing from what I know now that this is related to the effects of the sun creating EZ water? I have seen recently that the traditional methods of using these filtration ponds also removes any traces of micro-plastic that is in sea water today. Would the EZ effect exclude these micro-beads?

### Sue-UK — 2018-02-26T13:18:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beautiful-man-and-nature-in-tune-and-producing-unrefined-sea-salt.20949/post-235754>

> [CarolandNick said:](/goto/post?id=235749)
>
> I am not a scientist, but my instinct is that the sea salt is superior, especially when it is produced using the methods in the above film, as they are in tune with nature's cycles and the salt is not filtered or dried by artificial means - just the sun and the wind.

From what I've read, we seem to choose between contaminated by modern seas/radioactive elements/be more or less bioavailable/ higher or lower levels of x,y,z .... blah blah .... so I'm not sticking to one.

I recently bought back 1kg of sea salt from Fuerteventura, also done in the traditional way. The harvesting season is longer, but it was out of season, so I had a wander round the ponds and watched the video at the museum. I've got some in a pot with an approx 3/4 teaspoon spoon and its yum. Rather than using a salt mill I spread the salt in little dollops over the plate of food and get some bites of very salty food, some almost none. It seems a more natural way to eat it than evenly coating using a salt mill or using a fine ground salt. I'm drawn to it more than the Redmond salt at the moment. I also make ghee and once I've strained it I add 2 tablespoons of a commercial mix of 50% Hebridean Seaweed (Knotted wrack ground frond) and 50% Cornish Sea Salt per batch made from 500g of butter. That's good tossed on wilted kale or spinach, or dolloped on any veges or meat, on any non seafood meal. When I've run out of the commercial mix I'll make it up with individual seaweed sprinkles and different salts, and ring the changes.

### JanSz — 2018-03-01T15:53:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beautiful-man-and-nature-in-tune-and-producing-unrefined-sea-salt.20949/post-236046>

# Thread: What can happen in a decade when you go vegan under blue light?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-can-happen-in-a-decade-when-you-go-vegan-under-blue-light.20950/>

### Jack Kruse — 2018-02-26T00:19:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-can-happen-in-a-decade-when-you-go-vegan-under-blue-light.20950/post-235719>

In 2009 we have this news........
Sridevi turns vegetarian.......The gorgeous Sridevi owes her svelte and shapely frame to the yoga that she has been practicing for five years. But not many know that the actress has also cut out meat from her diet to maintain her ageless figure. Her trainer Manish Tiwari tells us about her routine.
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

"Sridevi has been doing yoga for five years. She practices for one hour, five times a week. It has changed her body inside out. Yoga cleanses you internally. As long as your organs function perfectly, you will not get any disease. She is looking younger, and there is a glow on her face due to the Surya Namaskars and headstands that she does," remarks Manish.

"_Pranayam, Kapaal Bhaati,_ 50 _Surya Namaskars, Cobra Pose, Padmasana, Naukasana_ (boat pose), _Halasana_ (plow pose) and _Mastyasana_ (fish pose) are some of the postures that she does. In one session, we do about 30-40 postures. All these tones the muscles regulates breathing increases blood flow, and balances everything in your body, resulting in a feeling of well-being and health. She likes all the postures in yoga," says the yoga expert.

Regarding Sridevi's diet, he says, "She eats everything that is healthy and nutritious. She is not allowed to eat fried or oily foods, but once a week she can indulge herself. In fact, we must all indulge ourselves once a week. She used to love non-vegetarian food, but has become a vegetarian now because she feels that is better for her body."

Link [http://www.starboxoffice.com/newsDetails.aspx?xfile=2009/May/News\_20090514\_82](http://www.starboxoffice.com/newsDetails.aspx?xfile=2009/May/News_20090514_82)

Looks like Yoga is not preventative in this case considering she was found in a bathtub drunk........why is that? Is dopamine related to booze use? Are dopamine and blue light related? Is a low dopamine state and veganism linked? Mitochondriacs already know the answers.

### JanSz — 2018-03-22T14:12:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aussies-deuterium-depleted-water.20955/post-237731>

I suggest that in the mean time everybody who plans on using DDW, do Breathy and urine test.
To have a baseline.
Otherwise future discussions will be handicapped, (and have less added value).

(I meant urine and not saliva).
Boros makes no difference or small difference between saliva and urine testing.
When I am doing mine, decided on urine.
Guessing that it should be little more repeatable of the two (and little easier to get sample).
(Saliva, may become a mixed with some blood or thinned by recently eaten or drank food).

....
Currently I am waiting for my third set of tests.
All using (one part of DDW-25ppm) mixed with (two parts of water)
Use only for drinking (including cofee). Not used for cooking.

My tests are:

Baseline
30 day
90 days

\----------------
After my 30 day tests, I had suggestions from CignatureHealth to change to (one part DDW-25 with one part of water), but decided to stick with (1+2) in attempt to see my (likely maximum) gains with least costly version.

..............................

Yes.

Actually there are some additional details.

So my first 30 days were on two parts of Coconut water (156 ppm).
The next 60 days were on two parts of Aquafina.
I keep using Aquafina for now, not sure if I will continue (in the view of AU fiasco),
since I tested my Costco water (that is real cheap), and have 149.4 ppm
\----
As you know, Australian Aquafina (152 ppm)
not to be confused with Aquapana (148 ppm)
neither of them worth much as far as D is concerned.

How (I am guessing, American) Aquafina got 139.5 ppm is interesting (but I have seen that number with my own eyes.), it diapered since I published it.

Purea, I have never heard of it.
I heard Jason speak about:
Qlarivia
Evian
Aquapana
Aquafina
Egyptian water
Icelandic glacial
Fiji
Voss

Indeed when hearing Aquapana there was a poor sound and I re-winded to hear it second time, still poor sound.
I thought I was hearing Aquafina twice, but since there were two different ppm numbers, it must be about different waters.
So possibly it is not exactly Aquapana, but I am guessing that it was not Purea.
I am talking about the Q&A presentation, there might been others.
\------
Generally speaking Australian waters are higher in D.
But Jack is praising some AU wines.
Possibly water from springs located there are worth investigating.

\-------
RO (I guess common RO) does not change D content.

...........

Not sure if my poor sound reception is because that how it was recorded or because of my equipment.
Sometime for example, some people, not all, asking questions come extremely loud (crisp and clear),
and right after that Jacks answers have such a low volume that I can hardly hear anything.
The bad part is that I listen to it at my maximum volume, so no room for adjustment.

I have specially bought a speaker with additional amplification.
I would not be able to hear much at all when it would be coming directly from my computer speakers.

@[Brent Patrick](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19843/)
In a way,
since I live in USA and use Aquafina,

What is the ppm value for Aquafina that you heard Jason saying?

I realize that there may be difference between USA, AU and other locations where Aquafina is sold.

\===============
AU waters have wide ppm difference, 149-161
161 is worst than my coconut water (156ppm).
It may be worth to be selective when using water that is add on to Qlarivia.

\===============
My experience with DDW is posted, including all my tests.
Since beginning till now I am on 1+2 mix.
My next test results are expected to come at about Aripril 1/18.

Unless I get input from @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) that will change what I do,
my overall plan is to get max ATP-RPM.
(I may miss what Jack says, either miss his posting or may not sink into my brains properly),
so I would appreciate help of others.

\===========================
My overall impression about use of DDW.

#1-- everybody, without testing, can use 125ppm, based on 125ppm being given for drinking on DDW conferences in Hungary, as shown on pictures.
#2-- likely everybody can use 105ppm and 85ppm, but it would be nice to do testing. People currently having high blood glucose or high HgA1c or high fasting insulin, specially those who take medications regulating above, should increase monitoring. That is monitoring of (glucose, HgA1c and insulin). Breathy and urine/saliva tests from Cignature health are nice to have).
#3 DDW lower than 85ppm would call for decent monitoring

How dr Somlyai see treatment of cancer stricken mammal he posts in his book.
I have included page from it below.
Note time and progression over planned 14 months therapy.
**Note that DDW-25 is newer used.**
This is from the old book, within some research papers they talk about using DDW-25.

Page#2

### Lahelada — 2018-03-23T15:25:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aussies-deuterium-depleted-water.20955/post-237829>

Jansz, there is a very important factor to this. How do you feel? Are you ripping out trees with your bare hands , can now carry a Venti coffee instead of a grande?
We have no idea yet what the numbers mean and if the numbers should be different according to where you live. I do not only mean geographically but environmentally. We are looking for the ideal number on a scale that ranges from autophagy to tumor metastasis,trying to assure one while excluding the other. That will be determined by environment. Really everything said about changing environment etc has to come first.

Maybe Joe Bloggs should only drink water as deuterium depleted as it occurs naturally and leave the stronger versions for disease mitigation with help. Most of use do not structure biohacks scientifically enough to assure quality. We talk about ppm,but we do not talk about how often and how much and how long. We may get to a point where for survival we need to throw caution to the wind. Are we at that point yet or do we have some rope ,some unexplored road left,a more inconvenient one? I am not talking about science here but our personal comfort zones. DDW is a convenient form of supplementation. One can even feel virtuous about spending so much on ones health.

Do not get me wrong. This is exciting stuff that will save lives!!! It needs an awful lot of research still though so whoever wants to be an early adopter needs to get into the nitty gritty of things beyond copy cat behaviour. Just reading here on the forum without being able to put the information into personal context will be too little information to go on.

### JanSz — 2018-09-03T20:20:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aussies-deuterium-depleted-water.20955/post-250060>

SMOW measurements given is -35.7 to -41.
\---------------------------------------------------------------
\-35.7 promille--->> -3.57 percent
155\*3.57/100=5.5335
155-5.5335=149.4665

**I tested water from my kitchen sink, 150.2
your water have less deuterium.

Yours is good water to use when mixing it with
Preventa-15
or
Qlarivia

Today is a 3rd day when I mix two parts od 25ppm water with one part of water similar to yours.
I buy Costco's RO, their cheapest.
But really will have to just use my kitchen sink faucet more.

According to latest known to me information from dr Boros
the goal should be to have
(90-125)ppm
in body fluids.
**

\---------------------------------------------------------------
\-60 promille = -6 percent. VSMOW = 155ppm deuterium, 6 percent of that ~ 9ppm, so Gerolsteiner has about 147 ppm deuterium. Which is the number you can find in Germany, confirmed by other sources.
\-60 promille = -6 percent. VSMOW = 155ppm deuterium, 6 percent of that ~ 9ppm, so Gerolsteiner has about 147 ppm deuterium. Which is the number you can find in Germany, confirmed by other sources.

Bad news now confirmed for OZ. Australian 5G update
Telstra is using a vendor (Ericsson) to roll out 5G equipment across the country same way they installed 4G. They're using these new Ericsson 64 Transmit x 64 Receive Massive MIMO 3.5GHz antennas (with integrated radio units inside). Current 4G antennas are up to 8 Transmit x 8 Receive in total). Input port powers look to be the same as 4G but of course, the waveforms are way denser.
“Adelaide's first 5G mobile base station is in Flinders Street; Canberra's is in Pialligo, with more going live on Thursday in Phillip, Lyons, and Fyshwick; while Perth's first 5G site will be in Narrows Bridge in the CBD. Telstra said it now has 50 base stations live across its 5G network; however, there are no commercial devices that currently make use of it."
"Penn last month told ZDNet that Telstra already has its 5G network live across 15 towers -- 13 in the Gold Coast, one in Toowoomba, and one in Brisbane, with 200 to go live by the end of 2018."
"Initially by having the 5G network ready, what that enables us to do is to trial and test the early versions of the handsets and dongles and mobile hotspots and tablets, and the manufacturers come through and test them outside of the lab environment and in a commercial environment."

### JanSz — 2018-11-21T15:26:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aussies-deuterium-depleted-water.20955/post-256177>

Hydrogen
δX=-0.8
δX=-0.8+1.5=0.7
δX=-0.8-1.5=-2.3

### Bezza — 2018-11-22T00:52:37+0200

Thank you so much [@JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) ! You're a superstar! Even the lab said it was too technical for them to do the conversion

The water i had tested wasn't from Australia, unfortunately. It was Qomolangma Glacier water that i picked up in China. The packaging has government seals, serial numbers, iso certification, etc. and claim this water is -143‰ (which i worked out was approx. 132.8ppm but it is actually ~156ppm). Maybe i didn't sample it correctly but glad i had it checked. Seems this company can't be trusted so I wont be buying any more of it. So far it still looks like Qlarivia is the cheapest. Even other Chinese DDW worked out to be between A$35/ltr to over A$100/ltr delivered to an address in China!

Thanks again for your help on this. At least i now have a lab who can test deuterium in Australia. I sent my Breathy sample back to Cignature Health yesterday. Looking forward to receiving results soon....

A change in 0.7%. Hope this helps someone

# Thread: Howdy from Torquay, Victoria, Australia

### Lyndal — 2018-02-26T10:12:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/howdy-from-torquay-victoria-australia.20956/post-235748>

Howdy folks,

Totally feeling way in over my head here. Trusting as my being heals, my smarts will improve and I’ll understand more and more.

That being said, the golden nuggets I’ve received thus far are priceless and motivated me to join as a Gold Member and persevere further expanding my knowledge and mindset.

I’m beginning with the Epi-Paleo Rx, Sunrises, purchasing some Blue Blockers (for the whole family) and would like to incorporate CT once I get my head around these first few steps. Oh and Water. After listening to today’s Q & A, so much to learn about Water.

I trust Dr Kruse immensely, something in my gut says to do so and have eaten Seafood very rarely over the past few years due to, what was diagnosed as, severe Mercury / Heavy Metal poisining. I had my first dozen raw Oysters last night for dinner and am looking forward to my second sitting tonight!

Does anybody have a recipe for Seafood Broth they would be willing to share with me please?

Is the eCookbook coming back any time soon?

I look forward to sharing this journey with you all.

Cheers,
Lyndal

### Laura C — 2018-02-26T16:31:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/howdy-from-torquay-victoria-australia.20956/post-235762>

Greetings and Welcome! I had this spiritual moment, where the puzzle pieces of optimal wellness came together when I started reading Dr. Jack's blog articles. A facebook friend had shared a Dr Jack Kruse post and I have been devoting my internet time to reading the articles and listening to podcasts. I had been seeking wellness, for the last 25 years, through diet, minerals, exercise and missing the solar foundation entirely. I've lived in the northern Midwest, USA, my entire life save the year I lived in Alaska and the summer I spent in Florida when I was 18 yrs old. Everything here from marketing to doctor advice is hide from the sun, cover yourself with clothes, sunscreen, hats and sunglasses because the sun causes cancer. I crave seafood and meat(raw to boot) and love sitting out in the yard weeding by hand. In Wisconsin, where I live, eating local fish isn't recommended due to mercury and PCB's in our waters. Being outside, felt natural to me and I didn't burn and stopped using sunscreen 3-4 years ago. Anyway, enough about me...

Do you know why you had heavy metal/mercury poisoning? Was it cosmetic related, dental, water?

We recently moved from a community that had a bad water that contained aluminum (factory dumping) and hexavalent chromium in the water and was on some federal cleanup list. That's when we started drinking bottled water.

Thanks for the welcome Laura and a bit of a bio from you too. It’s funny how we can believe we are on the path of wellness, when in fact we are killing ourselves. Actually, apologies, I can only speak for myself.

When I was diagnosed with Mercury / Heavy Metal Poisoning I thought I was the healthiest I’d ever been in my life. Was eating a vegetarian / vegan diet, I’d lost over 60kg (132 lb), my body clock had me rising at 4:30am and on it goes.

They say the Mercury poisoning was from an accumulation of a mouth full of amalgams, being a sick child I had way more injections than usual, eating fish and who knows from what else? They say the weight loss, combined with a dodgy tooth at the time, a fever too, is why it went to my brain.

I’m looking forward to creating true health!

# Thread: Maple Syrup
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maple-syrup.20958/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kris90 — 2018-02-26T15:37:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maple-syrup.20958/post-235760>

So up here in Canada, we are getting ready for maple syrup season which I believe begins mid March.

I wasn't planning to add carbs back to my diet until probably May, but in terms of eating locally, I wanted to get some opinions on maple syrup? Does Mother Nature have a purpose to provide this sort of sweetness prior to the budding of trees, or is it likely a trap? Maybe a way for animals to put on a little bit of fat just to help get them by until spring? Deuterium content in maple syrup?

I also wonder if we continue our CT and remain cold adapted, would having some sugar while its available be simply burned off as heat from our BAT activation assuming we had a good redox and could uncouple? Or would having a load of sugar from maple syrup turn insulin signalling back on, similar to how bears eat honey after hibernation? I assume if we keep high FFAs flowing in the blood, it should be enough to drive physiological insulin resistance and uncoupling if we continue cold stimulus.

### kris90 — 2018-02-26T17:25:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maple-syrup.20958/post-235771>

> For typical **sugar** maple **sap** concentration is about 2%. It takes high amounts of about 40-50 gallons to make one gallon of syrup.

I think that kind of answers itself. When you concentrate a ridiculous amount of sap to make syrup, you dramatically increase the sugar content. Doesn't seem natural.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-02-26T17:33:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maple-syrup.20958/post-235774>

OK when sun gets stronger.....harvest then eat

# Thread: John Ott and Red light
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/john-ott-and-red-light.20961/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 23
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Omelchenko — 2018-02-27T03:33:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/john-ott-and-red-light.20961/post-235821>

Hey everyone, I just finished the first book in the stack of books I purchased on quantum biology. That book is Health and Light. In the book Dr. Ott discusses breifly major issues with mammels under red light. Essentially, there are issues anytime plants or animals are under any sort of artificial light. I know red light doesn't affect circadian rhythm to a large degree. Is the recommendation for red light at night simply the lesser of the two evils? As in, it doesn't mess with melatonin but constant exposure would cause issues? Also, in regareds to blue blockers, would that be of concerns due to it transmitting an unnatural spectrum to the eye? Or is it more akin to fire light at night? Thanks for the help

### WalterNL — 2018-02-27T08:50:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/john-ott-and-red-light.20961/post-235827>

I believe blacklight is the least of all evils at night. And regarding you last question about an unnatural spectrum, for that reason, Alexander Wünsch prefers glasses that reduce all frequencies as opposed to just blocking blue. There's something to be said for that, though I guess it largely depends on how much blue is in your environment after sunset.

### Sue-UK — 2018-02-27T12:02:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/john-ott-and-red-light.20961/post-235834>

When I was looking for a chart to show the 0 degree solar elevation frequencies I came across this. a) is for rural b) for city. (source [https://www.nature.com/articles/srep26756#f1](https://www.nature.com/articles/srep26756#f1) )
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" } [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/upload_2018-2-27_9-5-12-png.6870/)
I don't use red light or red blue blockers at night because I read that red raises IOP. Also I don't know that using red doesn't affect circadian rhythm, because it might be doing it by preventing what it should see. There is a lot of deeply saturated blues in the post sunset solar elevations, visible red isn't dominant. I think we're looking for highly saturated, deepening into darkness frequencies, with no spikes. (Reality 4 says that melanopsin works with light 430-465 nm at dusk, so I'm interested in the dusk frequencies). The frequencies change, and the transitions are important I think because the brain responds to change, and doesn't expect "same". I'm also not sure about using UV at night, with or without red. From the frequency charts I've seen, yellow starts to rise dramatically at a +6 solar elevation in the morning (when UV would be coming in) and decrescendos in late afternoon, with a very low ratio by -6 solar elevation in the evening. If its the drop in yellow and absence of UV that signals circadian rhythm changes at that time (eg for body temp to rise, the leptin receptor, or the blood to come to the skin surface to harvest light, when it doesn't risk UV bleaching the red blood cells), then UV at night might interfere with one or several rhythms.

### Awainer1 — 2018-02-27T18:19:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/john-ott-and-red-light.20961/post-235853>

Wunsch said in a podcast that any light after dark reduces melatonin. He said that we’ve had enough time using fire to adapt to it and also that light at night should be used from a lower level not from above. I use a littlite with DC input and use the red filter at night.

### Sue-UK — 2018-02-28T12:30:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/john-ott-and-red-light.20961/post-235928>

From a different source, the +6 and -6 frequencies and irradiance differences.
[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/upload_2018-2-28_8-53-49-png.6875/) (Source [http://jeb.biologists.org/content/219/12/1779](http://jeb.biologists.org/content/219/12/1779) _Using light to tell the time of day: sensory coding in the mammalian circadian visual network).
_
This one gives a visual of a colour sensitive SCN neuron versus a brightness sensitive SCN neuron.
[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/upload_2018-2-28_9-1-12-png.6876/) (Source [http://journals.plos.org/plosbiology/article?id=10.1371/journal.pbio.1002160](http://journals.plos.org/plosbiology/article?id=10.1371/journal.pbio.1002160) _A Colourful Clock_)

_"Thus, entrainment of the circadian system combines both environmental irradiance and colour information to ensure that internal and external time are appropriately aligned".

"The spectral composition of light reaching the earth shows less day-to-day variability in spectral composition than in irradiance, and thus, it may have a high predictive value about the position of the sun, as originally predicted by Foster (e.g., 2001)."

_

### JanSz — 2018-02-28T18:15:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/john-ott-and-red-light.20961/post-235958>

I like to test.
Light or absence of light is a driver for circadian Rhythm.
The best way that I know about testing Circadian Rhythm is testing cortisol and melatonin.
I newer before tested melatonin.
I tested cortisol previously and found it very low on, barely over low curve.
I live in USA zip 07054
So latitude about 40 deg north
Based on @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) teachings I am doing my best with sun exposure,
and also have series of artificial light next to my desk where I spend lots of my time.
My artificial lights, (and day/night time) are best described when reading this post:
**Artificial lighting management for a human locked in small room.**
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738)

I am 77yo.
I did DUTCH test in December 2017

**Comments are welcomed,**
but from DUTCH I got comments that my curves are highly exaggerated.
They have not seen such curves before.
(I would like to assume that this exaggeration is positive and due to my light management.)
Remember, I started with being real close to the low limit.
Previously (before learning about light effects), I was using HC-hdrocortisone or Medrol.
Latter I learned (a little) about light, so that allowed me to wean out of HC and medrol.
But Jack's teaching on light did the rest.

My melatonin is rather low.
Not really sure how to deal with it (naturally) (other than what I am doing now).
///

...
One possibility about melatonin is that I force myself to stay in bed for 8hrs.
Practically I need less bed time, likely 7hr would be enough or even less.

### Sue-UK — 2018-03-01T11:26:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/john-ott-and-red-light.20961/post-236027>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=235958)
>
> **Comments are welcomed,**

"My current understanding, may change any minute.
If someone sleeps erratic lengths of time he is exposed to a jet lag.
**Artificial lighting management for a human locked in small room.**
Day length=24hrs
Light--14 hrs
dark--10hrs
sleep--8hrs
Use minimum to no clothes 24/7
\----------------------------------------------
Wakeup-6AM
Bed time-10PM
\----------------------------------------------
At 6AM for 2-3 minutes look directly at one Solar Glow 160W
to shut off pituitary hormone production
edit 6/7/2017
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/methylene-blue.19593/page-2#post-215566](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/methylene-blue.19593/page-2#post-215566)
\----------------------------------------------
Light 6AM-8PM using
2-Exo Terra Solar Glow 160W
2 of RubyLux ALL RED
1 RubyLux Infrared Bulb NIR-A
\----------------------------------------------
Light 8PM-10PM
2 of RubyLux ALL RED
1 RubyLux Infrared Bulb NIR-A
\---------------------------------------------
Night
No light at all, any windows blocked completely from the light
Hand held red light similar to RubyLux ALL RED can be used on the way to the bathroom"

I don't agree that if someone sleeps erratic lengths of time, he is exposed to jet lag. At my latitude day and night lengths vary through the seasons, but as the seasons change, the day length increases or decreases by a few minutes daily (unless they are mucking about with an hour's change for British Summer Time ). The natural slight change over time in day length isn't erratic, it follows a cycle, and animals in the wild physiologically responding to natural changes in day length aren't experiencing jet lag. How long they sleep may vary depending on light cycle, temperature, or just how knackered/sick they are. I expect to sleep longer in winter (when darkness has fallen by 5 p.m. and the sun doesn't rise until nearer 8 a.m. ) than in summer when day lengths are much longer. But because its a slow transition in line with the solar rhythm, I don't think it is "erratic". Alexander Wunsch told a member at a consultation that he thought we needed more than 8 hours darkness to regenerate at night, so being in total darkness for up to 12 hours in winter at a latitude when I'm dealing with little or no sun during the day, would be natural, and having done it over the winter myself, I have slept longer. The longest I have slept is probably about 10 hours, but I was more physically and mentally tired than usual. If I was sleeping 10 hours plus a night 365 days a year at my latitude I would be worried, but I see it as compensating for sleeping less in summer because of the light cycle.

I see the solar glow at 6 am is a mismatch. There is no UV at sunrise, and if I was to use UV then I would be keeping the pituitary hormones switched off, instead of turning them on and then turning them off at the right time. The 6 a.m. to 8 p.m. set up is also "same" - it seems more like noon from 6 a.m. to 8 p.m, then there's a nightfall set up that is nothing like the evening frequencies I've seen. I think the brain is looking for the transitions. My thought experiment  has been .... assuming the only thing left to make a return trip to Mars safe was a circadian rhythm friendly lighting system ......set up to mimic my latitude ....how would I do it ....... I could be a while ...

### JanSz — 2018-03-01T14:24:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/john-ott-and-red-light.20961/post-236035>

My
**Artificial lighting management**
is not meant to be a perfect replacement of natural day/night cycle.
If anything it is extreme toward the good side.
I think of the longest day at my (or your) latitude as a model for it.

To me the other extreme was a year that I spend in Brazil, latitude 23 south.
After 3 or 6 months I had a hard time getting used to this 12h light/12 hr dark cycle.

And I force my self to stay in bed for 8 hrs,
but I do not use any alarm clocks.
Once or twice a year I sleep longer than 8 hrs.

....

### Sue-UK — 2018-03-02T12:07:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/john-ott-and-red-light.20961/post-236136>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=236035)
>
> My **Artificial lighting management is not meant to be a perfect replacement of natural day/night cycle.**
> If anything it is extreme toward the good side.
> I think of the longest day at my (or your) latitude as a model for it.
> ....

I think one problem with it is that the model picks a set of a range of frequencies and, even if they were "right" or similar enough to a particular solar elevation on the longest day, it doesn't change for 14 hours, and there's no seasonal variation (so if my light set up says longest day, should my eating pattern say the same, even in winter?). The human in a locked room (or working all day say in an office, arriving in the dark), would not have the seasonal crescendoing and decrescendoing light signals for a normal hormone pattern, including cortisol. I see noon as a momentary pause, between the crescendoing and decrescendoing of the morning and afternoon frequencies. If, by never changing, the set up signals "noon", I visualise it as saying "wait", which would have consequences on other circadian rhythms that rely on the missing crescendoing and decrescendoing light signals. I personally believe (at the moment .... it might change ....) that the descrescendoing frequencies of late afternoon, when yellow and UV are dropping, and/or when green is becoming more dominant over yellow, is the signal for the blood to come to the surface to harvest light, and repair the skin. I've watched my own skin pink up after getting out of high UV sunlight and under the shade of a leafy tree - going in and out of a green canopy alters my skin response in seconds.

If, in the morning its the early crescendoing light without UV that signals preconditioning the skin ready for UV, I see it that decrescendoing frequencies, such as yellow and UV dropping, which leave others more dominant at certain times, or in certain places (such as the shade of a tree) makes biologic sense for skin repair and signalling light collection.

### Sue-UK — 2018-03-03T10:45:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/john-ott-and-red-light.20961/post-236193>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=236139)
>
> Please post if you have idea on improving my setup, while keeping it simple and easy to use daily?
> ///

If I deliberately use artificial light during the day, its as a treatment 10-20 mins max, and I pick a time to do it that I think is least damaging to the circadian rhythm, and get outside and retune my thalamus afterwards.

In general, I use windows, and let the computer screen wear the blockers.  Even closed, the windows let in the NIR and all visible frequencies, so I sense the natural fluctuations. If I can't have the windows open to let in UV, I have a black light that I can switch on to put photons into the room, but I'm not looking at it directly, and it is never on before yellow is crescendoing in the morning, or after yellow has descrescendoed in the afternoon . But its rare I use it, I prefer getting outside to ground at regular intervals, even if its just for a few minutes. I can tap into other senses at the same time, which gives my brain more accurate information. I want inside the house to match the outside light frequencies as much as possible.

In terms of the whole house, before the winter we swapped out 5 doors that didn't need privacy, from solid wood to glazed. That, pale walls and a couple of large mirrors in an otherwise gloomy corridor, makes a difference. I haven't needed to use artificial ambient light between first light to sunset at all over the winter. I've wanted to match the seasonal light frequencies to the other environmental information I'm getting. My office is in an unheated conservatory. On good days over winter its a sun trap, and I've opened the doors to the main house and used it to heat the house during the day. Other days I just work in the cold. So as much natural light as possible during the day, and the least unnatural light after sunset, I've aimed for 10 -12 hours of total darkness at night in winter, whilst living in the cold lane. Next year I'm going to hack up to 15 hours darkness at night, if that's what the environment is giving me. Bring it on .......

### Sue-UK — 2018-03-04T17:23:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/john-ott-and-red-light.20961/post-236270>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=236262)
>
> So you would rather see 13 mid nights?
> Lets work on middle ground.
>
> ..

As it gets dark at night, light is no longer dominant, (or shouldn't be) and we enter the magnetotail, melatonin rising. I see midnight as the "pause" when leptin does its thing, and then we go through the phase of slowly exiting the magnetotail, which influences circadian rhythms such as lowest body temperature, cortisol beginning to rise etc. I doubt I could duplicate that with my lighting set up during the day if I tried to (say by blacking out the house or going deep underground 13 times), because my latitude isn't passing through the magnetotail at the time.

### Sue-UK — 2018-03-05T16:01:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/john-ott-and-red-light.20961/post-236332>

I thought this pic was interesting in relation to the changing colour frequencies as the day progresses. For shorter days the night time "wedge" would be larger.

[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/upload_2018-3-5_13-6-54-png.6946/)

I found the pic on this article about bio adaptive lighting [http://sun-lightsolutions.com/our-products/bio-adaptive-lighting/](http://sun-lightsolutions.com/our-products/bio-adaptive-lighting/). At first glance the pic seems to suggest that blue light in the evening is not bad, but from the article _" For example a study by the School of Psychology in Adelaide showed that the shorter wavelengths of 470, 497, and 525 nm showed the greatest melatonin suppression from 65% to 81%. It is important to manage certain wavelengths of the visible light spectrum at the right times in order to deliver effective bio-adaptive lighting."_ The - 6 solar elevation chart shows those frequencies dropping considerably when compared to the +6 solar elevation.
[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/upload_2018-3-5_13-43-55-png.6947/)

Alexander Wunsch says that at the red end of the spectrum, highly saturated red is stimulating, whereas unsaturated is sedating, but its the reverse for blue. So I read it that naturally occurring highly saturated blues becoming more dominant going into darkness in the evening are sedating whereas as the sun rises in the morning they become less dominant and the main melatonin suppressing frequencies increase.

Having seen the Ott video on improving hyperactivity in the classroom by a change in the lighting , this was interesting too ..

_"A study in the Netherlands found that increasing illuminance levels in schools at certain times and changing the colour temperature of the lights indicated a positive influence pupils’ concentration. The Philips Schoolvision case study in Hamburg showed remarkable results from applying controllable adaptive lighting in a school environment and empowering the teachers with the ability to affect the lighting conditions. Reading speed increased by almost 35%, concentration improved dramatically and the frequency of errors dropped by almost 45%. Hyperactivity and aggression were also examined. Although the perceived reduction in aggression was not found to be significant, video evidence showed a distinct change in levels of hyperactivity. Observed hyperactivity was reduced by up to 76% when pupils were given a mathematical problem to solve under the Calm lighting scene, a figure that the baseline measurement and control group did not even come close to."_

# Thread: What order of educating for Hashi's?
