### LieselK — 2018-02-27T22:26:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-order-of-educating-for-hashis.20965/post-235884>

I'm curious to hear from you Hashi folks on the order you would recommend reading stuff or watching stuff. I will be able to print out a large amount of material next week so I want to have a list ready. I've already read and printed:

CPC #9: Hashimoto's and Melasma: Gateway Diseases (read through comments also)
BG 6: Epi-Paleo Rx (comments also)
BG 12: Dare to Disagree? (comments also)
Leptin Rx
Leptin Chap 1
Redox Rx
CT Rx (comments also)

I've watched:
Vermont 2016
Vermont 2017

Feeling overwhelmed and like I'm not really understanding my disease. Ugh. I read Jack's book in the past as well as Dr. Kharrazian and Isabella Wentz... course I lent those books out and they are MIA.

### LieselK — 2018-02-27T23:37:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-order-of-educating-for-hashis.20965/post-235893>

> [Dylan Petkus said:](/goto/post?id=235887)
>
> I'm sure others here with Hashi's that have improved can chime in to what they consider as essential in understanding the disease. I can only make some general reccommendations.
>
> There is no one best path. You'll have to wander a bit, but make sure you keep a map! That is, journal/ take notes so that you can begin to connect the dots that Jack has laid out.
>
> You don't have to learn about Hashi's per se. You must learn about Hashi's in your context (environment)!
>
> Learn the traditional understanding of Hashi's first. Draw some connections to our poor understanding of this disease with your learning.
>
> Best of luck!

Thanks! I am meandering and not sure how hopeful to be. Started my journal a few weeks ago and I'm going to create a paper binder at home for all my reading. Paper and I work well together. Keepin' on keepin' on...

### cap — 2018-02-28T02:49:57+0200

So, I went to have a skin check and the dermatologists for curiosities sake and she clearly pointed out age marks ...sunspots formed on my face. After embracing the advice on the blog it makes me a little scared. I use no lotions/cremes of any kind besides coconut oil/olive oil.

So the sun does in fact cause these?
Are they reversible?
What should I do?

### Sean Waters — 2018-02-28T13:02:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunspots.20967/post-235929>

### Patrick K — 2018-02-28T21:53:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunspots.20967/post-235977>

yes UV does damage DNA in studies (and face skin is very thin)... Now have they controlled for fake lights, hydration, deuterium....I dont think so...but the sun's UV can destroy stuff ...its very powerful (which isnt a bad thing) ...so be gradual to let the body gradually adapt like a muscle...or you will over reach

I feel that if you've got plenty of good water in you and that you are grounded while exposed ...damage is less...and getting a progressive tan will help.

Also...get out in the morning or before UV to help the body with the UV (red IR)...

To restore damaged skin...I ordered some Methylene Blue face cream...will update...sounds goodf

### Lahelada — 2018-03-12T15:40:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunspots.20967/post-237040>

Melanocyte-stimulating hormone (MSH) is a collective name for a group of peptide hormones produced by the skin, eye, pituitary gland and hypothalamus in response to ultraviolet radiation. It plays a key role in producing colored pigmentation found in the skin, hair and eyes of mammals. It does this by inducing specialized skin cells called melanocytes to produce melanin which store UV light, until night fall, when our bodies begin to harvest this light, to drive regeneration programs in our tissues. It will not work if blue light, specifically is being sensed by our surfaces. (**TIME 12**)

"UV turns off your hormone and if you have a less than optimal hormone panel the ratio of early AM sun/mid day UV needs to be optimized to more early AM sun"

BUT :

"Sunlight hits the skin and what happens? 40-60% of you blood volume goes to the surface and what light source does this help most? UVA. Look at the penetration it gets. Hb absorbs what? 250-600 nm light onto RBC's to deliver into the blood plasma which is 93% water. Did you know that MB can improve the skin's ability to absorb more light too? This is why MB is used in skin clinics. It makes you look younger because you absorb more UV-A light. **"[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/methylene-blue.19593/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/methylene-blue.19593/)**

My answer to this is to go out in am sun /sunrise without anything. When I go out next I put on an MB spritz. I want max UV to counteract betatrophin.(Betatrophin leads to less fat burning because it suppresses adipose triglyceride lipase, an enzyme that breaks down stored fat. _A lack of UV light from the retinal pathways raises betatrophin. **Time 12** again)_ I use red light in the evening. But too much blue still ,so limited/no results.

# Thread: Hello from Seattle
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-seattle.20969/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AstralJam8 — 2018-02-28T10:18:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-seattle.20969/post-235925>

Hi folks, I found this forum through an unusual set of circumstance. I've been all over the "alternative energy" scene since ~2000, so I've collected a lot of seemingly disparate information and woven it into a rolling context in the back of my mind. The ironic thing is, my pursuits in the realm of Energy/Efficiency were hamstrung before they began by a personal energy crisis, a losing battle with Chronic Fatigue which began a decade earlier when I moved to Washington State. Anyway, to how I got here, about a week ago I was boiling some water for a meal and had a glass of cold water sitting close enough to the burner to receive lots of Infrared but not so close that convection currents would transfer any heat. After I had turned off the heat and started my meal, I noticed that one glass of water tasted/felt incredibly good, I couldn't get enough of it! This happened a few times before but not reliably and somehow I hadn't made the connection with all the stuff I had read in the past about "structured water", EZ, etc. in places like Dave Asprey's interview with Gerald Pollack, translated articles from Viktor Schauberger and various other obscure references. So I started searching around for an Infrared connection and found that it is clearly implicated! Now I'm in the process of attempting to replicate this phenomenon more reliably, so I went looking for a community where this was discussed openly and without the usual cynicism that buries most threads on the topic, which led me here.

So about my health, I've had a gradually worsening CFS since the 90's when I moved from Wyoming to Washington, and it has gotten to the point where my gut has become extremely sluggish, borderline Gastroparesis (a common complication for Diabetics no less..) I've "interviewed" countless doctors who all ran the same tests and gave the same banal litany of non-answers / dismissals with no way forward. I've seen a few FM practitioners who were infinitely more helpful (1 is infinitely greater than 0 at least..) and discovered I had "Adrenal fatigue", "Thyroid insufficiency" and chronic Inflammation which explained why I was developing Arthritis in my 20's. After making some changes to my diet (something akin to Paleo, as close as I could match between work and home,) the Arthritis subsided but I was still Dead Tired 24/7. Some rather complex supplement regimens produced lackluster improvements and were too costly so I've been keeping it to a minimum of some minerals & aminos + digestive enzymes because apparently stuff was not moving and just rotting in my gut for days on end. I haven't been able to break 125lbs in my entire life because I just can't force myself to eat much (the stuff I do manage to get down doesn't even absorb that well) and I often don't make it out of bed. And before anyone asks, my avatar pic was taken last year, one of the "mystery symptoms" of my condition is that people frequently mistake me for someone in their late teens / early 20's, and spending so much time sleeping has earned me about the same amount of life-experience...

This year I've turned the focus onto my Mitochondria, though I'm not sure where to start. I hear that Jack has some protocols but so far I've just begun listening through his podcasts (lots of good info, and it falls right into place with all of the subjects I've been studying over the years!) It's funny to note, I've had a similar motto to Jack's "Who are you good for if you're not good enough for yourself..?" for some time, it went something like "How much good can you do in the world if you don't ever feel good enough??"

Does anyone have some recommended reading for someone starting out here with CFS and chronic inflammation?

### Jack Kruse — 2018-02-28T22:28:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-seattle.20969/post-235980>

Welcome.

Start with podcasts, youtube videos and then my book on Amazon The Epi-paleo RX. Then I think you read the older blogs and jump to patreon blogs and or membership.

### JanSz — 2018-03-01T01:29:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-seattle.20969/post-235991>

Get
[https://www.amazon.com/Diurnal-Cort...860379&sr=8-1-fkmr0&keywords=4x+cortisol+test](https://www.amazon.com/Diurnal-Cortisol-Cx4-Hormone-Imbalance/dp/B0087US47Q/ref=sr_1_fkmr0_1_a_it?ie=UTF8&qid=1519860379&sr=8-1-fkmr0&keywords=4x+cortisol+test)

Most likely your will get bad results.
Work hard (using knowledge found here) to get improved results on your next test in 6-9 months.

..

### caroline — 2018-03-01T07:32:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-seattle.20969/post-236019>

start a journal in the optimal journal section.......

### JanSz — 2018-03-01T13:58:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-seattle.20969/post-236031>

Before you think high tech, mitochondria,
think low tech, wild animal in his own environment.

Wild animal
does not have shelter
does not have clothes
have only sun to provide light, ok, fire
is not able to adjust seasons
eats whats nearby
no fast long distance travels to give jet lag

# Thread: The Brando resort on private Tetiaroa, almost perfect, ruined with WIFI
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-brando-resort-on-private-tetiaroa-almost-perfect-ruined-with-wifi.20970/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael CULLEN — 2018-02-28T15:34:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-brando-resort-on-private-tetiaroa-almost-perfect-ruined-with-wifi.20970/post-235931>

[https://thebrando.com/resort/accommodations/](https://thebrando.com/resort/accommodations/)

I needed to write about this after seeing a documentary on it. Even though the price range of this resort will mean not many, if any on here can visit, I still feel it is imperative we educate them. I have already emailed to share my concerns. I felt compelled to, because I feel they have good intentions but are clueless, like most.

This resort is on the private island of Tetiaroa, surrounded by beautiful Polynesian waters. They are apparently dedicated to preserving the natural eco system there.

Everything is all about being 'eco friendly'. No chemicals used for cleaning. Organic vegetables grown by themselves. Nearly all seafood from surrounding waters, caught fresh. Air conditioning unit using cold sea water.

'The Brando is a unique luxury resort on French Polynesia’s breathtakingly beautiful atoll of Tetiaroa. It sits on one of a dozen small islands (motus) that surround a spectacular three-mile wide lagoon. The motus are all privately owned and available exclusively to the resort’s guests and visiting researchers.
With access to the atoll by a 20-minute private plane flight from Tahiti, the all-inclusive resort features 35 secluded villas with private pools on beautiful white-sand beaches frequented by sea turtles and exotic birds.
This Polynesian style resort offers two restaurants showcasing Polynesian-inspired dishes, East/West fusion and classic French cuisine; a luxurious spa and wellness center with several massage and treatment villas around a serene lily-covered pond; an organic garden where fruits and vegetables for the resort’s restaurants are grown; a lagoon-view bar in the canopy of the palm trees; a beach bar near a large infinity pool; a tennis court; library and cultural center; boutiques; and an environmental research station with guest programs. And while enjoying the villa you will have the satisfaction of knowing that everything that surrounds you has been designed around clean, renewable energy sources.'

'The Brando is unique in concept and scope, combining **environmental purity**, luxury and Polynesian charm into an exceptional experience'.

One giant issue. I am sure they are ignorant about is wifi. They have wifi in all villas and public areas. How can they protect the wildlife and plants with this?

Once again ignorance is blatantly obvious here. They are clueless about the harmful effects of nnEMF. They could do with inviting Jack to speak to them and present some information, which may change their approach. They have a million $ facility for scientific testing of the waters and eco system on the Island, yet have wifi everywhere.

If Resorts on private Islands like this can wake up, we have more hope. First they have to be educated.

### drezy — 2018-02-28T15:51:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-brando-resort-on-private-tetiaroa-almost-perfect-ruined-with-wifi.20970/post-235933>

How else are they supposed to watch Netflix on their mobile devices instead of looking at the beautiful landscape?

# Thread: I'm back, and making progress.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-back-and-making-progress.20971/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### WereBear — 2018-02-28T16:50:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-back-and-making-progress.20971/post-235939>

To be clear, I never stopped "Krusing"

Dr. Kruse's principles were the bedrock of my attempts to climb out of the pit of extreme fatigue, crushing insomnia, auto-immune issues, and my continuing loss of Executive Function that no doctor would even try to understand, except my gem of a GP. After so many tests which came back "normal," he told me these were "stress symptoms" and I had to find the stress and get rid of it.

Turns out, I was born with it. First I thought it was Hellish Menopause, then I figured it was Adrenal Fatigue, and then I discovered Cortisol Resistance, Dr. Kruse, and part of the answer. I improved and then I got stuck.

I think I've got the final part of the puzzle now. I have Asperger's Syndrome.

I'm not unhappy about it  My first reaction to following my hunch to an online test was, "This explains so much." Obviously I am high functioning, but also I couldn't have possibly been diagnosed as a child, because the condition didn't exist then. I was seen as simply "weird and gifted;" as I still am.

However, this was also a nightmare when it comes to medical help; as in, _there is none_. As little understood as autism still is, how it manifests in women is even more obscure. As I like to say now:

> Those bacteria who live in vents on the ocean floor, under tremendous pressure, and existing on geothermal energy? Science knows more about _them_ than they do about women with Asperger's Syndrome.

So even though I have been thrown back on my own devices, I'm better at it than most "experts." I am embarking on a way of "fixing" what I think is a key element: I have been working my brain so hard in the absence of needed hormones (via an idiot doctor who threw me into surgical menopause) I have essentially given myself a Traumatic Brain Injury. From the inside.

In an ideal world, I could rest up and otherwise follow the protocols for people who are recovering from such injuries. Of course, we don't live there. I've got a full time job, a husband with a chronic illness, and a fledgling business that have all been continuously juggled out of sheer necessity.

After trying artificial estrogen and progesterone, tentative experience with steroids from serious crises, and some amino acid supplementation, I have decided that I should let my body manage this crisis. I am taking increasing amounts of **pregnenolone**. All with good results.

The only cautions I have run across regarding this hormone/neurotransmitter precursor is that when people take exogenous hormone supplements at the same time as pregnenolone, this interference with body regulation leads to a imbalance. So I'm not taking any other hormone or neurotransmitter supplements with it.

I'm essentially sending a series of cement trucks to the job site, and let the Supervisor decide to build a wall, or fire stairs, or a chimney, as needed.

This turns out to be the missing element. I was faithfully wearing my amber lensed sunglasses and working natural light and avoiding fluoride and eating as well as I could, like lots of mackerel. (Fortunately, I love it!) Now, things are finally improving again.

There really is light at the end of this tunnel. Sunlight

# Thread: Hello from Michigan (and newbie questions)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-michigan-and-newbie-questions.20973/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Krysten Voss — 2018-03-01T00:00:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-michigan-and-newbie-questions.20973/post-235987>

Greetings from Grand Rapids, Michigan!

I am a new Bronze club member. I was just introduced to Dr. Kruse's material a few days ago when a friend sent me the 2017 Nourish Vermont talk on YouTube.

I would like to start making some immediate changes in my life, including waking up earlier to get out into the a.m. sun, protecting myself from blue light and doing some DNA testing to determine my optimal place to live.

Here are my questions:

\*
Many of the slides in the 2017 Nourish Vermont talk were very blurry and hard to read... I wasn't able to make out the hours on the chart showing the sun's rays at different times of day...
\- What are the times in the morning during which it is best to be nude in the sun and to take in the red light?
\- And at what time of day do we need to start protecting ourselves with blue-blocking glasses?

\*
In the 2017 Nourish Vermont talk Jack mentions using 23andme to do find out one's haplotype, 677, 1298, CDS and VDR in order to find out what area you're optimized to...
\- When he says haplotype is he referring to haplogroup?
\- Is there a specific reason he recommends 23andme over the other DNA testing companies? When I researched the various DNA testing companies, I found that FamilyTreeDNA offers a more comprehensive mtDNA test than that of 23andme.

\*
I have a Sunlighten full spectrum infrared sauna... does this mean that I am taking in Infrared-A when I am using the sauna?

Thank you and I welcome any other tips/advice for newcomers

### Jack Kruse — 2018-03-01T02:13:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-michigan-and-newbie-questions.20973/post-235997>

welcome

1\. sunrise through the first hour
2\. sunset or anytime time in fake light during day
3\. yes
4\. no
5\. check sauna with a spectroscope to be sure

### Krysten Voss — 2018-04-19T03:36:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-michigan-and-newbie-questions.20973/post-239542>

Thank you for the clarification!

Got my DNA results ~ According to my data below, which geographic area would I be optimized to live in?

Haplogroup: H10f
HVR1: 16093C, 16519C
HVR2: 263G, 309.1C, 315.1C
Coding Region: 750G, 1438G, 1842G, 4769G, 8860G, 9632G, 14470A, 15326G

# Thread: Thyroid and CT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-and-ct.20974/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LieselK — 2018-03-01T02:56:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-and-ct.20974/post-236004>

I know I heard or read something about CT turning on the brain to sort of bypass the thyroid in a way, but I don't remember where. Can anyone point me to some more info on thyroid disease and CT? I'm not sure what my "CT goals" are, I just know I'm supposed to be doing it.

### LieselK — 2018-03-01T15:29:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-and-ct.20974/post-236043>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=236014)
>
> CT4 blog

Thanks Jack!

### LieselK — 2018-03-02T18:14:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-and-ct.20974/post-236150>

So my "goal" is to be cold adapted, but I'm not sure to what extent. Is the implication that once adapted I cut back on CT and do maintenance CT periodically to stay cold adapted or because I have thyroid disease it's something that I just do regularly long-term?

### Lahelada — 2018-03-02T19:32:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-and-ct.20974/post-236155>

Just do. In this abnormal world you cannot do enough. Remember that even if you were in the Yucatan you would be doing CT. Once cold adapted you will even be craving it probably.

### LieselK — 2018-03-02T20:53:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-and-ct.20974/post-236161>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=236155)
>
> Just do. In this abnormal world you cannot do enough. Remember that even if you were in the Yucatan you would be doing CT. Once cold adapted you will even be craving it probably.

Face dunks I crave... the submersion not so much yet! I'll look forward to that. Soooo... if one wanted to enjoy a glass of delicious Malbec, and CT and wine don't mix... then how would you plan for such an occasion? Is there a certain number of days to take a break from CT?

### Lahelada — 2018-03-03T01:42:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-and-ct.20974/post-236169>

If one was not still doing the leptin reset one would just have the wine and one would get back on the horse the next day. If one was doing the Leptin reset still then one would not have wine.

### Lahelada — 2018-03-03T01:42:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-and-ct.20974/post-236171>

If one was not still doing the leptin reset one would just have the wine and one would get back on the horse the next day. If one was doing the Leptin reset still then one would not have wine.

### drezy — 2018-03-03T03:46:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-and-ct.20974/post-236183>

> [LieselK said:](/goto/post?id=236161)
>
> Face dunks I crave... the submersion not so much yet! I'll look forward to that. Soooo... if one wanted to enjoy a glass of delicious Malbec, and CT and wine don't mix... then how would you plan for such an occasion? Is there a certain number of days to take a break from CT?

I've tinkered with this. I've crashed it and driven it successfully. Along with Lahelada's points I'd add that if you are doing CT in sunlight and you hydrated well with some cold water ahead of time leading up to the CT then I'm not too worried about you.

You're a parent so none of this will shock you. If you didn't hydrate well enough instead of flowing out your wee wee the protons will flow out your poo poo and be a bit messy. Just up your water and sunlight next experiment.

If you start setting up a minibar (with JD, stoli, and crown royal) as part of your CT tub maybe that's not the best idea. That said as a parent I fully understand why someone might want to set that up. If some saw such a setup they might think "ahh you're an alcoholic." I'd instead first think "Ahh you must have small children and this is your 'me' time..."

### drezy — 2018-03-03T04:03:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-and-ct.20974/post-236184>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=236155)
>
> Once cold adapted you will even be craving it probably.

Yep!

I worked all day outside today clearing trees and scrub brush with a chainsaw. I picked my boy up from school and he helped me move a large portion of it all into a burn pile and separate out good post candidates from the cut scrub.

Once we finished my boy said "It's time for the cold pool." and he was right. My soreness was totally gone after 25 min in 63 F. I used to do this same work when I was 20 and in college to make a little spending money. After the cold today I'm feeling way more like 20 year old me.

### LieselK — 2018-03-03T16:46:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-and-ct.20974/post-236204>

Thank you @[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/) and @[Lahelada](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6531/) for your responses! I'm moving past the Leptin reset now and adjusting my meal portions accordingly. I travel next week alone with all 3 kids and while there won't be any wine for me on the trip I plan to keep up some CT to get me through the Pittsburgh gray and "wintry mix" in the forecast. I have a bottle of Malbec waiting for post trip when I'm settled in at home and needing that "me time"

Does anyone know of a facility that can test for deuterium in water in Australia?

I have found a potentially interesting water available locally, I've asked them and their response is: commercial labs don't have the facilities to test for Deuterium, a research facility might." They are happy for me to test if I find a facility that can though.

Failing that, does anyone have experience with test kits such as this: [https://www.cignaturehealth.com/product/2hwaters-test/](https://www.cignaturehealth.com/product/2hwaters-test/)

Ps. I'm new here! If this has been covered, please point me to it!

### JanSz — 2018-03-01T18:06:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-testing-in-australia.20975/post-236066>

> [Trev Douglas said:](/goto/post?id=236021)

> Does anyone know of a facility that can test for deuterium in water in Australia?
>
> I have found a potentially interesting water available locally, I've asked them and their response is: commercial labs don't have the facilities to test for Deuterium, a research facility might." They are happy for me to test if I find a facility that can though.
>
> Failing that, does anyone have experience with test kits such as this: [https://www.cignaturehealth.com/product/2hwaters-test/](https://www.cignaturehealth.com/product/2hwaters-test/)
>
> Ps. I'm new here! If this has been covered, please point me to it!

I have used that test.
Coconut water 156ppm
Costco RO water 149.4ppm

Also CignatureHealth volunteered information on numerous (famous) waters, bunching all them together into (from memory) (145 -155) ppm category (ie. even 145ppm is meaningless).

Keep in mind that the first goal (there is more) is to have a body's general ppm near 130ppm.
Note that on their conferences Preventa-125ppm is dispensed (to everybody who is thirsty.
Note that Australia (and their waters) are specially bad place, (it is used by @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) as indicator (of bad things to come), representative of canary in a coal mine.

I would say that the (meaningful) conversations about DDW starts at 125ppm,
and then go lower (for currently) healthy people.

Older people (I am 77yo (middle age I hope), and specially when currently sick,
start at 105ppm or lately even at 85ppm
and then go down from that.

////
That is a long way to say that testing local water for D, tap water, spring water, glacial water, alpine water, Icelandic water, cenote water, etc
does not make sense,
but it is a nice pass time, and good way to spend vacation in those places.

......

Q&A is already available.
Gold and up should have gotten e-mail already, I did.
How to buy Q&A via Parteon I am not sure.
I think you have to be a Parteon member, and then you will get e-mail when Q&A are available.

//
San Pelegrino in USA as far as D is concerned have no advantage over tap water.
VOSS, Icelandic have some small (insignificant) advantage.

The thing that I am concerned about is that,
everybody (who is currently healthy),
Australia and everybody else,
really should be drinking 125ppm water per my estimate.
So drinking Voss or Icelanic does not cut it but they are very expensive (when considering their Deuterium level as a metric).
I am not talking about occasional vacations to cenote or Iceland or similar places.
I am talking daily living.

Expensive comparing to Qlarivia-25ppm.
So, until someone builds DDW plant locally to everybody,
Qlarivia-25 is the one to beat.
Actually more expensive Preventa-25 may also be cheaper then Voss or Icelandic or some glaciar water, when counting deuterium.

////
I drink Preventa-25ppm
I mix one part of Preventa-25 with two parts of plain water.
assuming my water have 150ppm, I drink
(25+2\*150)/3=108.3 ppm

if one would mix three parts of plain water with one part of Preventa-25 or Qlarivia-25
he/she would get
(25+3\*150)/4=118.75 ppm
which is about what everybody (who is healthy) should drink (in my estimation).

........
What can save money & and make everybody more healthy (and spending less $$ on DDW)
**is eating fat.**
Fat have advantages.
1- it is (significantly) lover in deuterium.
2-eating it affects body number of ways, reduces deuterium, **reduces amount of consumed water**, produces matrix water 1.1 pound per pound of consumed fat. Less deuterium increases energy produced by mitochondria.

///

# Thread: 5G: Non-Linear (Exponential) Increase?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-non-linear-exponential-increase.20976/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AdamF — 2018-03-01T19:06:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-non-linear-exponential-increase.20976/post-236073>

Jack,

This article made me realize that every large teleco. provider is going to compete fiercely to deploy their own infrastructure, meaning there may be _multiple_ 5G infrastructure builds in certains places where these companies these companies do not co-locate.

[https://9to5mac.com/2018/02/27/t-mobile-5g-plans/](https://9to5mac.com/2018/02/27/t-mobile-5g-plans/)

Thank you again Trae. I appreciate your time. I live in a villa style home with my neighbors’ bedroom on the other side of an adjoining wall with their electricals in that wall. My bed is on the opposite wall that has no electrical wires through it. I assume that a curcuit breaker for my room will not be sufficient given the neighbours. Any thoughts on how I can overcome this? I do not have the money to move and would love to use the canopy. What devices do you use to test inside the canopy? Again many thanks.

### Trae — 2018-09-12T03:55:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-vs-emf-canopy.20977/post-250797>

You can use a digital multi-meter which has two prongs on it. One prong connects to a grounding cable to use as the base signal, then the multi-meter will show you the **difference** between whatever is touching the other prong. So if the ground outside has an electrical charge of let's say, 0 volts (it doesn't but for simplicity sake), and your body has a charge of 5 volts, the multimeter will show a charge of 5 volts. Now you can touch that same prong to your canopy and watch what happens to the multi-meter, it will probably jump up to 10, 15, or 20 volts depending on your setup, and that displays the volts that are going through your canopy. If you are upstairs it will be very high.

People incorrectly think when they ground their canopy that it now has a zero voltage, when actually _it shows a zero voltage because the canopy is connected to the ground as well so the difference in charge between the ground and the canopy of course is zero_. What's actually happening is there is a constant charge of EMF running through the canopy and then into the ground over and over and over and the multi-meter isn't fast enough to pick it up, but you better believe your body is picking it up. This is probably why you feel like garbage when sleeping in it. Headaches maybe, or just a feeling of over stimulation, maybe your eyes even hurt.

That being said, if you want an instrument that is fast enough to pick up those charges and really see what's happening, you need an oscilloscope which can be expensive. But for what you're doing here a multimeter should work and they are like 20 bucks.

### Sun Disciple — 2018-09-12T16:21:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-vs-emf-canopy.20977/post-250821>

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK26894/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK26894/)

If we generally have 40+ trillion cells in the human body...
And we have hundreds to thousands of mitochondria per cell...
And each mitochondrion has an average # of <insert ATPases>?

Anyone want to guess how many ATP Synthases we have per mitochondrion?

Then, let's calculate how many ATPases there are in an average human body.

Each ATPase rotor spins around 1,500 times per second.

### Sean Waters — 2018-03-01T23:10:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/atpases-per-human-body.20978/post-236097>

I do not know, but this made me research ATPases and I have found there are many different types of this enzyme or nano machine...

V types (vacuole membrane), F types (inner mitochondrial),

There are also P types - some of which produce Gastric acid by pumping H+ into the stomach acid. For anyone with "low stomach acid" taking Betaine HCL etc. Look no further than the Deuterium / Light story...

If you cannot run these motors fast with pure H+ via Exclusion Zone water from Sunlight or Cold, then you will have a slower deuterium filled pump mechanism, meaning less H+ (Low stomach acid) or slower build up of acid = reflux or indigestion. This is one Food Guru problem that has never been understood or solved. Common in northern latitudes... I wonder why...

It makes sense to me personally, because if I ate breakfast in India and felt full or bloated, if I went outside and allowed the morning light to hit my eyes I would notice my mouth begin to salivate and my gastric juices flow. I would actually then begin to digest my food and feel better. I thought it was lights action on my hormones via the eye/pituitary, therefore activating my gastric cells acid production... but it is actually Light hitting the T.I's of the Gastric cells and changing the water to EZ to run those ATPases faster and pump more H+ and exclude Deuterium. This is after Light hits our eyes Blood cells and is stored via the charge and light information stored in the hemoglobin and water and that is carried to our stomach/gut cells in rapid time...

It may even be less physical than we think, hard to explain, but maybe the water/blood hit by light in the eye doesn't have to actually make its way to the gut but maybe light is passed along through the network of water in blood to reach our whole body. Really trying to put my quantum goggles on for this kinda thing because I don't believe in the biological perception anymore. I know that from Mae Wan Ho's book, you have cascade reactions which can mean Light hitting a rod cell causes unbelievably fast transmission to our brain. But you also have Quantum mechanics in Light in Shaping Life, which shows how electrons move through plants during photosynthesis, and how they can navigate and disappear across many steps and be in 2 places at once... This is where it gets blurry for me, but I can imagine a connection between Eye and Gastric ATPase pumping H+ faster than waiting for blood to be carried round.

Sorry for the ramble Scompy! Totally unrelated, but felt like I needed to put this here!

1500 times per second, Is that right?

Below paper suggests

**_The rotation rates at various ATP concentrations obeyed the curve defined by a K m of ≈30 μM and a V max of ≈350 revolutions per second (21 000 revolutions per minute) at 37°C._**

[https://www.pnas.org/doi/10.1073/pnas.0407857102](https://www.pnas.org/doi/10.1073/pnas.0407857102)

### John Schumacher — 2023-02-10T19:51:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/atpases-per-human-body.20978/post-319540>

> 1500 times per second, Is that right?
>
> Below paper suggests
>
> **_The rotation rates at various ATP concentrations obeyed the curve defined by a K m of ≈30 μM and a V max of ≈350 revolutions per second (21 000 revolutions per minute) at 37°C._**
>
> [https://www.pnas.org/doi/10.1073/pnas.0407857102](https://www.pnas.org/doi/10.1073/pnas.0407857102)

The idea that the membrane-embedded Fo (a component of F-type ATP synthase (FoF1)) is a theoretical model which believes a proton moves through a Fo_c_\-subunit ring (_c_\-ring).

Statements like: Based on the signal intensity and structure of the tFo_c_\-ring and tFoF1, six pairs of _c_N23 and _c_E56 surrounded by membrane lipids take the closed form, whereas the other four in the _a_–_c_ interface employ the deprotonated open form at a proportion of 87%. _<- These statements are mathematical calculations which they believe express and validates their assertions._ Specifically in rotation analyses

Here's an assertion: "In a rotary motor FoF1-ATP synthase that couples H transport with ATP synthesishydrolysis, it is _thought_ that an Foc subunit oligomer ring (c-ring) in the membrane rotates as protons pass through Fo and a 120° rotation produces one ATP at F1."

I’m looking for experimental evidence not theoretical modeling. Below is additional mathematical thought exercises on this subject.

[https://pubs.acs.org/doi/10.1021/jacs.2c03580](https://pubs.acs.org/doi/10.1021/jacs.2c03580)

### Trev Douglas — 2018-03-02T02:34:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here-from-melbourne-aus.20980/post-236116>

Thought I should introduce myself...

I'm Trev, from Mordialloc, a bayside suburb of Melbourne, Australia.

Been taking in as much as I can from Jack via FB, Insta, here and some podcasts etc the last couple of months.

I must say I'm blown away! This has cracked open a whole new thing for me...

As a former Arborist, hearing Jack talk about light and Mitochondria using Plants and Photosynthesis as an analogy helped make it quickly make sense to me that there is something in this...now hooked on learning more!

I'm now getting up for sunrise, watching sunsets, drinking better water, dodging blue light, conscious of nnEMF exposure, and have added Seafood and some meat after almost 5 years vegetarian.

I have been using blue blocking glasses for a few years, but more because it helped my eyes on being on computers so much. I have a new understanding of them now.

I did have a recent health episode (pneumonia) that was a wake up to get serious about all this.

Thanks!

# Thread: Grounding and Body Voltage (in the Sun and Indoors)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-and-body-voltage-in-the-sun-and-indoors.20981/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Greenie — 2018-03-02T08:36:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-and-body-voltage-in-the-sun-and-indoors.20981/post-236129>

I've been testing myself with a body voltage meter indoors and outdoors. With the electricity on, the reading with rubber-soled shoes reads double than with bare feet (.48 vs .24). [Click here for photos of the readings.](http://www.evernote.com/l/AQUVWPz8dyBMXp6-tQawCq6O9XvvLSErqfQ/)

Surprisingly, damp concrete outside had an inverse reading with bare feet reading nearly three times than when wearing the same shoes (.26 vs .09). These results were approximately the same regardless of where I stood—even when on unpaved sections.

In light of these findings, does standing outside with rubber-soled shoes a better idea than going barefoot, or does the sun negate the increased voltages? Can one still pick up electrons from the ground even when separated by an insulated barrier (e.g., tunneling)?

### Jan Christer — 2018-03-03T02:28:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-and-body-voltage-in-the-sun-and-indoors.20981/post-236175>

In the pictures, I assume you connect the red lead to your body (or holding it in your hand).

Where do you connect the black lead?

### Greenie — 2018-03-03T05:44:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-and-body-voltage-in-the-sun-and-indoors.20981/post-236186>

> [Jan Christer said:](/goto/post?id=236175)
>
> In the pictures, I assume you connect the red lead to your body (or holding it in your hand).
>
> Where do you connect the black lead?

The black lead goes to ground. I used both a ground rod and the ground from an electrical outlet with the power off. The readings were different; however, the relative differences were identical.

### Jan Christer — 2018-03-03T06:24:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-and-body-voltage-in-the-sun-and-indoors.20981/post-236189>

> [Greenie said:](/goto/post?id=236186)
>
> The black lead goes to ground. I used both a ground rod and the ground from an electrical outlet with the power off. The readings were different; however, the relative differences were identical.

Ok, the indoors readings are explainable by the floor being partially grounded so you reduce your potential by standing barefoot on it compared to using shoes.

Another possibility, if you use "ground" in house wiring for the black lead indoors, then the floor may be a better ground, and you may be almost completely grounded barefoot to the floor, and the reading comes from the potential of the wired "ground".

For the outdoors pictures I assume that you stand in the same position with the body and in the same location being barefoot vs. using shoes.

I would recommend that you only use a ground rod at least for outside readings, since the "ground" in house wiring can carry voltage at least as large as your largest readings.

And in that case you could get 'inverted' voltage readings, since your bare feet on concrete could be closer to a real ground than the "ground" in the house wiring.

The location of your ground rod is also important, the farther away from buildings the better, and the potential of the ground also depends on closeness to underground cables.

If you want to determine the cleanest location for an outside rod, you have to test voltage between rods in several locations until you get close to zero voltage between them consistently, and to a rod far away (maybe 100-200 feet) from buildings.

If still you get the surprising outdoor readings to a clean rod, like you described, and with your body far away from electrical cables, then we have something more interesting to look into!

There is of course a superficial explanation to your outdoor readings: There is a potential difference between the point you stand on, and your "ground" on the black lead, which you pick up being barefoot. With shoes on you don't pick up most of this potential difference, and therefore get a lower reading. This is assuming that the upper part of you body is not largely affected by nearby cables.

### Greenie — 2018-03-03T09:01:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-and-body-voltage-in-the-sun-and-indoors.20981/post-236192>

> [Jan Christer said:](/goto/post?id=236189)
>
> Ok, the indoors readings are explainable by the floor being partially grounded so you reduce your potential by standing barefoot on it compared to using shoes.
>
> For the outdoors pictures I assume that you stand in the same position with the body and in the same location being barefoot vs. using shoes.

Yes, readings were taken from essentially the same position.

I used a grounding rod, which measured lower readings than when using the in-house ground. For example, a reading of .26 versus .13. (Interesting that the actual reading differed so much.) But, as I mentioned, no deviation in relative terms was created between the grounding type. (The rod was far away with no other buildings nearby.)

Perhaps the higher reading outside of barefoot in comparison to rubber-soled shoes is attributed to something else. I know there are at least some electrical wires running under the concrete. What I wonder is if using shoes outside might be more effective than going barefoot.

I'll try seeing if height affects the reading. Will explore that tomorrow. Thank you for the feedback!

### Jan Christer — 2018-03-03T16:47:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-and-body-voltage-in-the-sun-and-indoors.20981/post-236205>

> [Greenie said:](/goto/post?id=236192)
>
> Yes, readings were taken from essentially the same position.
>
> I used a grounding rod, which measured lower readings than when using the in-house ground. For example, a reading of .26 versus .13. (Interesting that the actual reading differed so much.) But, as I mentioned, no deviation in relative terms was created between the grounding type. (The rod was far away with no other buildings nearby.)
>
> Perhaps the higher reading outside of barefoot in comparison to rubber-soled shoes is attributed to something else. I know there are at least some electrical wires running under the concrete. What I wonder is if using shoes outside might be more effective than going barefoot.
>
> I'll try seeing if height affects the reading. Will explore that tomorrow. Thank you for the feedback!

You are welcome!

It would be interesting to see what readings you get outside on ground in several different locations, a little farther away from the house, barefoot vs. with shoes.

But even if you get a lower reading with shoes than barefoot, you are not grounded with shoes on and don't pick up electrons from the earth.

### Greenie — 2018-03-03T23:15:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-and-body-voltage-in-the-sun-and-indoors.20981/post-236219>

> [Jan Christer said:](/goto/post?id=236205)
>
> You are welcome!
> But even if you get a lower reading with shoes than barefoot, you are not grounded with shoes on and don't pick up electrons from the earth.

That's what I figured. With this in mind: is it better to be grounded in the sun with a higher body voltage than an insulated barrier and a lower voltage?

### Jan Christer — 2018-03-04T00:52:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-and-body-voltage-in-the-sun-and-indoors.20981/post-236226>

> [Greenie said:](/goto/post?id=236219)
>
> That's what I figured. With this in mind: is it better to be grounded in the sun with a higher body voltage than an insulated barrier and a lower voltage?

If you are not in the presence of electrical fields with your body, and stand on clean ground, not close to buildings, underground cables, or other sources contaminating the ground, then I recommend grounding with bare skin as very beneficial, to get electrons from the earth.

With insulated shoes on, you don't ground your feet, and lose this benefit, unless you touch the ground with other parts of the body.

And in the absence of external electrical fields, I think body voltage readings lose their significance in the context we have discussed, and rather involve other factors instead, which I don't know about. The reason we do body voltage readings as discussed here is to measure how our body picks up external electrical fields.

### Greenie — 2018-03-04T02:33:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-and-body-voltage-in-the-sun-and-indoors.20981/post-236233>

> [Jan Christer said:](/goto/post?id=236226)
>
> And in the absence of external electrical fields, I think body voltage readings lose their significance in the context we have discussed, and rather involve other factors instead, which I don't know about. The reason we do body voltage readings as discussed here is to measure how our body picks up external electrical fields.

Ought there be a direct correlation between a body voltage reading and an electrical field reading (e.g., from a milligauss meter) in that a higher electrical field=higher body voltage? Or is this not necessarily the case always?

### Jan Christer — 2018-03-04T02:44:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-and-body-voltage-in-the-sun-and-indoors.20981/post-236234>

> [Greenie said:](/goto/post?id=236233)
>
> Ought there be a direct correlation between a body voltage reading and an electrical field reading (e.g., from a milligauss meter) in that a higher electrical field=higher body voltage? Or is this not necessarily the case always?

There is definitely a correlation, since AC devices emit both AC fields, which we pick up as body voltage, and EMF's as measured in milliGauss. And most of the time, if one is higher, the other is probably higher as well. But I don't think the correlation is linear.

### Greenie — 2018-03-05T00:42:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-and-body-voltage-in-the-sun-and-indoors.20981/post-236292>

> [Jan Christer said:](/goto/post?id=236234)
>
> There is definitely a correlation, since AC devices emit both AC fields, which we pick up as body voltage, and EMF's as measured in milliGauss. And most of the time, if one is higher, the other is probably higher as well. But I don't think the correlation is linear.

Yeah, it doesn't appear to be at all linear, at least based on the comparisons I have made with the two meters. But then again, my understanding is biological systems are not linear based.

One question that arose in my mind: if one grounds via a grounding pad or similar device while wearing rubber-soled shoes, how are electrons able to pass through that person? Do they pass through the feet (if they are standing) or through another route(s)?

### Jan Christer — 2018-03-05T00:59:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-and-body-voltage-in-the-sun-and-indoors.20981/post-236293>

> [Greenie said:](/goto/post?id=236292)
>
> Yeah, it doesn't appear to be at all linear, at least based on the comparisons I have made with the two meters. But then again, my understanding is biological systems are not linear based.
>
> One question that arose in my mind: if one grounds via a grounding pad or similar device while wearing rubber-soled shoes, how are electrons able to pass through that person? Do they pass through the feet (if they are standing) or through another route(s)?

You collect electrons from the earth with whatever part of your body touches the ground.
In your example you collect electrons via the grounding pad, if that's connected to the earth, independently of what you wear on your feet.
But you collect electrons more efficiently the larger area of your body that is grounded.

### Greenie — 2018-03-05T01:18:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-and-body-voltage-in-the-sun-and-indoors.20981/post-236294>

> [Jan Christer said:](/goto/post?id=236293)
>
> You collect electrons from the earth with whatever part of your body touches the ground.
> In your example you collect electrons via the grounding pad, if that's connected to the earth, independently of what you wear on your feet.
> But you collect electrons more efficiently the larger area of your body that is grounded.

So the rubber-soled shoes do not obviate or retard the passage of electrons through the feet? Or does the rubber act as a resistor of sorts even when in contact with a properly grounded pad? Thank you for your patience in fielding all of these questions.

### shiran — 2018-03-02T16:53:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electric-distillation.20982/post-236143>

Anyone has experience with electric distillation? Recommend product?

### JanSz — 2018-03-02T18:41:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electric-distillation.20982/post-236151>

Excellent topic.
Lets see first what are we against when thinking of using distillation process to produce DDW.
Note;
I have no clue about it, this are just my doodles,
(constructive) criticism is welcomed.

First, we already know that it can be done.
We also know that it is energy hog, so lots of expenses come from need for energy after cost of equipment, its installation, instrumentation and time spend for maintenance is accounted for.

When thinking of ethanol/water mixture we have it easy.
Water boils at 212°F 100°C
Ethanol boils at 173.1°F= 78.37°C
So we are dealing with (large) temperature difference, 38.9°F 21.63°C
\--
Heavy water boils at 214.5°F 101.4°C
temperature difference 2.5°F 1.4°C
that is a different universe.

From what dr Boros told us from one step on (highly accurately made with very super dupper instrumentation) we can expect (on average) 4% difference when installation works under atmospheric pressure or 8% when works under vacuum.
So forget vacuum
forget 4% average
forget highly accurate instrumentation
assume 3% average (if that)
and see where that puts us.

We really do not want any lesser DDW than
125ppm for healthy people
actually we commonly need
105ppm
85ppm
and we have family and friends, each drinks 2.5 or 3 liters a day
so say 10 to 15 liters daily.

Next assumption is that we will get RO installation and use that RO water as initial input.
Also assume water 150ppm, but some Aussies will have 160ppm to deal with.
Ok, change that to 155ppm for initial RO water.
one step gets 3% improvement,
so 155ppm becomes 155\*0.97=150.35ppm
next step 150.35\*0.97=145.8395ppm
and so on
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

This baby have 6 step column, is really made for the delta T one see in water/ethanol situation (38.9°F 21.63°C)
but **we want to use it in (2.5°F 1.4°C )**
**Jut sneezing** on one of those step column may **interfere in temperature balance** for an hour.

We need two of those in the row to get below 120ppm
We need tree in the row to get what I am drinking
We need four in the row for the next step.

I am not sure what that installation is able to produce while I would constantly watched it for 8 hrs.

I do not mean sound hopeless.
Somebody please improve situation,
come with improvements.

[https://brewhaus.com/complete-flute-moonshine-still-6-section/](https://brewhaus.com/complete-flute-moonshine-still-6-section/)

I am not sure yet, but above set up will cost more.
Off hand I see that it needs electric heaters and their controllers.
Plus likely something additional.

Also not sure how the above will work near the end when only little bit of fluid is left.
How it will heat that fluid.
We also need to monitor and stop evaporation when designated evaporation volume is reached.

During the process, maximum allowed heating is such that the bottom cell still have water/vapor interface.

This is quick, there likely will be many more thoughts before this installation will produce any (quality) DDW (if at all).

@[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/)
and anybody else willing to **add something constructive** (or nothing).

//

### JanSz — 2018-03-03T16:43:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electric-distillation.20982/post-236203>

> [shiran said:](/goto/post?id=236200)
>
> Im certainly trying along with people I know who understand this field and trying to find out what is the story off making DDW and how to produce it at home.
> I need a precise definition of what should be the end goal, what the target is and what the target and how much DDW should we get in the end.
> My husband has special machines in his factory that distill water in special ways. We are now checking all the directions that came from the last webinars with Jack
> I would highly appreciate any input and information on the process that Jack talks about
> thanks

@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) talks about many important topics.
DDW is "just" one of the latest.
With all other changes present and coming, DDW's importance quickly becomes #2.
Right after the sunlight, ie. observing natural day/night (Circadian Rhythm).

As far as DDW, the initial goal is to have (average body) represented by breath condensate, value of 130ppm (D/H) or somewhat less. And (more accurate I gues or complementary) is to have ATP-RPM in green area on the test. Also urine sample should have it's results in the green.
That is by BREATHY TM TEST and 2HEALTH TM TEST from CignatureHealth.com

To achieve that one should (exclusively) drink DDW and eat fat as much as he can.

Everybody that is healthy, should drink at least DDW-125ppm (D/H)
Everybody that is healthy and old, should drink DDW-105ppm (D/H)
possibly even DDW-85ppm (D/W) (do tests and find what you need)

DDW main benefit is that it frees mitochondria so it able again to produce energy at its original capacity.
That (with time, many months, over one year), allows to reverse (or some say reverse) many cancers and other, diabetes etc.

**You say:
My husband has special machines in his factory that distill water in special ways.**

So he may be already making some DDW and just not tested it.
For starters, it would be worth to double check that.

Hopefully your husband could review what I posted above and give us constructive comments.

Since he is selling what he produces, desired manufacturing capacity depends on what he is able to sell.

I would guess that the larger setup could be what he could start with.
Except that he may want to at least double (to 12 steps) or quadruple (24 steps) column.

Boiling water causes vibrations, (more heat more vibrations), those vibrations may/(will) interrupt column working, he may want to have column mounted separately (if he would decide on increasing column's stages.)

Only testing output water can tell us if all this will work.
I think it will work, just not sure of extend of it.

Heating water causes it evaporation.
One of the limitations is maximum heat delivered.
You want to see activity on at least the first column step.
You want to see water/steam there.
If you would heat too much only steam may go thru the whole column, and that would defeat the whole purpose.

Good amount of heat that creates steam but barely, will decrease overall output.

The big installation (that I posted above) mentions 33000W=33kW heating system. (It takes 90 minutes to heat it up to operating temperature.)

The small installation mentions up to three 1500W heaters, so 4.5Kw, but supposedly would work on just one heater. Additionally band heaters can be added to the kettle (the vessel on the bottom), then the 15 gallon kettle can be evaporated within about 4 hours.

One reason that we do not want to overheat water is that there is only temperature difference 2.5°F = 1.4°C between light and heavy water boiling points.

Water/ethanol distillation do not have that problem, there is temperature difference of , 38.9°F = 21.63°C

Certain amount of kettle's original content must be left un-evaporated.
Two reasons:
It is highly enriched in deuterium.
You do not want burn heaters when they are not immersed in the water.

\=====================

There is likely many more that your husband could tell us here.
He could also point out to any of mine errors or misconceptions.

\======================
I think that smaller machine (cost in vicinity of $2000) could be used to see if there is a merit in all this.

But, we always have to remember that dr Somlyai is already doing it for number of years.

/////
.

### JanSz — 2018-03-03T19:05:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electric-distillation.20982/post-236215>

When boiling water, steam evaporates (after required latent heat is added).
That is wet steam, may still have some droplets of water in it (lots of heavy water content there in those droplets).
If one would keep heating it, that (originally wet steam) will turn into dry steam.
Dry steam have temperature that is higher than boiling water or wet steam have.
When in say, power applications, driving turbines, wet steam must be further heated to become dry.
We do not want any water droplets in wet steam to erode or break turbine blades.
In discussed DDW application we want to deal only with wet steam.
Plus, we have a steam from light and heavy water.
We want to minimize heavy water steam, but the higher the steam temperature the more heavy steam it will contain.
Light water wet steam have temperature 212°F = 100°C
heavy water wet steam have temperature 214.5°F = 101.4°C
we really really want to have our wet steam on top of the distillation column to newer be higher than 100°C, any temperature over 100 °C will draw deuterium in where we do not want.
**This is very tough requirement.
This requirement will be the critical requirement when heating water in the kettle.**
Unfortunately there is very small margin there, just 2.5°F = 1.4°C, and the smaller part of that little margin we actually use the better will be D from H separation.

**I am assuming,
that when the first stage of distillation column have proper water level, that should satisfy those requirements (without need of actual temperature measuring)(which always would be not accurate enough).
So installation must have a way of observing water level on the first column stage.
Preferably that first stage should be build differently that all other, and should be able to accommodate water level measurement instrument that would be hooked up to heaters power supply and adjust power going to heaters.**
**I do not think any of the two installations have that provision, but it is not hard to implement.**
**When implemented, the installation could be on max possible producing capacity (most of the time). And at max possible quality of the product. (I think).**

///////////////////////

### JanSz — 2018-03-03T22:17:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electric-distillation.20982/post-236218>

Kettle, the vessel containing water that will be processed, have electrical heaters.
Those heaters to work must be immersed in water.
There must be instrument measuring water level.
When it gets below certain point it should tell the switch to shut down power.
The cycle is over.
We will wait until installation cools down.

The smaller (15 gallons kettle) installation will likely have three 1500W heaters.
I assume that two of those heaters could be working all the time (until they are shut down by end of cycle sensor). The third heater will likely be toggled according to sensor on first column stage. Third heater will be shut down permanently on end of cycle with the other two.

### JanSz — 2018-03-04T00:18:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electric-distillation.20982/post-236221>

Approximate Kettle Dimensions:
14"d x 28"h
Walls of kettle are approximately 1.2mm thick with heavier bottom
304L grade stainless steel
15 gallon

Assume that the power shut off to heaters will happen when water level is 3" from the bottom.
\=3.14\*14^2/4\*3=461.58 in^3
1 cubic inch=0.004329 gallons
1.99817982=2 gallons
\---------------------------------------------------------
Mean abundance in ocean water (from VSMOW) 155.76 ± 0.1 ppm (a ratio of 1 part per approximately 6420 parts), that is, about 0.015% of the atoms in a sample (by number, not weight)

So in 15 gallons of water the volume of heavy water is
15\*0.015/100=0.00225 gallons
We can disregard that volume.

...

Very interesting info JanSz. I don't know much, if anything about distillers and will go back and read more on how they work.

A thought that comes to mind though is that the key may be in running the still below water boiling point because the temperature of a body of water is an average of the temperatures of the water molecules it contains and there will be many water molecules at a lower temperature, even while those that reach boiling point are close to evaporating.

If the operating temp can be kept around say 85- 90 C, will it still work? At that lower temp, some H2O molecules will be reaching 100C and evaporating but I'm guessing that fewer D2O molecules will be reaching 101.4C than if the still runs at a full boil. Evaporation rate would be slower but perhaps a lesser proportion of D2O would be in the steam?

To understand better problems we are facing,
lets study azeotropic solutions and phase equilibrium.
First ethanol/water (which is super simple (to a point)
then H2O/D2O and possibly H2O/D2O/HDO
charts and data tables help in better understanding our problem
Anybody wishing to help, please do.
Also keeping in mind that (for this project) we are working under (mostly) constant atmospheric pressure.

**Materials.**
Kettle and distillation columns are usually made of stainless steel consumption grade.
Some parts can be made of copper.
When only stainless steel is used, copper shavings are sometime inserted. I am not clear about reasons for that. Likely that apply to alcohol and not to DDW, I hope.
Say,
Stainless steel and Chlorine are not friends. But that may be conditional. Do we have this conditions in our project? Water going to RO membrane was treated with salt (NaCl), after RO membrane do we have any salt left in RO water?? Does it matter?

Water that I would be using is a town water that goes first thru water softener that uses salt. Would like to keep it that way for simplicity.
RO water, unless treated does not have neutral Ph, it is base (at least the one I tasted in my house, years ago.
I would rather not post or pre process water.
Thinking of just plain vanilla RO membrane from Amazon or Walmart.
Something like this (minus all filters and storage vessel).
Is that ok??
On RO water container add sensor that tells electric valve (installed on incoming water) when container is full.
Note, the RO system that I used to have, discarded 3 or 4 gallons for one gallon produced RO water. (Does that matter?)(say in California or Israel).

So far the highest water usage will be the water cooling condenser.
I think, hope, guess, garden hose half open for 24 hr
to run 15 gallon kettle 6 times over and over (to produce 13 gallons of usable DDW) good for 20 days for one person. ???
Wonder how much water actually will be used.
That is, should I make arrangements to use that water again, say for lawn watering?
In Japan, in Tokyo this may be serious question.

### JanSz — 2018-03-08T20:11:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electric-distillation.20982/post-236705>

On 3/8/2018 1:06 PM, Emmet at Clawhammer wrote:
\> Hey Jan,
\>
\> It's Emmet Again. Here's email number 2 of 5 in the Distillation Mini-Course.
\>
\> Here's another question we hear a lot:
\>
\> **Should I get a copper still or a stainless steel still?**
\> In short, it all comes down to personal preference, but there are still a few considerations to make before deciding. Here are a few:
\>
\> The Main Differences Between Copper and Stainless Steel
\> Copper has been used to make stills for centuries, mainly because it's a great conductor of heat, it will naturally remove sulfur compounds, and it is an easy metal to work with. The main drawbacks of copper are the higher material cost and the fact that they are more difficult to keep clean than stainless steel stills.
\>
\> **Stainless steel is less expensive than copper, but it is still very durable, and also easy to clean. The main drawbacks of stainless steel are that it doesn't conduct heat as well as copper, and stainless steel won't naturally remove sulfur compounds from the wash.**
\>
\> Reasons to Buy a Pre-Built Still
\>
\> Our stainless steel still is your best option if you’re less interested in building your still and just want to get to distilling.
\> A stainless steel still can be easily adapted to use an electric heating element, and the column can be filled with copper packing which does the job of removing sulfur compounds that normally wouldn’t be removed by using a stainless steel still.
\> We carry a pre-built stainless steel boiler with a copper column still, which gives the benefits of both stainless and copper.
\> If you're in the market for a still and this sounds like what you're interested in, check out our 8 gallon, pre-built, stainless steel distiller.
\>
\> Reasons to Build Your Own
\>
\> If you are a purist and want the "old timey" look, a copper still is the best choice for you.
\> It is less expensive to build your own still over buying one that is pre-built.
\> Traditionally, the hardest part about building your own still from scratch is fabricating the parts, so that’s where we come in. Our DIY copper moonshine still kits don't require copper fabrication - all of the the parts are pre-cut and ready for assembly.
\> If this is more your style, check out our collection of copper stills.
\>
\> That’s it for now!
\>
\> Still can’t decide which material is best for you? Check out this article where we go into more detail about the differences between stainless steel and copper.
\>
\> Other questions or comments? Feel free to reply to this email and I’ll get back to you!
\>
\> Emmet Leahy
\> Clawhammer Supply
\> Copyright © 2018 Clawhammersupply, All rights reserved.
\> Heads up: you're on this list because you signed up for the free eBook.
\>
\> Want to change how you receive these emails?
\> You can update your preferences or unsubscribe from this list

### Tanya — 2018-03-12T00:01:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electric-distillation.20982/post-237012>

This is Google translated:

There are a number of methods and technologies for obtaining potable light water, but many of them are too expensive to produce, or do not provide a sufficient level of isotope separation (for example, the known distillation method in distillation columns based on the boiling point difference between heavy and light water).

water from the resulting mixture of oxygen and hydrogen, depleted deuterium. In order to optimize the process and increase the separation factor, the electrodes are made of nickel.

The developed installation, according to the authors, allows producing a product - light water with any given degree of depletion, down to a very low deuterium content - 10 ppm. At the same time, the cost of the product is three times lower than in traditional technologies.

At present, light water production plants with a capacity of 0.3 l / h, 1 l / h, as well as two lines with a capacity of 3 l / h have been manufactured. The plants are certified. A patent was obtained on the line for obtaining drinking water with a modified isotope composition.

An interesting development in this field was presented at the Moscow exhibition of NTTM-2013 by young scientists and engineers from Krasnodar. Here, a method has been developed for the electrolytic production of water depleted of heavy isotopes of hydrogen, which makes it possible to reduce energy consumption by three times in comparison with the rectification columns.

The separation takes place according to the following scheme. Electrolysis of water is carried out, in which the molecules containing protium are mainly decomposed, because of the lower energy of the covalent bond. At this stage, the main condition is to maintain a minimum voltage, at which only the electrolysis process is started that is sufficient to separate hydrogen, but not sufficient to separate deuterium. Next is the synthesis of water from the resulting mixture of oxygen and hydrogen, depleted deuterium. In order to optimize the process and increase the separation factor, the electrodes are made of nickel.

The developed installation, according to the authors, allows producing a product - light water with any given degree of depletion, down to a very low deuterium content - 10 ppm. At the same time, the cost of the product is three times lower than in traditional technologies.

### JanSz — 2018-03-12T17:45:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electric-distillation.20982/post-237054>

\[qu ote="Tanya, post: 237012, member: 6641"\]This is Google translated:

There are a number of methods and technologies for obtaining potable light water, but many of them are too expensive to produce, or do not provide a sufficient level of isotope separation (for example, the known distillation method in distillation columns based on the boiling point difference between heavy and light water).

water from the resulting mixture of oxygen and hydrogen, depleted deuterium. In order to optimize the process and increase the separation factor, the electrodes are made of nickel.

The developed installation, according to the authors, allows producing a product - light water with any given degree of depletion, **down to a very low deuterium content - 10 ppm.** At the same time, the cost of the product is three times lower than in traditional technologies.

At present, light water production plants with a **capacity of 0.3 l / h, 1 l / h, as well as two lines with a capacity of 3 l / h have been manufactured**. The plants are certified. A patent was obtained on the line for obtaining drinking water with a modified isotope composition.

An interesting development in this field was presented at the **Moscow exhibition of NTTM-2013 by young scientists and engineers from Krasnodar.** Here, a method has been developed for the **electrolytic production** of water depleted of heavy isotopes of hydrogen, which makes it possible to reduce energy consumption by three times in comparison with the rectification columns.

The separation takes place according to the following scheme. Electrolysis of water is carried out, in which the molecules containing protium are mainly decomposed, because of the lower energy of the covalent bond. At this stage, the main condition is to maintain a minimum voltage, at which only the electrolysis process is started that is sufficient to separate hydrogen, but not sufficient to separate deuterium. Next is the synthesis of water from the resulting mixture of oxygen and hydrogen, depleted deuterium. In order to optimize the process and increase the separation factor, the electrodes are made of nickel.

The developed installation, according to the authors, allows producing a product - light water with any given degree of depletion, d**own to a very low deuterium content - 10 ppm**. At the same time, **the cost of the product is three times lower than in traditional technologies**.\[/quote\]

# Thread: Dehydrating vegetables
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dehydrating-vegetables.20985/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sunny Cowgirl — 2018-03-04T07:18:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dehydrating-vegetables.20985/post-236248>

I woke up this morning thinking about kale. As you do.

It's supposed to be a wonder food of some sort. I don't really buy that idea, especially if it has been grown with high deuterium water. Perhaps, if you really do want to eat vegetables the thing to do with high water content vegetables in places like Australia is to dehydrate them!

### JanSz — 2018-03-04T17:08:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dehydrating-vegetables.20985/post-236269>

And then hydrate them with DDW.

There is a lots of juices on the market.
They are advertised as 100% juice but reconstituted from powder.
It could be a good idea to get some of those powders then reconstitute it with DDW.

Same could be done with practically any food.
Deuterium is mostly in the water.
Anywhere else is less of it.

Wonder how that works in
fish eggs
urchin eggs

Jack mentioned to discard egg white and eat yolk.

/////////

### Jan Christer — 2018-03-04T18:56:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dehydrating-vegetables.20985/post-236276>

> [Sunny Cowgirl said:](/goto/post?id=236248)
>
> I woke up this morning thinking about kale. As you do.
>
> It's supposed to be a wonder food of some sort. I don't really buy that idea, especially if it has been grown with high deuterium water. Perhaps, if you really do want to eat vegetables the thing to do with high water content vegetables in places like Australia is to dehydrate them!

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=236269)
>
> And then hydrate them with DDW.

Yes, kale has lots of health benefits:

[http://www.whfoods.com/genpage.php?tname=foodspice&dbid=38](http://www.whfoods.com/genpage.php?tname=foodspice&dbid=38)

Also, plants like kale build structured water, which is the most beneficial for us, compared to plain liquid water. So you lose that if you dehydrate them. And you wouldn't get the plant-built structure back by rehydrating them with DDW.

However, yes, the deuterium content is definitely a concern in any food containing substantial amounts of water.
