# Thread: From Farley to Swayze
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-farley-to-swayze.21007/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Farley2Swayze — 2018-03-07T10:56:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-farley-to-swayze.21007/post-236536>

For the last seven years, I have been on a quest to not only find my health, but to also find an extraordinary level of energy, vitality, resilience, and ability--inside and out. At my sickest and brokest, I sat down with a notepad and wrote out the following goals:

-   Be physically fit enough to work hard, manual labor, survive off the land, and cope with disaster.
-   Be physically fit, agile, and conditioned enough to be able to defend myself and property from threats and the criminal element.
-   Be healed and stabilized of all medical conditions/disorders/symptoms.
-   Be physically attractive enough to find the gal of my dreams, able to pick and choose the gal I want to marry.

I was adopted in Las Vegas, NV at one month old. From what I know, my biological mother was 15. II am currently trying to open up the adoption records. I was born with a cleft lip and pallet, with much of my childhood filled with surgeries and orthodontics. I was soon diagnosed failure to thrive and nearly died. Chronic ear infections and five sets of tubes followed.

Growing up was the best of times and the worst of times. On one hand, I had the adventure of a lifetime, becoming very cultured, moving around as an Air Force bratt. On the other hand, health problems plagued me, including a misdiagnosis of Prader-Willi Syndrome that pigeion-holded my symptoms, haunted my childhood, and radically affected my schooling/upbringing.

The misdiagnosis was rescinded due to a genetics test that proved otherwise when I was 17. From then on, I went to the opposite extreme and did my best to forget about medical history and write any symptoms off as being a conditioned hypochondriac.

By the time I was 20, on the precipice of moving out on my own, I was losing jobs one after another due to symptoms of fatigue, grogginess, and excessive daytime sleepiness. I wrote it off as being lazy and “disciplined” myself to take Yellow Jacket stimulants and energy drinks to get by.

Crashing and burning soon after, I was diagnosed with Narcolepsy by a psychiatrist, who also added ADD and depression to the mix. I was soon on stimulants, which I took before bed to ensure I would wake up easier and not get fired from jobs.

Two years later, I rushed myself into the ER with chest pains and eventually being rushed into the OR for a gallbladder removal. I then had two years of varying levels of difficulty digesting fat, eventually developing IBS. I soon found myself on five medications, including two drugs prescribed to eliminate the side effects of two others.

Despite the health challenges and financial/time leverage required to hold things in check, I pursued my dreams with much tenacity, eventually starting and building up a chauffeur business. Unfortunately, my assumptions and beliefs blinded me to the dysfunctional synergy taking place between my health, my mind, my spirit, my time, and my money.

When the 2008 crash hit, I found myself leveraging everything to keep that business going. I knew I couldn’t lose what I had worked so hard to build and knew I was too sick to hold down a job that required any labor whatsoever. Everything came to a destructive end in 2010, with the dust taking more than half a year to settle.

July 6th, 2011, I began my health quest, dreaming of the optimal, somewhere in between Patrick Swayze and Diamond David Lee Roth. Since then, I have pursued this quest and this dream tenaciously, with obsession. This journey has cost me everything, including some of my friends, my honor, my word, my peace of mind, most of my prized possessions, thousands of dollars, and almost a decade of time I will never get back.

Along this journey, I have made many discoveries and have had many realizations. I now know one’s health is a foundation for anything else, since time and money are left to waste without the energy and vitality needed to utilize both. I now know that my diseases and disorders are a result of a methodical and concerted effort of the very few to control the many. I now know there are many others like myself looking for answers to questions, solutions for problems, and cures for diseases and disorders.

This is where the reward makes this quest worth it all. I now seek not only to cure myself, but to empower others to cure themselves. However, before I embark on a quest to cure others, I had better fix myself.

I was raised Irish Catholic. With this heritage comes a deep desire for finding the truth. There is something very wholesome and righteous about finding and proclaiming the truth. There is something very good and decent with empowering oneself and others with the truth. Let the truth set us all free!

### Jack Kruse — 2018-03-07T13:56:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-farley-to-swayze.21007/post-236540>

welcome. Something about your journey to consider. Stop focusing in on knowledge and begin to acquire wisdom.
**Wisdom is knowledge applied.**

Wisdom and knowledge are not synonyms. Knowledge is fundamentally useless until it is not connected, copied and pasted, by nature’s blueprint to become crystallized into a coherent organization.

Wisdom and knowledge are not synonyms. Those who seek wisdom must seek many truths to gain insight. They must be tenacious. Knowledge is a part of ignorance that man arranges and classify. Knowledge has the shelf life of fish in a market. It is not sustainable and does not always lead to truth. If knowledge is information, wisdom is the understanding and application of that knowledge and insight is the awareness of the underlying essence of a truth.

Sadly we can gain a lifetime of knowledge, yet never see the wisdom in it. We can be wise but still miss the deeper meaning of facts.

# Thread: Meal Timing Regulates the Human Circadian System
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meal-timing-regulates-the-human-circadian-system.21008/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### KalosKaiAgathos — 2018-03-07T15:08:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meal-timing-regulates-the-human-circadian-system.21008/post-236546>

Don't know whether this was posted already, but the message is worth repeating:

A study from 2017 (emphasis added by me):

**Meal Timing Regulates the Human Circadian System**
**Circadian rhythms, metabolism, and nutrition are intimately linked \[[1](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5483233/#bib1), [2](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5483233/#bib2)\], although effects of meal timing on the human circadian system are poorly understood. We investigated the effect of a 5-hr delay in meals on markers of the human master clock and multiple peripheral circadian rhythms.** Ten healthy young men undertook a 13-day laboratory protocol. Three meals (breakfast, lunch, dinner) were given at 5-hr intervals, beginning either 0.5 (early) or 5.5 (late) hr after wake. Participants were acclimated to early meals and then switched to late meals for 6 days. After each meal schedule, participants’ circadian rhythms were measured in a 37-hr constant routine that removes sleep and environmental rhythms while replacing meals with hourly isocaloric snacks. Meal timing did not alter actigraphic sleep parameters before circadian rhythm measurement. In constant routines, meal timing did not affect rhythms of subjective hunger and sleepiness, master clock markers (plasma melatonin and cortisol), plasma triglycerides, or clock gene expression in whole blood. **Following late meals, however, plasma glucose rhythms were delayed by 5.69 ± 1.29 hr (p < 0.001), and average glucose concentration decreased by 0.27 ± 0.05 mM (p < 0.001). In adipose tissue, PER2 mRNA rhythms were delayed by 0.97 ± 0.29 hr (p < 0.01), indicating that human molecular clocks may be regulated by feeding time and could underpin plasma glucose changes. Timed meals therefore play a role in synchronizing peripheral circadian rhythms in humans and may have particular relevance for patients with circadian rhythm disorders, shift workers, and transmeridian travelers.**

\-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Their conclusion is a bit weird: the researchers consider these implications only noteworthy for "circadian rhythm disorders, shift workers, and transmeridian travelers"

People on the LeptinRx know better...

### KalosKaiAgathos — 2018-03-11T19:47:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meal-timing-regulates-the-human-circadian-system.21008/post-236989>

Don't know if this was posted already, but another VERY interesting study, especially in light of the leptin reset:

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5470704/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5470704/)

I quote:

"**Throughout the four weeks, participants**

-   Eat three main meals per day, no snacks between.
-   Undergo a 12-hour overnight fast between dinner and breakfast, with just water permitted between.
-   Eat seven American cupfuls of plants (uncooked volume) per day (five as vegetables, two as fruit).
-   Eat protein with each meal (either animal, fish, eggs, nuts, or seeds).
-   Eat between 20 and 30 different types of plants (fresh herbs, vegetables, and fruits) over the course of a week for variety.
-   Use extra virgin olive oil and coconut oil as their default cooking oils.
-   Chew food thoroughly–aiming for approximately 20 chews per mouthful.
-   Do not count or restrict calories.

**In the second half of the plan (weeks 2–4) participants also**

-   Can eat butter and ghee.
-   Consume probiotic foods such as fermented milk kefir, sauerkraut, tempeh, and miso.
-   Increase their intake of prebiotic vegetables such as bananas, fennel, asparagus, cold potatoes, onions, garlic, leeks, fennel, Jerusalem artichokes, pak choi.
-   Consume bone broth/stock.

**Excluded from the diet throughout the four weeks**

-   Refined sugars.
-   Grains (e.g. wheat, rice, oats, maize, quinoa) and pulses (e.g. lentils and beans)
-   Alcohol
-   Caffeine
-   Dairy (can be reintroduced after 2 weeks if no adverse symptoms on re-introduction)"

While the study may not be perfect, this does come very close to the Leptin Reset. Outcome:

"The intervention, designed to manipulate gut bacteria, had a significant impact on digestion, reducing IBS type symptoms in this non-clinical population. There was also a striking reduction in negative symptoms related to cognition, memory and emotional wellbeing, including symptoms of anxiety and depression. Dietary gut microbiome manipulations may have the power to exert positive physical and psychological health benefits, of a similar nature to those reported in studies using pre and probiotics. The small sample size and lack of control over confounding variables means that it will be important to replicate these findings in larger-scale controlled, prospective, clinical trials. This dietary microbiome intervention has the potential to improve physical and emotional wellbeing in the general population but also to be investigated as a treatment option for individuals with conditions as diverse as IBS, anxiety, depression and Alzheimer’s disease."

### KalosKaiAgathos — 2018-04-04T10:33:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meal-timing-regulates-the-human-circadian-system.21008/post-238652>

Another one. Emphasis mine:

> **Meal Timing Regulates the Human Circadian System.**
>
> Circadian rhythms, metabolism, and nutrition are intimately linked \[1, 2\], although effects of meal timing on the human circadian system are poorly understood. We investigated the effect of a 5-hr delay in meals on markers of the human master clock and multiple peripheral circadian rhythms. Ten healthy young men undertook a 13-day laboratory protocol. Three meals (breakfast, lunch, dinner) were given at 5-hr intervals, beginning either 0.5 (early) or 5.5 (late) hr after wake. Participants were acclimated to early meals and then switched to late mealsfor 6 days. After each meal schedule, participants' circadian rhythms were measured in a 37-hr constant routine that removes sleep and environmental rhythms while replacing meals with hourly isocaloric snacks. Meal timing did not alter actigraphic sleep parameters before circadian rhythm measurement. In constant routines, meal timing did not affect rhythms of subjective hunger and sleepiness, master clock markers (plasma melatonin and cortisol), plasma triglycerides, or clock gene expression in whole blood. Following late meals, however, plasma glucose rhythms were delayed by 5.69 ± 1.29 hr (p < 0.001), and average glucose concentration decreased by 0.27 ± 0.05 mM (p < 0.001). In adipose tissue, PER2 mRNA rhythms were delayed by 0.97 ± 0.29 hr (p < 0.01), indicating that human molecular clocks may be regulated by feeding time and could underpin plasma glucose changes. Timed meals therefore play a role in synchronizing peripheral circadian rhythms in humans and may have particular relevance for patients with circadian rhythm disorders, shift workers, and transmeridian travelers.

At least the participants were subjected to the same light input: healthy participants in a laboratory!

### KalosKaiAgathos — 2018-04-04T10:51:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meal-timing-regulates-the-human-circadian-system.21008/post-238653>

Another one (emphasis mine):

> **Circadian Rhythm Profiles in Women with Night Eating Syndrome**
>
> **Night eating syndrome (NES) is characterized by evening hyperphagia and frequent awakenings accompanied by food intake.** Patients with NES display a delayed circadian pattern of food intake but retain a normal sleep-wake cycle. These characteristics initiated the current study, in which the phase and amplitude of behavioral and neuroendocrine circadian rhythms in patients with NES were evaluated. Fifteen women with NES (mean age ± SD, 40.8 ± 8.7 y) and 14 control subjects (38.6 ± 9.5 y) were studied in the laboratory for 3 nights, with food intake measured daily. Blood also was collected for 25 h (every 2 h from 0800 to 2000 h, and then hourly from 2100 to 0900 h) and assayed for glucose and 7 hormones (insulin, ghrelin, leptin, melatonin, cortisol, thyroid-stimulating hormone \[TSH\] and prolactin). Statistical analyses utilized linear mixed-effects cosinor analysis. Control subjects displayed normal phases and amplitudes for all circadian rhythms. In contrast, patients with NES showed a phase delay in the timing of meals, and delayed circadian rhythms for total caloric, fat, and carbohydrate intake. In addition, phase delays of 1.0 to 2.8 h were found in 2 food-regulatory rhythms—leptin and insulin—and in the circadian melatonin rhythm (with a trend for a delay in the circadian cortisol rhythm). In contrast, circulating levels of ghrelin, the primary hormone that stimulates food intake, were phase advanced by 5.2 h. The glucose rhythm showed an inverted circadian pattern. Patients with NES also showed reduced amplitudes in the circadian rhythms of food intake, cortisol, ghrelin, and insulin, but increased TSH amplitude. **Thus, patients with NES demonstrated significant changes in the timing and amplitude of various behavioral and physiological circadian markers involved in appetite and neuroendocrine regulation. As such, NES may result from dissociations between central (suprachiasmatic nucleus) timing mechanisms and putative oscillators elsewhere in the central nervous system or periphery, such as the stomach or liver.** Considering these results, chronobiologic treatments for NES such as bright light therapy may be useful. Indeed, bright light therapy has shown efficacy in reducing night eating in case studies and should be evaluated in controlled clinical trials.

This is the reason I cannot blindly follow Ray Peat either. It's quite problematic to see fasting as _per definition_ bad...

### KalosKaiAgathos — 2018-04-04T11:05:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meal-timing-regulates-the-human-circadian-system.21008/post-238654>

From the full text (emphasis mine):

> **Circadian clocks, feeding time, and metabolic homeostasis**
> Time of day of food consumption appears to be important for energy homeostasis however the mechanisms under which feeding at inappropriate time leads to obesity are not yet understood. Feeding rhythms drive rhythms in liver triglycerides and proteins independent of the circadian clock (Adamovich et al., [2014](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4444733/#B1); Mauvoisin et al., [2014](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4444733/#B32)). Feeding at “inappropriate” time entrains those rhythms into a phase opposite to the phase of other physiological rhythms dictated by the master clock. This circadian misalignment may result to inefficiency in energy expenditure and obesity (Mattson et al., [2014](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4444733/#B31)). In support of this hypothesis, correction of the feeding time in mice fed a high fat diet rescues the onset of obesity and restores the phase of rhythms in serum metabolites (Chaix et al., [2014](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4444733/#B5)). **The clinical relevance of the findings in animal studies is highlighted by the increased prevalence of obesity in the human Night Eating Syndrome (Gallant et al., [2012](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4444733/#B14)), characterized by a delayed pattern of food intake such that more than 25% of the total daily intake takes place after dinner and into the rest/sleep period (Allison et al., [2010](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4444733/#B2)). Some first evidence in humans show that volunteers on a weight loss diet lost 25 percent more weight when they consumed their largest meal earlier in the day (Garaulet et al., [2013](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4444733/#B15)).** In another study, consuming half of the total daily calories during breakfast as part of a weight loss diet led to greater weight loss compared to high caloric intake during dinner time (Jakubowicz et al., [2013](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4444733/#B19)). Further studies are required to elucidate the therapeutic implications of feeding time on energy homeostasis and body weight regulation.

Note that most studies were published AFTER 2011, hence, _after_ the LeptinRx...

# Thread: Hello, from Melbourne, Australia
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-melbourne-australia.21009/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### dbaverstock — 2018-03-07T18:50:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-melbourne-australia.21009/post-236592>

Hi folks,

Been lurking for a long time.
Got into 'bio-hacking' two years ago after my chronic fatigue flared up again and got sick of being sick.
Got really good at reducing the time it takes to 'bounce' back from the symptoms, although never really feeling like I've ever totally 'cured' myself.
Got well within 6 months which is a new record for me.
All thanks to the advice from Dr. Jack Kruse's blogs, Joseph Cohen's blogs and Dave Asprey's blogs.
Plus some extra help from friends I met locally in the last few years who are also into this.

Didn't really feel like introducing myself until I put some skin into the game, so to speak.
Now I'm invested I figure I should say hello.
May as well share my story and how I ended up here. Its a bumpy ride, feel free to skip it.

Ever since I can remember my life has been a living hell. Potentially before that too but I can't remember.
Suffered constant psychological abuse since I was three.
Sexual abuse at five, seven, nine, eleven, fourteen and twenty one.
Sick with severe chronic fatigue from six until thirteen. Seventeen until twenty two.
I suspect the traumatic home life I had combined with multiple vaccinations around six is what tipped the scales off balance permanently.
My mother was and is useless at mothering. Couldn't take care of the house, let alone her three sons, never knew what she wanted out of life, the point being she was inattentive and ineffective. Later in life I learned she has sporadic bouts of depression and epileptic seizures. Explains a lot.
My father was a workaholic, and alcoholic, combined with being bipolar and depressed, worked painting houses. Probably the paint fumes didn't help.
Constant fights and disputes at home, oldest brother defended us two younger ones.
Middle child was bullied at school, he was born premature and has cognitive and social deficits because of it, even at nearly 33 years old he acts more like a young teen than I do. Quick to anger and always on edge.
He sexually abused me a few times when I was still very young. Not so great.
Being sick all the time since I was six I was rarely at school and so didn't have the influences to give me the right social skills at the right times.
The lack of energy and staying at home meant my mother tried to teach me (poorly).
My only real hobby was using the computer I had to play video games and then later on browse the internet so I could teach myself.
I remember having few friends at school and the ones I did have were poor influences on me, having poor home lives themselves. I often remember being put up and left out at their houses by my mother, presumably due to a bad home situation that I wasn't made aware of at the times.
My mother and my older brother moved out of the family home when I turned 12. We never really had stable housing or living until I was 14-15. Then dealing with the throes of my late onset of puberty didn't help. Yeah I didn't really start growing up until everyone else around me was halfway through finishing.
Always was the shortest and thinnest.
Had to get testosterone shots from 12 until 14, probably should have kept on with them perpetually, but family couldn't afford it. Suspect that I've never properly finished developing. Can't grow a full beard!
I suspect this lovely combo fused to create the environment to help me fail to thrive.

I didn't really end up shaking my chronic fatigue until around 9-10 where my mother took me to a few naturopaths and treated me right. Conventional medicine always seemed to fail me.
Got sick again after a bad young love went all sour around 15 and got glandular fever (epstein bar virus).
They say it never truly leaves you. Slowly recovered by 21.

My life never really got on track until I ended up back with my oldest brother taking care of me.
He was the only one who really cared properly.
My life never really properly started until I turned 24. Got a real job and by 25 lived out of home on my own.
The healing truly started from then on.
Well, I thought so anyway, I've learned that my job in IT is killing me.

I still suffer from chronic fatigue, albeit in a lesser form, I can still function in a day job but can't do anything much beyond that, I have anhedonic style depression, I'm not ever really 'sad' but I never truly enjoying the fruits of life, I can fake it quite well though. I don't feel things, all the trauma as a child turned that off.
I obsess over topics, have a few pet ones, biohacking, computers and now oddly gardening. I'm told that's an autistic/aspergers thing. Especially since I don't enjoy socialising much unless its to talk about those specific things, so I have very small friend groups for just that. Talking to people just doesn't trigger any pleasure.
I often have intrusive thoughts and my thoughts race, have a hard time focusing in on organising things as you might tell by how this whole post has been structured.
I can be quite intelligent though, quite good at matching together data across different disciplines, which is probably why I enjoy biohacking and Jack Kruse's approach to health.

I've taken my health into my own hands. If life up until now taught me anything its that literally no one gives a shit about you, life isn't fair, and I've been lied to my entire life by everyone around me.
You could say I have some trust issues...

So what got me healthier?
So far, lots of things that alter or reduce anxiety/cortisol, boost NAD+ or mitochondrial function and repair cognitive function.
PQQ, CoQ10, Bacopa, Piracetam, Lion's Mane, Memantine, Selegiline, Shilajit, Moclobemide, Tianeptine.
My favourites for helping me 'function'.
I've tried CBT with a psychologist but could never really stick at it long enough. I suspect I have some PTSD lingering untreated.

Sorry for the long rant style and gaps.
Its painful to me for trying to remember those times which is probably why I struggle to do so coherently.

Anyway.

What lead me to these parts of the internets?
Well, it seems the only place where everyone is on point with their health and correct.
If anyone can help me onto the road to optimal its you guys and gals.

All I want to do is thrive, succeed, and use my life experience to help treat others much younger than I am now so they don't have to go through everything I did, wasting time, not living.

I want to be the best damn medical doctor there is here and incorporate the teachings we have here so people can truly get better.

Err.. yeah. So, Hi!

I do wonder if my story resonates with anyone else?

Peace.

### Jenny S — 2018-03-08T05:08:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-melbourne-australia.21009/post-236653>

Hi & welcome. I'm living at Bridgewater, just north of Bendigo at them moment but not planning on staying here. Moving north again.
I had chronic fatigue & at Paleo Josh suggestion went up to Cairns, got as much sun & beach as I could. Had blood tests before & after 2 weeks up there & showed a remarkable improvement in that short time. He is really helpful & has an osteopath business in Bendigo & Torquay - if he's still at both if you wanted to see someone. Good luck.

Welcome!

Sounds like you've had quite the start in life...thank you for having the courage to share.

In the area of past trauma and processing it mentally in a way that may help you to move forward, I highly recommend looking into the work of Dr John Demartini.
He applies Quantum principles to psychology in a way that makes a lot of sense, to me anyway. He holds a 2 day workshop called The Breakthrough Experience that is well worth doing.
You can read a lot of his content online and in books, but the "Experience" part is key and you can only really get that by "doing" it.

Also from Melbourne here.

Hi Jenny, nice to meet you. Yes I am aware of Josh and have been trying to arrange an appointment with him though so far unsuccessful.
I agree the sunshine is best for curing it, I had some good experience with that the last time I was able to take leave from work for three weeks and spent the whole time outside in the park in the sun during Autumn. It was enough to let me pull through the rest of the year until just recently. I feel like I'm slipping back down into the depths of my condition.

> [Trev Douglas said:](/goto/post?id=236743)

> Welcome!

>
> Also from Melbourne here.

Hi Trev, nice to meet you too. Thank you for sharing your advice I think it may really help if this person is of a similar way of looking at things to Jack Kruse.
Yes its been one hell of a ride but now finally on the mend and getting on with things. At least I'm still alive, well, I tell myself that anyway, a living shell is still living right?

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=236744)
>
> Please give Dr. K. your Mom's and Gran's health history.
>
> Have you read Dr. K's book? The leptin reset. It is the first couple of years of his blog.

>
> What are you eating? drinking?
>
> what is your nnEMF situation ...at home? work?
>
> How is your sleep? your libido?

Hello Caroline, pleasure to meet you.
My Mom has history of cardiovascular issues, same for her dad and mom, some mild epilepsy issues too, definite MTHFR mutation and passed onto me (i've got hers and mine 23andme data). No idea of more than that I should ask my Mom more about it. never knew my gran tbh. My mom had me when she was relatively old which probably didn't help the situation.

I haven't read it yet. Only really started getting serious about Jack Kruse's work beyond Facebook recently.
Slowly getting through a few old blogs.
Same for the podcasts, talks, etc.

Diet is a funny thing. I definitely think I'm leptin resistant based off my short readings of a few blogs. I'm perpetually skinny, never seem to have a great interest in food though its not from an aversion to it. The appetite just isn't there most of the time.
When I do eat I seem to be drawn mostly to proteins, chicken, salmon, sardines, eggs, those are my staples. Usually with loads of butter. I rarely eat carbs these days.
Meals tend to be only once a day, sometimes twice. Breakfast if I fit it in and dinner.

At home I'm nnEMF conscious, well as far as only lowering the output power on my wi-fi router to "low" so it doesn't reach my bedroom, turn it off at night.
I live by myself so the only nnEMF I am exposed to is the surrounds of wi-fi by neighbours, which is thankfully not too strong and only 5 networks.
A far cry from my last living situation a year ago in an apartment block three stories up and 20 wifi networks blaring at me 24/7.
I wear blue blockers at home and at work whenever artificial light is in play. Usually sit in the dark at home with only the laptop screen lighting things up.
Work is all CFL tube lighting and no natural sunlight. Evaporative cooling.
I work in IT fixing computers so typically the day involves being surrounded by 40+ nnEMF emitting things at any given time.
It is literally an nnEMF hell but its all I know how to do right now and so I keep on bearing it until I can change my skillset.

My sleep had been mostly good up until this week where I've slept only 2 nights for about 5-6 hours, the rest about 2-3.
I'm often extremely restless when trying to get to sleep and toss and turn manically until I'm physically exhausted enough to sleep. Its not ideal.
I've been taking Baclofen to help me relax for the last month, it was successful and it seems now I've built a tolerance to it as it no longer works, even after doubling the dose, so I've stopped trying with it.

Libido is sparse. I might feel some urges every week and a half to two weeks but it subsides quickly. I have no good outlet for that so I try to ignore it.

### johnsonmd — 2018-03-07T19:22:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shelleys-journal.21010/post-236599>

I am a middle aged, red head, Oregon country gal aiming to optimize my life. My best sermon of how to live is by example. I love to teach, but action speaks louder than words!
I have begun the leptin reset and epi-paleo diet and drink spring water. I use the magnetico, grounding, CT, sun bathing, blue blocker glasses, red and black lights in the house.
I am in menopause. I have gained 35 pounds in the last couple years. My goal is to get to my optimal weight, eliminate back and joint pain, build muscle strength, energy and think more clearly.

In February, I made a big keto soup on Sundays. I ate it during the week. It worked great for staying faithful to diet plan! I lost 5 # the first week, maintained my weight the rest of the month and then gained 5# the last couple days of the month.
I am wondering if hormones would help.

My plan for now is: more sun, more CT, more grounding and less cream in my tea.

### johnsonmd — 2018-05-16T04:18:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shelleys-journal.21010/post-241320>

Tried progesterone cream. Really helped with energy and brain fog. Went to Dr for bloodwork.. Began weighing food for more precision with portions for my diet. Lost 5 pounds this week. Bkft smoothie: coconut milk, lettuce and 1/4 cup blueberries plus 6 oz fish. Dinner 4 oz salmon and 1 cup salad and 1/2 cup cooked veggies.

### johnsonmd — 2018-06-11T19:55:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shelleys-journal.21010/post-243195>

I was really shocked when I talked to the receptionist about my labs and she said I had zero hormone (not sure which one, until later)! No wonder I need help! I picked up my lab report and it said: Prog-<0.5; Est-<15; test-26; TSH-2.52; T4- 1.3; T3- 3; vit D- 30. I decided to take some bioavailable hormones. I have more energy and have lost 10 pounds already! I am wondering if having my tubes tied means I am forced to take hormones...

### Jack Kruse — 2018-06-11T23:17:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shelleys-journal.21010/post-243206>

Not a surprise given where you are at. Trashed hormone panels are what the Quantum Thermodynamic series is all about.
**Hormone crash** is a low O2 surface environment. When oxygen drops cells have to use glycolysis and the PPP because they cannot use the TCA and urea cycle. This means that immune function is suboptimal when oxygen levels drop. This is why mitochondrial diseases of aging exist. Therefore it can be made worse with any deficit in AM morning sunlight too. What does pseudohypoxia (low NAD+) mean to our hormone panel? The cost of a high aerobic capacity is low fertility. This is why the pregnenolone steal syndrome exists too and why males suffer low testosterone and females have an upside down PG/E2 ratio in menopause and why they dehydrated. People forget that pregnenolone is made from cholesterol and pregnenolone needs Vitamin and T3 to convert to the sex steroid hormones. Blue light via the eye or skin destroy Vitamin A and T3 production. It is no wonder it happens. It is more shocking people do not see how the pieces fit. This process is not what the anti-aging docs think or sell to the public. We need to be medical nihilists in a blue-lit world. Cortisol over sex steroid production is due to a lowered melatonin level and the inability to use of oxygen in the TCA and urea cycles as the terminal electron acceptor in human mitochondria. This is increased in a really bad lit environment indoors and out of the sun. In a women’s gut at menopause, their LES allows more O2 to leach into the gut lumen and this helps simplifies the gut flora changing light release to the enterocytes and affect activation of the GALT. The blue light on th skin at the same time ruins the guts enterocyte clocks forcing the alimentary canal to keep cells normally replaced there too long. This affects the liver’s ability to detox our blood plasma and this lowers immunity as the process continues.

### johnsonmd — 2018-07-04T05:58:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shelleys-journal.21010/post-244928>

After reading about the sizes running small, I got the largest kiniki bikini top. I was relieve when it fit perfect! YAY! I have been wearing it with shorts as I build our cabin. We framed up the walls and got the plywood on the outside! The sun at 5000' elevation is stronger than I am used to. I managed to burn a bit in the places that were not used to the sun. I threw on my shirt when guys showed up a few times, but felt it was not see thru. The bioavailable hormones have not increased libido much, but the sun with the kiniki sure did! I was surprised!

I seem to be losing inches and top size is down a size or two. No more hot flashes or food cravings. More energy: redecorated the whole kitchen, carried the sheets of plywood by myself, hammered lots of nails, lots of hoeing and gardening, lots of outings with the grandkids!

I have focused on having seafood every day or every meal sometimes. Focused also on lo carb veggies every meal.

### johnsonmd — 2018-07-26T04:56:01+0300

### johnsonmd — 2018-09-20T23:48:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shelleys-journal.21010/post-251430>

Not a great day today... Feeling exhausted! Spent the last 2 days putting in our firewood with 3 of my grandkids, while camping at the cabin. It wasn't convenient to eat seafood and I helped the kids eat chips! Also drank coffee mocha for energy! The plumbers are getting their job done! Now, I am enjoying listening to the June webinar while relaxing in my recliner. I am wishing I was outside grounding and sunbathing but the yellow jackets are nasty today! Thinking about getting a lawn recliner that could ground too.

I am due to recheck my hormones and get another implant of estrogen, but don't want to pay a bunch of money for it. I am wondering if it would be more cost effective to do the hormone boot camp.

Note added later: I read my journal post above and decided I obviously needed sun. So I exposed myself to the sun and the bees and survived! Actually felt energised! Yay!

"A cell is said to be homozygous for a particular gene when identical alleles of the gene are present on both [homologous chromosomes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Homologous_chromosomes).[\[2\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zygosity#cite_note-Henderson's-2) The cell or organism in question is called a **homozygote**."

### johnsonmd — 2019-01-16T17:38:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shelleys-journal.21010/post-260502>

I have been doing hospice care for my stepdad. I hate watching what the therapy is for leukemia. Chemo, IV , pills and getting platelets as needed. I keep offering ideas and rejoice when he listens to me. Grounding pad, better diet (protein, fats, seafood) trying to figure out more sun! Dr appt 3-5 times a week!
I have enjoyed having a can of broth for lunch this week. Trying to have bkft and dinner as only meals. My BP was 107/73 this AM! Yay! Great improvement from 2 weeks ago (183/103)! Love having Ahi (4oz) for bkft with 3 pieces of bacon and my green smoothies! Love all the sunshine we are getting this winter! Have a grounding pad on the sofa. Get alot more grounding that way!
My right shoulder still has some nasty pain, randomly! From my fall the first weekend of December. My right temple still has a little numbness where I hit it on the toilet ( when I slipped getting out of the shower).
I notice at times that I can't think through things as well as I used to.
I have been reading through Jack's papers, I printed up. It's way better to read it on printed paper! I especially like the one on concussions! Diet is important!

### drezy — 2019-01-16T17:42:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shelleys-journal.21010/post-260503>

> [johnsonmd said:](/goto/post?id=260502)
>
> My BP was 107/73 this AM! Yay! Great improvement from 2 weeks ago (183/103)!

Wow! That's fantastic!

### johnsonmd — 2019-02-21T04:13:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shelleys-journal.21010/post-263688>

Yay! Stepdad has been declared cancer free! A year and a half ago November we were told his leukemia was incurable, better get your life in order! We didn't expect to have him last even one year!

I began to meet with a few friends this month to support each other in losing weight and improving health. I love the opportunity to share Jack's wisdom! This week I said "Dr Kruse says that light, water and magnetism are more important than food, tho food is important too." Then when someone said she lost 8 lbs on vacation last week and the gal next to her said I lost 8 lbs last vacation I went on too! I said me too! Then asked each one if they had spent time outside walking around sightseeing. Yes, each one who lost lbs said they had been outside alot! I loved the look on their faces!

Last week I woke up monday feeling like I was coming down with a bad cold or flu. I did everything I could think of and was well by evening! Sunbathing on front porch, HOT tub with epsom salt and ginger for an hour, grounding pad to sleep/nap all day, oregano oil, activated charcoal, garlic every 4 hours. Seafood, spring water. YAY!

I am writing a spiral notebook journal and tracking my weight loss, successes and ideas for improvement. I enjoy doing it with friends!

### kvond — 2019-03-24T22:15:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shelleys-journal.21010/post-266360>

I have only one of the MTHFR snps - how does it change your needs if you have one or the other but not both?

### johnsonmd — 2019-05-31T18:57:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shelleys-journal.21010/post-271700>

Amazing how time gets away from you! Have been reading and watching videos but neglecting journaling.
On mother's day our freezer thermostat malfunctioned and caused a fire. It burned the shop, house and dogs. The inspector said freezer and refrigerator fires are very common. Keep the generators vacuumed out! It's usually the relay that causes the fire. Ours was rare. The thermostat usually just breaks in the off position and your food thaws out. Ours broke in the on position and caught fire.
I will need to focus everything on rebuilding our home, so will leave this membership for a time.
I have really learned alot and enjoyed everyone!
Thank you!

### Marko Pollo — 2019-06-01T00:01:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shelleys-journal.21010/post-271721>

Very sorry to hear of the fire, especially for the dogs. Hope you recover quickly.

### Phosphene — 2019-06-01T00:38:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shelleys-journal.21010/post-271724>

Oh Shelley—so very sad to hear that. Our thoughts and prayers are with you.

So this was your main home and business, not the new cabin?

### johnsonmd — 2019-06-02T19:40:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shelleys-journal.21010/post-271837>

Main home and shop.
We will have to put the cabin building on hold. Thank you for prayers!
Its weird, but we feel really expectant for good things to happen in the future. It feels really good to be "camping" and having our power off! Less EMF and it's cool... especially at night!


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: David's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/davids-optimal-journal.21011/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### dbaverstock — 2018-03-07T19:55:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/davids-optimal-journal.21011/post-236610>

Hi folks,

I'm not really sure how to start one of these but I figured a start is as good as anything and better than not starting at all.

So.
I believe I am suffering from Chronic Fatigue Syndrome, since I was six.
Brought on by a bad home environment and a multiple vaccination at the time which unfavourably tipped the scales.
Coupled with some Aspberger's, OCD, PTSD, Bi-polar, Schizophrenia and anhedonia style depression and possibly remnants of an Epstein Bar Virus infection from when I was 15/16.
I'm now 27. I also tend to be quite restless and anxious.
Most recently had another relapse into severe CFS symptoms about a year and a half ago.
Which I held off with lots of cortisol, NAD+, style supplements. Since ceased that.
Currently taking Moclobemide, Selegiline, Baclofen, Memantine, and Tianeptine, to treat the mental disorders.
These are self prescribed after researching them. The Baclofen has started to not work though.
Not really doing anything else. I started taking these only a month ago and mental issues subsided somewhat and have only now started to return...
I work in IT under shitty fluro blue artificial lights with no natural sunlight during 9am - 5pm.
Outside of that I try to get one or two hours of sunlight a day.
I often have trouble sleeping, especially getting to sleep and when I do I don't think I sleep deeply.
I wake up with a dry mouth and so I assume I breathe through my mouth when I sleep.
I sleep on my back.
I often have bad breath because of the sleep problems.
I am quite thin. Weighing in at 44kg. I'm 170cm tall.
Always been this way. Late onset of puberty. Had to get testosterone shots between 13-14 and didn't continue on with them but probably should have. Can't grow a full beard to this day.
I don't drink coffee anymore, especially after starting my meds to treat the mental issues, as I find it is extremely strong and long lasting, was definitely contributing to the sleep issues.

I don't have any blood work or hormone panels to share. Though last time I got my blood test done it showed normal nutrition and blood work if I recall correctly though our standard testing here probably is less than desirable.

I \*have\* got my 23andme results with raw data decoded through LiveWello, Genetic Genie, SelfDecode and am happy to share if its relevant?

Pretty much working off a blank slate here minus the pharmaceuticals for mental issues and hope to seek advice on where I should go next to start treating things.

Open to anything.

Shoot.

Sorry if my post is a bit all over the place but it sort of reflects where my head is (and my life) is at.

### dbaverstock — 2018-03-09T13:25:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/davids-optimal-journal.21011/post-236751>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=236637)
>
> welcome

Hello. So glad you stopped by to welcome me it means a lot.
Today I read some of your EPCOTx Rx blog. Still digesting it. The melatonin story really hits home for me especially considering the majority of my environment is an nnEMF hell.
A real wakeup for Oxytocin too since my understanding is that is what helps us bond, which frankly, I don't do.
When I start to 'get' more of your blogs I feel I may share my understanding here as I go.
Stay tuned

### dbaverstock — 2018-03-10T05:59:13+0200

### dbaverstock — 2018-03-10T07:36:04+0200

### dbaverstock — 2018-03-10T11:33:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/davids-optimal-journal.21011/post-236824>

Geez. My head has exploded from all the truth you dropped in Vermont 2016.
There is now zero doubt in my mind about anything you say.
Not to say I have become blinded in faith. Not at all. Merely my mind is more open.
I've always had a healthy distrust of myself too. I get you.

I went twice to my cold bath today. Air dried. I feel more calm today than I have in a few days.
Naked at home while its 34 degrees here. Its nice.
Why did we ever decide to become so disconnected from where we came from?

We have to fix this!

### dbaverstock — 2018-03-10T11:47:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/davids-optimal-journal.21011/post-236825>

Hey you're right! I'm not nearly as hungry from doing this whole hot/cold hot/cold business during the day.
Increasing the electron flow.

### dbaverstock — 2018-03-11T02:16:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/davids-optimal-journal.21011/post-236903>

Got up roughly around 7:30am, so not quite at sunrise but enough to still catch some yellow/orange rays.
Had 105g John West Red Salmon (in spring water), 100g Brunswick Sardines (in spring water), 350g strawberries, 250gb blueberries, 6 figs and 500ml of evian spring water for breakfast. Was almost full, not stuffed, probably should have bought more for breakfast but oh well. Its weird how my appetite is increased what seems like 10-fold if I manage to catch the sunrise somewhat.
Ate everything while sitting out on an open grassy field with my shirt off and pants legs rolled up, eyes closed facing the sun.
Stayed there for roughly an hour while everything digested. After all, you say its a light story not a food one and I figure I must get proper sun exposure while I digest so the light is assimilated correctly.

Bouncing off of that, went home, got my laptop and then instead of sitting inside to read your blogs I sat out on my bean bag without shoes on, shirt off, on the front lawn, for the whole street to see, but I don't care. I don't have anywhere else at 9:30am to continue my full sun exposure.

Got through quite a bit and its now 11:13am. Moved my position to the backyard now where I can now get sun in private.

Finished reading BOOK REVIEW 1, BEING A SMARTER HACKER, AVOIDING FATES OF RONALD REAGAN AND MICHAEL J FOX (AVOIDING ALZHEIMERS AND PARKINSONS DISEASE), ARTIFICIAL BLUE LIGHT DEHYDRATES YOUR CELLS, ARE YOU ENTANGLED TO YOUR KID VIA MORNING SICKNESS (i was born via c-section), ALS, CTE and injuries in sports.... , ACOUSTIC RESONANCE, HRV, and MITOCHONDRIA, A SMALL BIO-HACK ON LIGHT AT A HIGH ALTITUDE, A PILOT'S STORY IS A LESSON FOR YOU in nnEMF, 30 Things from the innovator's cocktail party.

Its odd, your writing has become somewhat easier to read since watching Nourish Vermont 2016. Perhaps I've suspended my cynical goggles now and just let it all flow through.
Perhaps more accurately now I've heard you talk for long enough I can muster the imagination to hear your voice as I read your words in your tones.
Maybe this is how I best learn? I need to imagine the sound of the person speaking it to me while I read it> so maybe I'm not such a great reader until I get imagination flowing.

Anyhoo. Off to read some more of your content.

### dbaverstock — 2018-03-20T11:35:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/davids-optimal-journal.21011/post-237596>

Further proof that you're entirely correct.
Late yesterday and today I've been severely struck down with illness.
Yesterday I had a fever and for four hours stabbing pains in my guts, lots of gas and extreme fatigue.
It went away.
Today I am freezing cold despite having the heating on.
I have bright red rashes under my eyes. From reading your forum i'm lead to believe its related to shingles/herpes which I have had.
Clearly the environment inside of me is no longer hospitable to any other forms of life so they're trying to leave me.
That's probably what started yesterday.
Oh boy....
My IT job really is killing me.

### dbaverstock — 2018-08-13T09:12:38+0300

Wow. Old me was a real asshole on here.

Now going past October 2014.
Man its been one hell of an interesting journey.

Learned heaps about water and collagen now.
So much so I can coherently and simply explain how the body is a battery to someone who has no idea.

Appreciate it.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-08-13T14:03:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/davids-optimal-journal.21011/post-248353>

Nice job of doing the heavy lifting that becoming a Black Swan requires. You're an example of what it takes to get to Optimal.

### dbaverstock — 2018-10-15T10:54:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/davids-optimal-journal.21011/post-253222>

OK.
So two months later and more heavy lifting.

Currently up to the June 2015 Webinars.
My goodness some gems to be had in those few months up to that time period.

I think I've nearly pieced together my own puzzle now and its through some sheer effort on your part, my part, and at least about five others.

Keep learning more about how I'm a trainwreck from birth.

Several genetic defects in mito complexes and glucose/fat metabolism combined with malabsorption issues due to low stomach acid by default.
Top that off with defects with calcium metabolism and essentially being primed for calcium efflux by default...
With some sleep apnea in the mix combined with the blue light nnEMF hell I work in its not a great picture.

However.

Learned that my VDR is severely impaired, one could stop at that but that confirms to me that I am built for the sun at a northern climate.
My ancestors must have lived outdoors somewhere in Northern Europe going by what I can do well with naturally.
My wild dreams of living in Iceland, Norway, Denmark etc. no doubt have some intuitive merit to them that I should not ignore.

With that in my mind.

The n=1 approach to fixing me is so far summed as:
\- 1-2 hours of AM sunlight. 1.5 hours of midday sun. 1-2 hours sun near sunset.
\- I need magnesium, lots of it. I need sulfates. I need sodium bicarbonates. When I have a cold bath with these combined with some NIR/RED from a panel I have its heaven.
\- I need Quercetin, I need alpha lipoic acid, I need n-acetyl-cystine. I need Vitamin B2. I need Vitamin B3. I need Vitamin B5, I need Methyl Folate, I need Methyl B12. I need Acetyl Carnitine. I need Resveratrol. I need Ubiquionol.
\- The joke that a lot of these things that give me a massive boost to metabolism are flurophores is not lost on me now knowing the things you've taught me.
\- Especially Alpha Lipoic Acid. That stuff just about glows in the dark and really fixes how I feel after eating literally anything.
\- I need to clear out a lot of the anaerobes in my gut and sinuses that I think try to keep me the way I am so they can survive and keep me weak.
\- I need OXYGEN!!! When I breathe deep and hard I feel so much better.
\- I note when I get better I take one step forward, two steps back, its like my body finally gets the energy it needs to begin to repair and fight any latent infection within me and then tires itself out again fighting things. This is especially apparent with getting heaps of sunlight. The next day often isn't pretty. Even if I do all the other interventions mentioned above.
\- My right eye is stronger than my left, my right \*side\* is weaker than my left. Still haven't worked that one out.

Lastly. No one is 100% correct. If I had stopped at the first 'guru' I fell in love with I'd be close to death.
All of this is a synthesis from many people but honestly what I gained here from you for your unique perspective gives the right lens to understand things in the proper context.

Still keeping on and while I don't post here frequently I think coming back with a progress report is useful, at least for me to summarise my thoughts somewhat coherently and then if someone else can see this for inspiration, great.

Chronic fatigue/EBV from childhood is a real bitch but I feel so close to resolving this it's stupid to give up now.

Hope to see you at some point next year in person when I've accumulated the wealth in health and everything else I need to make the journey work.
I want to shake your hand.
It'll be worth it.

### dbaverstock — 2018-10-16T12:48:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/davids-optimal-journal.21011/post-253368>

Got my eyes tested.
Slight astigmatism.
I feel like this partially explains the weakness to my right side.
Whole system must be shot out by this issue.

Well. Here's to putting in the hard work for correcting that. Eye'll see you on the other side...

# Thread: CPC #18 & Airport scanners
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cpc-18-airport-scanners.21015/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Simon Wright — 2018-03-08T06:41:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cpc-18-airport-scanners.21015/post-236656>

I have just read the lastest CPC blog and I have alot of metal in my shoulders from my a little over zealous sports life. I have 3 large metal screws in my left shoulder and many many staples in both left and right shoulders.

When i go thru scanners (they are the raptor mm wave scanners) in Aus I can see this metal in my body .....even though they say the waves don't penetrate the body. Resultant to this i have a dull pain for 48 hours in my shoulders.

It is a legislated mandate that you must go thru these scanners in Australia and are not give the option of a pat down.

How ever over the last year and a half i have begun to show all my scars (quite a few) and explain to the person that their waves actually do penetrate my skin and ask (extremely politely) that they pat me down so i do not have to suffer such pain after going through their machine. Thankfully to this day i have been able to be patted down.

So Jack you are saying as this 5g business kicks into full swing this 'dull pain' 'could' become a reality?

Is there ways one could mitigate this?....ie can you walk barefoot ?...or wear say protective clothing? ( i know the clothing is an very open garment but in terms of a shoulder it can be well enclosed in a shirt of such.?

Any thoughts anyone?

### Jack Kruse — 2018-03-08T17:30:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cpc-18-airport-scanners.21015/post-236679>

That is what Jack is saying.......and your results are a tell of what is to come.

### Michael CULLEN — 2018-03-08T17:58:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cpc-18-airport-scanners.21015/post-236684>

Australia is a terrible, fascist police state.

Interesting picture on Jupiter's poles reminds me of ........Raleigh Benard convection cells I think occur in the retina where the blood has to span the distance from the photoreceptor in the RPE to the circulatory system (arteriole of the ophthalmic artery)

These new infrared images of planet-size cyclones clustered around Jupiter's north pole are just nuts. Fabulous work from [@NASAJuno](https://twitter.com/NASAJuno).

> Agree ,,but money is not the number one priority for me quality of water is. Here in Australia deuterium is one aspect of the water issue the other is the charge of the water which Australia is lacking.
> Hence the importance of Ozzies drinking imported waters.
> Having said that Jan I have already sent an email to Preventa regarding the purchase of there DDW ,no response as yet.
>
> On the positive side Australia is blessed with great Sun and strong UV even in our Autumn which is now our UV index is still around 10 until mid afternoon

If the money is not an issue
if you are otherwise healthy
order and drink and cook only with Preventa-125
or
mix one Preventa-25 with three bottles of Pellegrino or VOSS or Icelandic or similar.
(do not drink Preventa-25 straight).
///////////
...

### JanSz — 2018-03-13T17:14:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-arctic-tundra-of-mpls-mn.21022/post-237117>

> [Annemarie Heise said:](/goto/post?id=237114)
>
> Can you please comment on why you advised not to drink Preventa straight? Thank you

**My own guesses.**
After reading @[WalterNL](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19189/) complaints (when drinking Qlarivia-25 straight), and reading that DDW among other helps in diabetes.
I think starting with highly depleted water may lower blood sugar excessively.
But
getting there gradually should be acceptable (tolerable, or lesser of two evils), so it would be good idea to monitor
fasting glucose and insulin while using highly deuterium depleted water.

Drinking (successfully) DDW may cause other discomforts, that may have to be dealt with.
Say having brain tumor, now dead tissue, how do one lives with that? Surgery is/may be need, to extract dead tissue, discomfort in mean time.

@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)
As we speak, I am having discussions with my friend in Poland.
Prostate cancer metastasized to sacral bone. Prostate removed over 10 years ago.
Numerous radiations on sacral bone including very recent one.
He drinks DDW.
Started having pain, dilutes DDW only as much as he is able to tolerate his pain.
Is that good solution?
**Possibly he should get (major class) pain killers and go for stronger DDW?**
FENTANYL, morphine
I also suggested **that he assumes** that his pain is due to his (dying) cancer.

The plan is to follow this:

### drezy — 2018-03-13T17:49:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-arctic-tundra-of-mpls-mn.21022/post-237119>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=237117)
>
> Started having pain, dilutes DDW only as much as he is able to tolerate his pain.
> Is that good solution?
> **Possibly he should get (major class) pain killers and go for stronger DDW?**

That's a good question.

I know you know this but for reference Pg. 36 of that book Notes that "Temporary increase of pain" is a side effect and does suggest to "bridge this period with the help of prescribed painkillers".

Knowing full well my opinion has the value of a filled spittoon I'll share it anyways. I've avoided surgeries, anesthetization, and pain killers(not counting a couple advil) completely for my whole life and intend to continue to do so. Cancer scares the shit out of me though. By default, even having a strong aversion to any drugs, I think I'd be telling my family "Looks like I'll be high as a kite for the next month or two. Strap me down in the sun daily and I'll see you in a couple months."

### JanSz — 2018-03-13T18:07:44+0200

### Antonis — 2018-03-10T09:46:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shaking.21024/post-236820>

I noticed that am shaking a lot during the day especially in the mornings. Don't know what momig cause it. I researched but I could not get to the proper answer. Do you know or experience anything like this? Any thoughts will be appreciated.

### shiran — 2018-03-10T17:13:25+0200

what do you mean by:
shaking a lot dutdur?

### Antonis — 2018-08-07T12:51:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shaking.21024/post-247784>

I saw a local doctor and he said that I probably have shaking during the day because I am thinking too much about something that causes anxiety and I get stressed. Yea I am thinking about the environment here that sucks and I am also thinking of my family and friends who don't understand anything about the environment and they don't want to believe. But I move on with my life aswel.
When I do a physical activity like moving or lifting a heavy thing for few seconds, I feel the heartbeat afterwards and am shaking. I don't know if this is related to my problem by meaning that I have fast heartbeat and shaking for no reason. When I swim or free dive I feel good. It's probably the nnemf that cause my problem according to anything you taught us.
Would [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) recommend me to workout for the next 5 months as long as I live in this environment? Would lifting weights and sprints be good for my situation (anxiety)? Or swimming fast would be enough for me? Or nothing at all? Thanks.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-08-07T16:59:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shaking.21024/post-247808>

No idea. If you have anxiety change the environment.

# Thread: Maybe this videogame plotline will become truth someday...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maybe-this-videogame-plotline-will-become-truth-someday.21025/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### dbaverstock — 2018-03-10T12:07:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maybe-this-videogame-plotline-will-become-truth-someday.21025/post-236827>

**Second Disaster**

[EDIT](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster?action=edit)
[SHARE](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#)

_"I had hoped you might find this place – and finish our work. But it is too late. You and the Templars have squabbled over our refuse. You have wasted centuries. And so you have lost your chance. You cannot hope to stop the end now, Desmond. Only to survive it."_
―Minerva, to Desmond Miles.[\[src\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Assassin%27s_Creed_III)
[DIA satellite accident](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/DIA_satellite_accident)
[](https://vignette.wikia.nocookie.net/assassinscreed/images/5/55/2012_cme.png/revision/latest?cb=20131029224540)
**Second Disaster**
**Date**
21 December 2012
**Place**
Global
**Outcome**

-   [Earth](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Earth) protected:
    -   [Human civilization](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Humans) largely unaffected
    -   Death of [Desmond Miles](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Desmond_Miles)
    -   [Juno](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Juno) released from the [Grand Temple](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Grand_Temple)

**Affiliations involved**
[Assassins](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Assassins)
[Isu](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Isu)
**Participants**
Desmond Miles
Juno
[Minerva](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Minerva)
The **Second Disaster**, so named because it was a recurrence of the [Toba catastrophe](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Toba_catastrophe) that nearly destroyed the [Isu](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Isu) civilization, was a would-be cataclysmic event on 21 December 2012 triggered by a [coronal mass ejection](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/coronal_mass_ejection)great enough to devastate the entire Earth. Predicted, but ignored for much of recorded [human](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Humans) history, it promised to see the near complete destruction of mankind.

Ultimately, the calamity was prevented through the efforts of the [Assassins](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Assassins), who, guided by messages left by the [Capitoline Triad](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Capitoline_Triad), journeyed to the [Grand Temple](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Grand_Temple) that housed a device called [the Eye](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/The_Eye) capable of generating a planetary shield. On the day of the disaster, the Assassin [Desmond Miles](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Desmond_Miles) used the Eye to protect Earth from the CME, at the cost of his own life, and the release of a vengeful [Juno](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Juno) from the Grand Temple.

**Contents**
\[[show](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#)\]
**History**
**Predictions**
Following the [Toba catastrophe](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Toba_catastrophe), which saw the near complete extinction of both the [Isu](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Isu) and their human [enemies](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Human-Isu_War), the three known as the [Capitoline Triad](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Capitoline_Triad): Juno, [Jupiter](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Jupiter), and [Minerva](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Minerva), sought to help protect the world from a predicted second event. To this end, the three developed the [Vault program](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Temples), and built the Grand Temple to store their research.[\[1\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-AC3-0)[\[2\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-Revelations-1)

After much experimentation, the three settled on one idea, but before they could bring their plan into action, Minerva and Jupiter learned of Juno's treachery and sealed her within the Grand Temple. Following the eventual extinction of the First Civilization, humanity passed on the warning through word-of-mouth, though soon it became mired in myth and legend – ignored by many as superstition.

Ezio informing his allies of the disaster Minerva predicted

In 1499, Minerva appeared to [Ezio Auditore da Firenze](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Ezio_Auditore_da_Firenze) in the [Vatican Vault](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Vatican_Vault) and warned him of the coming disaster, and how to prevent it. Aware that Ezio's descendant Desmond Miles would watch this meeting via the [Animus](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Animus), she addressed the message directly to him.[\[3\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-AC2-2) However, instead of finding the temples, Ezio and [Niccolò Machiavelli](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Niccol%C3%B2_Machiavelli) concentrated on unseating the [Borgia](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/House_of_Borgia)'s control over the [Papacy](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Papacy), and left his [Apple of Eden](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Ezio%27s_Apple_of_Eden) for Desmond in the [Colosseum Vault](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Colosseum_Vault).[\[4\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-Brotherhood-3) In 1512, Ezio viewed another message for Desmond from Jupiter left in [Altaïr Ibn-La'Ahad](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Alta%C3%AFr_Ibn-La%27Ahad)'s [Apple](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Apple_of_Eden_2).[\[2\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-Revelations-1)[\[5\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-4)

Even by late 2012, neither the Assassins or the [Templars](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Templars) truly knew what was about to occur, although the Templars had their suspicions,[\[6\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-5) having read Minerva's message in the [Prophet's Codex](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Prophet%27s_Codex),[\[7\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-6) and sought someone with Ezio's [genetic memory](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Genetic_memory) to locate his Apple.[\[3\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-AC2-2)

**Juno's machinations**
Before her consciousness was imprisoned within the Grand Temple, Juno disposed of the discs that were left behind by Jupiter and Minerva to guide Desmond, so that he had only her words to follow upon his eventual arrival.[\[8\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-7)

Following this, in 1769, Juno used one of the [Crystal Balls](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Crystal_Balls) to converse with the future Assassin [Ratonhnhaké:ton](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Ratonhnhak%C3%A9:ton); she urged him to join the Order in order to obtain the [Grand Temple Key](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Grand_Temple_Key) and hide it, so that his descendant, Desmond Miles, might one day find it.[\[1\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-AC3-0)

Then, in February 2011, the Assassin [Clay Kaczmarek](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Clay_Kaczmarek) was captured by the Templar front company – [Abstergo Industries](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Abstergo_Industries) – for use in their [Animus Project](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Animus_Project). Forced into the Animus, the [Bleeding Effect](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Bleeding_Effect) soon overcame Clay, and he found himself unwillingly exploring the memories of his ancestors without the use of the Animus, driving him insane. It was in this induced state that Clay happened upon Juno.[\[9\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-TLA-8)

Desmond killing Lucy after Juno revealed she was a Templar

Juno used Clay to pass on the word of the true nature of the coming apocalypse to another Assassin, Desmond Miles, in the hopes that he might be able to reach the Grand Temple and activate the Eye.[\[9\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-TLA-8)In order to facilitate this meeting, Juno later manipulated Desmond, who had reached the Colosseum Vault on 11 October 2012, into assassinating his close friend and Assassin colleague [Lucy Stillman](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Lucy_Stillman), and drew him into an induced coma.[\[4\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-Brotherhood-3)

Once freed from his coma, and furnished with the knowledge of what he needed to do, Desmond Miles and his companions entered the Grand Temple. After a month, wherein they found themselves forced to locate several batteries for the Temple, as well as the Key itself, the Assassins finally managed to enter the Temple's inner chamber, and on 21 December 2012, approached the Eye.[\[1\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-AC3-0)

**Release of Juno**
**Minerva**: "_Better the world burn than she be loosed upon it._"
**Desmond Miles**: "_Whatever Juno's planning – however terrible it might seem today – we'll find a way to stop it. But the alternative, what you want... There's no hope there._"
―Minerva and Desmond on the consequences of his actions.[\[src\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Assassin%27s_Creed_III)
Desmond sacrificing himself to save the world

It was here that the Assassins finally learned the truth about Juno, and what exactly saving the Earth would mean for humanity.[\[1\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-AC3-0)

If the Eye was used, the Earth would be saved, but the binds restraining Juno in her prison would be severed, and she would be free to take her perceived vengeance upon humanity. If Desmond let the world burn, however, the cycle would begin anew, where Desmond would act as a leader whose message would become misinterpreted after his death, leading to humanity repeating its history of war and conflict; but Juno would remain imprisoned.[\[1\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-AC3-0)

Ultimately, Desmond decided that the cost of keeping Juno imprisoned – the near complete extinction of the human race, followed by his legacy being turned to justify mass genocide – was too great, and that at least humanity stood a chance against Juno, following which he activated the Eye.[\[1\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-AC3-0)

The Eye then activated what was described as a "global _aurora borealis_", which shielded the Earth from the worst part of the coronal mass ejection, and ensured the continued survival of modern human life. In doing so however, Desmond sacrificed his own life and subsequently released Juno from her confinement.[\[1\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-AC3-0)

**Trivia**

-   The Second Disaster is based upon the real world [2012 phenomenon](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2012_phenomenon), in which many believed the world would end on 21 December 2012, at the end of the 5,126-year-long cycle in the [Mesoamerican Long Count calendar](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mesoamerican_Long_Count_calendar). Incidentally, a [solar storm](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Solar_storm_of_2012) took place on 23 July 2012, missing the Earth as well.

[http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second\_Disaster](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster)

# Thread: Testing RF levels - help interpreting the data
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/testing-rf-levels-help-interpreting-the-data.21033/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ObstreperousPolarBear — 2018-03-12T05:36:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/testing-rf-levels-help-interpreting-the-data.21033/post-237025>

I'm a new member from Denver. I just bought an RF meter and was checking levels driving out of Denver, down route 83 (it runs southeast and ends up in Kansas). I wanted to share my results and ask people for help understanding them.

I am using a Model AM-1o acoustimeter from EMFields.
\- it does not give a breakdown of exactly what frequencies it is picking up
\- it is omni-directional
\- it can detect down to 0.02 V/m or 1 uW/m2
\- what units do most people use to express EMF levels? I will list my results in V/m.
\- basically, everywhere along that route is above 0.50 V/m, probably averaging around 1.0 V/m
\- this seems high, considering that the lowest detectable by my meter is still something like a million or a billion times the background EMF levels
\- I drove all the way to Elizabeth, CO, and saw little if any drop off in RF levels, which is shocking. It is a very rural area. There are horses and cattle - probably more of both than actual people.
\- That's more than 40 miles outside of Denver - yikes!
\- Are the persistently high levels likely due to military activity?
\- I did not go off the main road down side roads
\- I saw no difference with my car radio on or off
\- I saw no difference with my car on or off (it's a 2017 jeep wrangler, they are low tech, for those of you who are in the market for a new vehicle)
\- I did not see a difference with my phone on or off, unless it was actively transmitting
\- I did not see a difference with passing cars, pedestrians, etc. Readings where I stopped and parked the car were pretty steady.
\- in relocating, what level does Jack give an A? I am tired of moving - I only really want to do it one more time. I'll be testing the area before I go.
\- surprisingly, my home just southeast of Denver is actually fairly low in terms of EMF exposure. Tonight, my reader is not detecting practically any radiation. This afternoon, peaks were around 0.05 V/m and averages during the rest of the day were about 1-10 microwatts per meter squared. I'm curious what people think of that in terms of severity of exposure.
\- The area southeast of Denver is rolling hills/plains. Would people expect there to be pockets of lower exposure West of Denver, in the mountains? Aside from Lookout Mt, where all the radio stations are.

Thanks for any help interpreting this data. I appreciate it.

### Omelchenko — 2018-03-15T18:35:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/testing-rf-levels-help-interpreting-the-data.21033/post-237251>

that's odd. I live outside of a major city in South Carolina. The area here isn't ideal due to a close cell tower and an airport a couple miles away. So, I'm doing the same as you and checking out property further out in the country to move to. The things that screw the reading for me are cell phones and the car. I have to turn the cells to airplane mode or off and also turn the car completely off to get an accurate reading of the area. If you have both those in check then I'd start looking for cell towers, airports, railways and broadcasting towers. [http://antennasearch.com/](http://antennasearch.com/) is an amazing resource for this.

At least a mile away from all but airports I read at about the lowest point on my Acoustameter. Either, the meter has issues or theirs something else going on in the area. My bet is something else. You mentioned military in the area and that could definitely be the culprit.

### Sun Disciple — 2018-03-15T18:57:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/testing-rf-levels-help-interpreting-the-data.21033/post-237252>

Acoustimeters have the audio feature so you can pinpoint the source by sound. Im starting to get the hang of differentiation but if your in a super saturated area good luck.
if you scroll down to the bottom right of this page you will find example sound files of sources.

# Thread: Importance of EPA/DHA from seafood, NOT grass-fed beef

**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet

### KalosKaiAgathos — 2018-03-12T19:53:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/importance-of-epa-dha-from-seafood-not-grass-fed-beef.21035/post-237061>

From Calories proper:

[https://www.patreon.com/posts/13840426](https://www.patreon.com/posts/13840426)

Quote:

"After adjusting for a plethora of confounding factors, patients with red blood cell phospholipid DHA > 8.1% had a significantly lower risk of all-cause mortality than those with DHA < 7.2%. _No effect of EPA._ "

For those who are wondering why there's no effect with EPA:

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23206328](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23206328)
