# Thread: Prostatitis
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/prostatitis.21037/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Roger Seagunn — 2018-03-12T20:20:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/prostatitis.21037/post-237066>

Hello,

I'm not sure if this is anyone's specialty, but I need help with prostatitis issues.

I'm 29. About two years ago I had recurrent sexual contact with someone who had tested positive for chlamydia (I'm an idiot for continually seeing this person, I know already). I must have gone on antibiotics seven different times over 2 years (aziromythrozin, doxycyclin) and had positive tests for chlamydia. I guess I waited too long one time and I remember taking aziromythrozin and it not working very well. Ever since, I've slowly developed progressively worse prostate issues. I have, however, tested for Chlamydia now and test negative. The prostate issues, however, remain.

My urethra is red and swollen. My ejaculate doesn't come out 'strong', instead it kind of seeps out and looks clumpy. I have constant background 'soreness' there. From time to time I have voiding issues or other urination issues. Occassionally, sexual contact hurts. I'm quit terrified of eventual infertility as I want kids someday. I've been to several different doctors. I was put on doxy for 10 weeks, which didn't work. I had a digital rectal exam and had definite prostate inflammation. They tried to put me on fluroquinoline drugs (floxx), and since I've been a Kruse follower for years I refused. I had an allergic reaction to Sulfa meds (hives). Another doctor said there is no cure, it's likely damaged tissue/nerves and I should just try biofeedback relaxation techniques. (Yet, what if it is something else bacterial/viral and I am infertile years to come from now?)

Are there tests I can take that can specify the issue I'm having?

Should I suck it up and take the floxx medicine? Are there other antibiotics that may be effective? Could it be chlamydia that is still in my prostate even though I tested negative? I remember reading through Jack's book once and he said he believed enough glutathione c0uld destroy chlamydia. Could this simply be a low T issue? I'm at a loss at what to do...I'm thinking of going to a sperm bank just in case my testicles are slowly destroyed over the next few years. Any help is appreciated. Many Thanks.

I must have gone on antibiotics seven different times over 2 years (aziromythrozin, doxycyclin) and had positive tests for chlamydia. I guess I waited too long one time and I remember taking aziromythrozin and it not working very well. Ever since, I've slowly developed progressively worse prostate issues. I have, however, tested for Chlamydia now and test negative. The prostate issues, however, remain.
>
> My urethra is red and swollen. My ejaculate doesn't come out 'strong', instead it kind of seeps out and looks clumpy. I have constant background 'soreness' there. From time to time I have voiding issues or other urination issues. Occassionally, sexual contact hurts. I'm quit terrified of eventual infertility as I want kids someday. I've been to several different doctors. I was put on doxy for 10 weeks, which didn't work. I had a digital rectal exam and had definite prostate inflammation. They tried to put me on fluroquinoline drugs (floxx), and since I've been a Kruse follower for years I refused. I had an allergic reaction to Sulfa meds (hives). Another doctor said there is no cure, it's likely damaged tissue/nerves and I should just try biofeedback relaxation techniques. (Yet, what if it is something else bacterial/viral and I am infertile years to come from now?)
>
> Are there tests I can take that can specify the issue I'm having?
>
> Should I suck it up and take the floxx medicine? Are there other antibiotics that may be effective? Could it be chlamydia that is still in my prostate even though I tested negative? I remember reading through Jack's book once and he said he believed enough glutathione c0uld destroy chlamydia. Could this simply be a low T issue? I'm at a loss at what to do...I'm thinking of going to a sperm bank just in case my testicles are slowly destroyed over the next few years. Any help is appreciated. Many Thanks.

Hey Roger - I'm experiencing pretty much exactly the same thing as you - have you had any progress?

### Inger — 2020-06-23T11:15:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/prostatitis.21037/post-290455>

> [Ed Hodge said:](/goto/post?id=290428)
>
> Hey Roger - I'm experiencing pretty much exactly the same thing as you - have you had any progress?

Hi Ed,
Do you already tan naked, eat plenty of seafood and paint your testicles with iodine?

### Saichi — 2020-06-23T16:15:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/prostatitis.21037/post-290460>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=290455)

> paint your testicles with iodine?

# Thread: Cold after CT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-after-ct.21040/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael F — 2018-03-13T14:03:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-after-ct.21040/post-237103>

Hi.
I'm a new member here but I've followed the doc and the protocols for almost a year. With CT, I started with face dunks, then ice blocks and then cold baths 6 months ago. I'm doing CT 3-4 times a week, half an hour in 50-55F. I feel ok during the session, skin turns pink by the time I'm done. I don't shiver though and it takes hours to heat up. I'm cold all over, my hands and feet are like lumps of ice and my lips are blue.

Should I continue doing CT, will it get better with time - or is it possible that it doesn't work for me? Would drinking DDW water help?

### shah78 — 2018-03-13T15:47:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-after-ct.21040/post-237110>

how much dha do you eat?

### Jason Coates — 2018-03-13T16:29:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-after-ct.21040/post-237112>

Do you dress or shower immediately upon exit? I would suggest air dry or dry off and then get under a blanket or in a gown that allows for heat the build up around you.

### drezy — 2018-03-13T17:08:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-after-ct.21040/post-237116>

FWIW I time my sessions as much as possible to get sun. Lately I'm getting UVi 7 - 9 sun right after a session, but I notice that even on cloudy days I still can't stand being indoors for a while after a CT session. Your "solar panels" are at peak efficiency cold so avail yourself of sunlight right after CT if at all possible.

### freesia — 2018-03-13T21:47:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-after-ct.21040/post-237128>

Agree it helps immensely to get some sun during or afterwards!

What's your body temperature doing while your skin's exposed to 50 - 55 F?
...Mine rises - but it took a few months for this to be happening consistently and had to build up the duration slowly to avoid crashing, afterdrop, mild hypothermia and other various horrors.

### drezy — 2018-03-13T22:27:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-after-ct.21040/post-237130>

> [freesia said:](/goto/post?id=237128)
>
> What's your body temperature doing while your skin's exposed to 50 - 55 F?
> ...Mine rises

I haven't checked but I suspect it rises because I turn red.

> \- but it took a few months for this to be happening consistently and had to build up the duration slowly to avoid crashing, afterdrop, mild hypothermia and other various horrors.

Do you know or suspect why it took you a while? Was it thyroid related?

### John Saf — 2018-03-14T00:02:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-after-ct.21040/post-237134>

I was also very cold after CT. No shiver, but very cold "inside". It lasted up to 90minuts and was not good.
But it never happens anymore, i think my mitochondria has become stronger.

### freesia — 2018-03-14T00:21:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-after-ct.21040/post-237135>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=237130)
>
> I haven't checked but I suspect it rises because I turn red.
>
>
> Do you know or suspect why it took you a while? Was it thyroid related?

I started with a low basal temp and extreme cold sensitivity. I'd experienced this since early adolescence...pre developing ME/CFS. I strongly suspect thyroid, though I didn't have recent tests when I started CT.
My temp at first went down while in a 10 minute cold bath and it took literally hours to warm up afterwards. After about 5 weeks my temp was rising 1/2 a degree celsius immediately on exposure to cold. Then a bit further on I observed it was actually rising \*in anticipation\* of an icebath - ie when I was getting the ice out of the freezer and into the bath. Conditioning I guess.
From memory by the end of the first winter my temp (measured under the tongue) was rising .7 of a degree celsius while in the bath.

(My body temp has been normal since 2012. Woot!)

### freesia — 2018-03-14T00:25:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-after-ct.21040/post-237137>

> [John Saf said:](/goto/post?id=237134)
>
> I was also very cold after CT. No shiver, but very cold "inside". It lasted up to 90minuts and was not good.
> But it never happens anymore, i think my mitochondria has become stronger.

Yep the brown fat becomes more immediately responsive in my experience. Magic.

I pushed it too far once - about 3 weeks in I think - got mild hypothermia and took 9 hours to come back to baseline. I caution everyone to take it slow.

Thanks for all comments and ideas, everyone!

> [shah78 said:](/goto/post?id=237110)

I have been eating 2-3 dozens of oysters a week plus other seafood daily, but the last 3 months I have eaten about 50/50 seafood and piglet belly for deuterium depletion. I have not noticed any difference in cold tolerance.

### Brent Patrick — 2018-03-14T10:08:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-after-ct.21040/post-237175>

**Inger certainly is an inspiration! But is it necessary to go to such extremes? I am not directly overweight and do not have any significant health issues that I know of. Or what exactly did you have in mind?
**
Extreme is really only defined by each individual and their perceptions ,as others have mentioned DHA ,,,has to be pre-loaded into the system.
Do you consume high fat /protein meal before CT ?
Bitter Melon ? helps stimulate BAT from WAT.

You may still have excess omega 6 the closer your 06/3 ratio is to 4:1 the better your response will be to the cold. You will need more Omega 3's for CT and a cold environment to provide proper cell signalling and increase fluidity in the cell membrane

Oxygen + DHA + Water =Paramagnetism = increases the electric and magnetic fields in mitochondria

Magnetic fields repel cold

I also think attitude plays a big part I actually get a dump of dopamine with just the preparation of CT then with execution, the thought of getting in the Cold gives me a high.

Hang in there Michael don't give up the benefits of CT are many ,you just have to find your n=1

And this ,,

**Individual difference in humans who cold adapt**
Special forces training has been at the fore front of this type of information. I have a few friends who are former Seals and Rangers. I spent a lot of time talking to them picking their brains about their training. Most of what they were taught and trained for is not published for obvious reasons. Here is what I found. Cold tolerance is increased by large body size (small surface area – body mass ratio), abundant subcutaneous fat, good physical fitness (good ability for heat production, good circulation), male gender (predominantly due to larger body size), young age (via muscle mass and circulation), cold adaptation and good health. **Recently it has been shown that also personality affects thermal responses, especially the levels of extrovertion and neuroticism. Increased level of neuroticism dampens or slows the autonomic thermoregulatory responses whereas increased extrovertion has an opposite effect.** [https://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-5-biologic-magnetism/](https://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-5-biologic-magnetism/)

**The cold PUFA connection to grounding effects**

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/the-cold-pufa-connection-to-grounding-effects.18433/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/the-cold-pufa-connection-to-grounding-effects.18433/)

You may want to consider scuba diving shoes and gloves ,this makes a big difference to CT.I personally always wear the shoes as my feet get really cold as well.

### John Saf — 2018-03-14T20:27:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-after-ct.21040/post-237197>

It took me me aprox 6 mounts. Now it feels great during (not the first 30sec into ct), and after. In this time several things changed:
More seafood
More animal fat
Less nnemf
No bluelight after aprox 18.00
All the sunlight this place can give me in the winter (often with air CT) living at 62' N Norwegian mountains

### freesia — 2018-03-14T21:40:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-after-ct.21040/post-237204>

Glad you've ruled out thyroid issues @[Michael F](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21174/)
Blue light after sundown will have your body set firmly to 'winter-resistant' but it sounds you likely have all that and a lot more under control.
The only further suggestion I can offer is that as an experiment you ease back on the duration and/or the water temperature and monitor how you respond to that. Training the body to re-warm (the thermogenesis part of the deal) doesn't go smoothly for everyone. In my case pushing too hard just resulted in 'post exertional malaise' which undermined forward progress.
I used a hot water bottle, and my dog, to warm my feet after for the first few months. And going into my 7th winter at this I still wear polypropylene socks in cold water. Believe me my feet still get cold enough!

### Michael F — 2018-03-15T08:28:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-after-ct.21040/post-237239>

> [John Saf said:](/goto/post?id=237197)
>
> It took me me aprox 6 mounts. Now it feels great during (not the first 30sec into ct), and after. In this time several things changed:
> More seafood
> More animal fat
> Less nnemf
> No bluelight after aprox 18.00
> All the sunlight this place can give me in the winter (often with air CT) living at 62' N Norwegian mountains

Thanks. So maybe there's hope. I have been doing this for 6 months. I don't appreciate the first few seconds either, but the rest of my session feels perfectly ok. I could stay in longer, I don't feel really cold, but I tend to feel bored. It's when I have got out that I see that I don't get warm.
I would suppose I could eat more seafood again - and less pork belly. Albeit I was hoping depleting deuterium through eating you animal fat would help.
I use artificial lighting as little as possible daytime and none between sunset and sunrise. In the evening I have my fire place as the only lighting at home.
nnEMF is impossible to avoid fully where I currently live. However, my bedroom is well shielded and I don't get any readings on my nnEMF meters there. When inside at home, I spend as much time as possible in there.

> [freesia said:](/goto/post?id=237204)
>
> Glad you've ruled out thyroid issues @[Michael F](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21174/)
> Blue light after sundown will have your body set firmly to 'winter-resistant' but it sounds you likely have all that and a lot more under control.
> The only further suggestion I can offer is that as an experiment you ease back on the duration and/or the water temperature and monitor how you respond to that. Training the body to re-warm (the thermogenesis part of the deal) doesn't go smoothly for everyone. In my case pushing too hard just resulted in 'post exertional malaise' which undermined forward progress.
> I used a hot water bottle, and my dog, to warm my feet after for the first few months. And going into my 7th winter at this I still wear polypropylene socks in cold water. Believe me my feet still get cold enough!

Thank you for sharing your experience! Perhaps I could try to make my sessions shorter. Instead of 30 minutes 3-4 times a week I could do 15-20 minutes, more or less every day? I like the idea of using a dog to heat up.
My feet don't really feel cold while in the tub, it's only that they and my hands are the slowest parts of my body to heat up afterwards.

### Jason Coates — 2018-03-15T15:24:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-after-ct.21040/post-237243>

Even a pair of woolen socks helped me in the water.

### Wendy Harris — 2018-03-13T20:54:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-on-my-gut-analysis-very-strange.21041/post-237125>

Hi- I have had gut issues most of my life but became awful 4 years ago ( huge weight lose, SIBO -and systemic inflammation). Much much improved now as. Follow epi paleo diet and avoid diary etc.
I have had a gut analysis done by American gut project ( & hoped to feed the good stuff and kill off the bad ), but my results have made me scratch my head, even with Michael Moseley's book I can't get a handle on it ..
Any help would be appreciated...

I have 4 rare Taxa -not commonly found in samples and most people do not have them.
Genus Varbaculum.
Genus Naissaria
Genus campylobacter
Unclassified ML615J-28

I expected the normal stuff and got this --
Abundant microbes
Family provotella 24.9%
Family ruminococcacaae 13.4%
Family Lachnospiraceae 10.1%
Genus Bacterodes 8.1%

Enriched microbes

Taxonomy sample population fold
Clostridium 2.5%. 0.3%. 7x
Finegoldia. 0.7%. O. 17x
Prevotella. 24.9%. 2.6%. 9x
Collinsella. 0.9%. 0.1%. 8x

I've tried googling these results and get cows hind leg bacteria ( haha - only started eating meat again recently, so doubt its really from a cow )...

# Thread: And so it begins...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/and-so-it-begins.21044/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lyndal — 2018-03-14T04:58:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/and-so-it-begins.21044/post-237162>

I’ve been doing CT in the ocean each morning for about a week now, then have a cold shower when I get home. Amazing how warm the water is. I do find myself shivering afterwards for a while some days.

I’m struggling to only eat within the Epi-Paleo proticol. I keep giving into my sugar addiction...grrr. I’m trusting the more sunlight I get and the more CT I do, the more I’ll want to eat Epi-Paleo only and get my Leptin working properly. In the past I’ve been off all sugar for about 2 1/2 years. Never seemed to reach Leptin Sensitivity though. Back on about 6 months ago. Really screwed myself by giving in to it again.

I have an opportunity to go up North for a few months. Up near Byron. Here I’ll be able to focus fully on myself. I can spend hours at the beach from first light. Going in and out of the ocean as need be. I can rest and relax to my hearts content. I can live in an environment that has no one else bringing sugar in to the house. I can have full control over lights, wifi, sleep, etc. I can get educated in the realm of Optimal Health, applying, evaluating and adjusting accordingly. I can leave the daily stresses behind me.

It’s an opportunity to reconnect with myself as well. Work out who I truly be and what it is I would like to be doing with my life.

The only downside is I’ll be leaving my family behind. My husband will come up when he can, a few days at a time. Our kids (23 & 25) have said they’ll come up and visit too when they can. I’m thinking the benefits will far outweigh the negatives or so I keep telling myself.

I’m over living in survival mode. I want to have the energy to engage in life and stop sitting on the sidelines watching it pass me by. I’m sick and tired of being sick and tired!

And so it begins...

### caroline — 2018-03-14T06:32:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/and-so-it-begins.21044/post-237168>

Hi Lyndal - You are making a fantastic beginning! go for it girl! Dr. K. always tells us to take care of ourself first .......and your family will reap all the benefits.

There is a clothing optional beach in Byron - probably lots of them!

Don't look back ...this is your time to shine - your journey to optimal!

Are you eating a big enuf BAB?? that should knock out all cravings. Don't give in because you will set yourself back to the beginning.

wishing you magic and sending you a Koala bear hug x

### Lyndal — 2018-03-14T07:16:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/and-so-it-begins.21044/post-237169>

Thanks Caroline, got it!

I’ve been to the one at Tyagarah before, not the one at Kings Beach. Planning on hanging out there as much as I can whilst I’m up that way. I’ll have a look at getting some tan through bathers as well.

I’ll keep looking forward...keep my head towards the sunshine.

Hmm...maybe I’m not eating a big enough BAB. Having changed over to seafood I’m not yet sure what’s enough for me. Will play around with quantities and see what works best. Hadn’t thought about it stemming from breakfast. Makes sense now you’ve shone a light on it.

Thanks again Caroline, greatly appreciate all your assistance, encouragement and support, as well as the magic and Koala bear hug. Which I’m sending back to you too

### caroline — 2018-03-14T09:16:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/and-so-it-begins.21044/post-237172>

Your BAB is hugely important for so many reasons........

### Jason Coates — 2018-03-14T09:34:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/and-so-it-begins.21044/post-237173>

Caroline's point on the big breakfast cannot be underestimated.  It will help sort out those cravings.

### caroline — 2018-03-14T22:54:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/and-so-it-begins.21044/post-237212>

Have you tried pork belly for BAB .........simply awesome! very filling and it will knock out those cravings.

with a side of prawns!

### Lyndal — 2018-03-22T04:26:52+0200

### Lyndal — 2018-03-22T05:09:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/and-so-it-begins.21044/post-237720>

Ok, so my accommodation near Byron fell through - oh well.

That’s had me have a rethink and I’m feeling up higher north might be a better option. I’m liking where Richelle is at Agnes Water.

The plan is to head off next week. My husband will drive up with me, then he’ll fly back a few days later.

It really is time to make my health and well-being a priority. That being said, I’m so fricken nervous and exhausted, it’s quite overwhelming. I’m trusting I can follow through with this plan. My mental health has taken a bit of a dive of late too. I’m sure it’s fear, patterning, guilt, shame, my saboteur trying to stop me, let alone lack of sunshine. I’ll cry my way there if I have to!

If anybody has any other suggestions of where to locate my self for a few months for Optimal health and healing in the land of Oz, I’d greatly appreciate the recommendations.

Still struggling with the sugar cravings. Working on the BAB and will give the pork belly and prawns a go, as Caroline suggested. Seafood alone, no matter the quantity, doesn’t appear to be able to knock those cravings off.

Continuing to CT and ground every day, mostly at the beach at sunrise, followed by a cold shower.

Been working on the oxytocin too

### caroline — 2018-03-22T08:17:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/and-so-it-begins.21044/post-237724>

I think Agnes Waters sounds like a great idea ......baby steps. Rome definitely wasn't built in a day! Give yourself a break - cut yourself some slack.

Think of all the positives in your life ....enjoy the road trip up with your DH. Make some great memories while you are at it.

Have a lot of pork belly for BAB ........

One day at a time Lyndal........keep focusing on the three legged stool. You are already doing great things for yourself ...and for your family.

if you want to talk - pm me

It will be great - you only need to concentrate on yourself and getting well ....before long you won't recognise yourself!

Plan a trip to Mexico for next Christmas to meet Jack.......

>
> Thanks again Caroline, greatly appreciate all your assistance, encouragement and support, as well as the magic and Koala bear hug. Which I’m sending back to you too

Maybe you need more fat for your breakfast...that was my big help to stop the cravings. I usually eat 4 eggs (scrambled, fixing it with 2 tablespoons of butter) and some fish (in total about 50g of protein). As soon as the eggs are done I just put one or two extra tablespoons on top of the eggs and let it melt. It´s delicious. those 2 spoons made a big difference for me. I can easily stay without eating until 5 p.m.

# Thread: Methylene Blue consolidated applications
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/methylene-blue-consolidated-applications.21045/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AstralJam8 — 2018-03-14T09:34:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/methylene-blue-consolidated-applications.21045/post-237174>

Hi all,

I've been looking for a thread that consolidates the applications of Methylene Blue / MitoBlue / MB and have only found a few mentions scattered here & there, so I'm thinking it might be a good time to ask everyone about their ideas and applications so far, and gather any questions or suggestions.

I'll get it started on something I've been thinking about trying with it. I want to use it in conjunction with PBM on a scar that I had received during my last relocation. It spans two fingers so I'll have a "control" for comparison. It has been several months since the incident so the tissue is mostly "repaired", but it's not too pretty.. My idea is to do a localized exfoliation using some Co-Q10 lotion mixed with Diatomaceous Earth to gently "re-activate" the wound site and gradually remove the excess fibroid tissue, then dab with MB and apply concentrated IR in the 650nm and 810nm ranges from LEDs, pulsed at ~10Hz to get the Mitochondria buzzing. Here's the article that gave me the idea about IR PBM "re-activating" scars:
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3926176/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3926176/)

And more about the specific effects of PBM on skin fibrosis:
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4848333/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4848333/)

When I get the LED rig setup I'll start posting more details and pictures and hopefully figure out doses for both the MB concentration and PBM duration (suggestions are welcome!) Hope to see your ideas, applications and results here as well!

### AstralJam8 — 2018-04-09T04:10:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/methylene-blue-consolidated-applications.21045/post-239021>

Ok, sorry for the long delay, I ran into a problem where my 3D "PrintrBot" couldn't receive files bigger than 3mb so I had to reverse-engineer Brook's crappy cloud service before proceeding past the MK1 design

So here's what I've done so far:

Build the PBM prototype MK1
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" } [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/pbm_mk1_800_600-jpg.7302/)

One LED per wavelength doesn't seem like enough, so I printed an emitter array for the MK2 design (all LEDs focus to a single point 1 inch from the parabolic dish)
[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/pbm_mk2_complete_800_600-jpg.7304/)
[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/pbm_mk2_emitter_800_600-jpg.7305/)

Add a TTL driver board, 3 channels (3x 650nm, 3x 530nm, 8x 810nm) controlled by Arduino
[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/pbm_mk2_driver_800_600-jpg.7307/)
And here's the pair of scars I'm working with (smaller one on the middle finger will be the "control")
[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/pbm_scar_test1_800_600-jpg.7308/)

Also, while reading Robert Becker's "Cross Currents", I happened across an interesting tidbit in one of the later chapters, in which it was found that **Silver ions dediferentiate Fibroblasts into Stem Cells**! So, I want to try this:

1\. Exfoliate with Diatomacious Earth in Co-Q10 lotion (should also re-activate the healing process via micro-abrasion)
2\. Apply ~800 millivolts with a Silver positive electrode for a few minutes
3\. Apply MB
4\. Apply PBM with 10Hz pulse rate for ~15 minutes a day

For the PBM, I'll have to experiment with the 3 wavelengths, either alternating the 530nm/650nm cluster in the middle with the 810nm outer ring, or all on/off. Looking forward to the weekly updates!

### AstralJam8 — 2018-04-15T04:22:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/methylene-blue-consolidated-applications.21045/post-239419>

quick update on the MB observations. I've been trying to get it to absorb deeper into the skin, and I noticed that if it's applied just after getting out of the shower (while the skin is still soft & expanded, but not "pruned") it will stain much better and can't be washed off. After letting it sit while the skin dries & shrinks down again, I run the PBM for ~15min. and notice the fibrosis will redden, but also the blue will start to disappear. I've gone to sleep and woken up the next day with the stain still present, but then after a few ~15min. PBM sessions it's gone! My question is, does this occur because of the enlarged capillaries carrying it away or is the MB getting reduced in an elevated oxygen environment due to the PBM? Here's a quote from wikipedia: "Solutions of this substance are blue when in an oxidizing environment, but will turn colorless if exposed to a reducing agent."
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Methylene\_blue](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Methylene_blue)

If it is getting reduced, what is the reducing agent? What is happening here?

### drezy — 2018-04-22T00:13:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/methylene-blue-consolidated-applications.21045/post-239664>

Have you ever use kicad or eagle and have boards fabed for you?

# Thread: Airplane mode

**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### shannoncorrin — 2018-03-14T16:24:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/airplane-mode.21046/post-237185>

apologies if this is a silly question. Does airplane mode really help decrease emf? Can I have my phone in my pocket to listen to podcasts if it’s on airplane?

### Sun Disciple — 2018-03-14T16:53:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/airplane-mode.21046/post-237187>

Yup thats a wise move. Ive measured on my Iphones and as long as you have location services, blutooth, wifi and cellular off your good to go.

### Omelchenko — 2018-03-14T22:09:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/airplane-mode.21046/post-237208>

if you still have to get calls, you can always turn off cellular data. You can still make and recieve calls but it stop your phone from pinging cell towers constantly. THen you only get the increased RF when a call is happening. I tested it with my RF meter. This is at least true for iphones

This has always been my concern with the ketogenic warriors on the net. They push it as a panacea and cannot defend it. If the host was on her game when Ivor jumped to try to change the focus to diabetes from cancer she would have pointed out that diabetic has a huge increased risk of cancer.

Neither Ivor, the guest or host seem to understand how food macronutrients relate to mitochondrial heteroplasmy and this is the link of health to T2D and to cancer.

# Thread: True Seafood Allergy
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/true-seafood-allergy.21050/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kai-Robin — 2018-03-15T22:01:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/true-seafood-allergy.21050/post-237261>

I have had TRUE IgE seafood allergy since I was a baby. I almost died from it as baby.

In my grown up years now it seems like at least some of the allergies are milder or maby gone. I can eat most shellfish without swelling up in the throut.
I can eat tuna but I'm haveing a small reactions to it, but small enough so that I dare eat it. All other fish I get stronger raction from, even handling a fish or to be in the cooking steam.

Should I keep on eating and try to train the allergy away, or could this be a dangerous game.

Any thougts suggestion. What should people with true IgE fish allergy do to become optimal ?

### Allin — 2018-03-16T18:30:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/true-seafood-allergy.21050/post-237314>

> [Kai-Robin said:](/goto/post?id=237261)
>
> I have had TRUE IgE seafood allergy since I was a baby. I almost died from it as baby.
>
> In my grown up years now it seems like at least some of the allergies are milder or maby gone. I can eat most shellfish without swelling up in the throut.
> I can eat tuna but I'm haveing a small reactions to it, but small enough so that I dare eat it. All other fish I get stronger raction from, even handling a fish or to be in the cooking steam.
>
> Should I keep on eating and try to train the allergy away, or could this be a dangerous game.
>
> Any thougts suggestion. What should people with true IgE fish allergy do to become optimal ?

_On the road to optimal this autoimmune reaction will turn off. You are not vibrating at the frequency you were designed to be at. Living in Norway you have to do A LOT of things right just to make headway. Can you get better - yes. Can you get to optimal - no. However you have made and can make more progress toward optimal: Keep eating as much shell fish as you can, eat as much pork and lamb as you can, STAY AWAY from fruits, drink deuterium depleted water everyday, get IR sauna everyday, do CT everyday, see the sunrise everyday in your bare feet, turn off the power to your house every night, use only candles, the fireplace and a kerosene lamp at night.....or move to 25 degrees latitude or less. Good luck brother._

### JanSz — 2018-03-16T18:59:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/true-seafood-allergy.21050/post-237320>

> [Kai-Robin said:](/goto/post?id=237261)
>
> I have had TRUE IgE seafood allergy since I was a baby. I almost died from it as baby.
>
> In my grown up years now it seems like at least some of the allergies are milder or maby gone. I can eat most shellfish without swelling up in the throut.
> I can eat tuna but I'm haveing a small reactions to it, but small enough so that I dare eat it. All other fish I get stronger raction from, even handling a fish or to be in the cooking steam.
>
> Should I keep on eating and try to train the allergy away, or could this be a dangerous game.
>
> Any thougts suggestion. What should people with true IgE fish allergy do to become optimal ?

Seafood is mostly about DHA and some micronutrients.
When I eat lots of seafood it does not cause me any problems,
but when I do Fatty Acids Analysis I find my EPA thru the roof.
So I limit my seafood part of food that I eat.

Why do not you get analysis and find out if you are actually missing on DHA.
DHA is available from many other foods, other that seafood.
DHA when in the body lasts long time (so it takes a while before one gets low on it).
Omega-3 fatty acids are strong when comparing to Omega-6.
Excess of O3 will deprive you of O6 that is also highly important.
O3 and O6 are not individual fatty acids, that are groups.
**You should monitor each individual fatty acid, as individual, not as a group.**
You can monitor your status as often as you wish.

May want to peruse my posts on this (short) thread:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/)

Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum

.................

Good luck brother._

### Kai-Robin — 2018-03-20T07:33:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/true-seafood-allergy.21050/post-237589>

Thanks for all the tips! I really enjoy the life here in norway, actually people are in pretty good shape over here consider the high latitudes. Scandinavians are generally alot outside in nature at the mountains, skiing, fishing, hunting and so on. I've moved to the southern part. I grew up above the artic circle at 67th latitude. But now I live at 58th in the most sunny part of the country. It makes a big difference in the winter. I try to go to tenerife about 3\* 2weeks in the winter to catch som rays. In summer I feel the sun here is more than strong enough. We stay around 6-7 at the UV index in summer. Enoug for my skin type.

# Thread: Can melting ice deplete deuterium?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-melting-ice-deplete-deuterium.21057/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kris90 — 2018-03-17T17:36:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-melting-ice-deplete-deuterium.21057/post-237365>

In terms of freezing water, we can dump the frozen layer and the remaining liquid should be relatively lower in deuterium. My understanding of how this occurs is that cooling water increases the EZ, and larger EZ = excludes protons including deuterium.

Now, what if you put a bucket of ice cubes out in the sun and let it melt? Would the melted water be deuterium depleted in a similar manner because sunlight builds the EZ? Wondering if this might be a good hack using ice cubes that come from my natural groundwater.

### Jonathin — 2018-03-17T18:00:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-melting-ice-deplete-deuterium.21057/post-237366>

I was thinking something similar and toss half the ice before it melts.

### kris90 — 2018-03-17T18:38:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-melting-ice-deplete-deuterium.21057/post-237368>

I am also thinking that boiling water could be another hack. The light water would evaporate first, so if there could be a way to capture the vapor and then cool it, and toss the left over liquid.

### Jan Christer — 2018-03-17T20:01:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-melting-ice-deplete-deuterium.21057/post-237372>

> [kris90 said:](/goto/post?id=237368)
>
> I am also thinking that boiling water could be another hack. The light water would evaporate first, so if there could be a way to capture the vapor and then cool it, and toss the left over liquid.

That process is called distillation and is addressed extensively here:

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/how-to-make-ddw-do-you-buy-this-50ppm.20927/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/how-to-make-ddw-do-you-buy-this-50ppm.20927/)

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/electric-distillation.20982/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/electric-distillation.20982/)

# Thread: Dr. Hoeper's Journal- Looking for all inputs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-hoepers-journal-looking-for-all-inputs.21059/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gregory H Hoeper — 2018-03-18T03:11:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-hoepers-journal-looking-for-all-inputs.21059/post-237386>

I'll go on about my history... dissect away I am looking for any and all advice.

28 (almost 29) year old recovering func. medicine doctor working hard to convince those the way of the mitochodriac. Born cesarean section, my mother was on antibiotics while she breastfed me. When I was 2 I got a toenail fungus on both big toes in a tropical island. I still have it 26 years later. I started to get fat when I was about 8 or 9, was very fat until age 16 (S.A.D.) then lost weight and was very skinny (for 4 years, skinny from 16-19/20). I took all those garbage weightlifting supplements for a long time. Maybe into my 20's (mid 25 ish some of them). Video game player. Then got huge-fatass in college (up to about 280) drinking sometimes tons of video game binging really gross with energy drinks I hope I can attone for all of my sins and went paleo and lost it all by the time I was 22. I got fatter a few more times getting to around 220ish. I am fairly muscular and have had somewhat impresive lifts in the past. However now with joint aches and whatnot it gets hard (29 years old and joint aches). Anyway, I've always dug for answers and was in the world of diet arguing, so finally happy Dr. Kruse was finally able to make food easy (environment stupid).

Anyway, got into func. medicine, and working with patients. Helping people, but my problems seemed to not resolve. And actually when I think about it, I can't remember when my energy started getting so bad, but I remember in college and highschool and all of it being bad. And then I also manual labored for some time. So i was probably healthiest then. But when I think about how unhealthy I have been my whole life, I am surprised I am still alive. I get what would appear to white patches of skin with white hair that used to get irritated and red and itchy with alcohol. I have experimented with sooooo many supplements, super high doses etc etc. I got some blood work done april 2017, showed poor kidney function, low cholesterol (like 145 or someething?) highish liver enzymes, high bilirubin, low vitamin D, whacky electrolytes, and a bunch of other stuff. SO i tried really hard with supplements and the whole 9 yards. And then ended up going into zero carb towards august (end, and probably too early) and did that for some time. Got bloodwork in November, there was some good and bad. I attached it but if you're lazy Cholesterol 256 HDL 95 BUN/Creatinine 24 Reverse t3 22 bilirubin 1.3 MCV 94.

\*\*\*MY LARGEST REGRET EVER: Using PEMF device on my head in the 8-10hz for a few weeks before I discovered Dr. Kruse." Deathly affraid currently I will get brain cancer yesterday. I have also made 3 transpolar flights, and did ground myself the third flight (all in the last 6 months)

Anyway, symptoms: I think I need coffee to use the bathroom, I usually have it after I eat breakfast now so I neve know. Brainfog, discoordination t feels like of my extremities and shoulder pain and leg pain. Freezing hands and stuff. It is super weird, but I can't figure out when I get it. So random all day. I have had bloating with vegetables in the past.

What I am doing currently:

I live in an apartment that has Wifi that I can't turn off completely The wifi band doesn't make it into my room all the time (1-2 bands) but sometimes I see neighbor wifi's. I try to get it off. Flux/orange glasses on pc. Block blue light indoors all day and have minute 1-2 minutes exposure max. Apartment is red and UV bulbs. Eat withing 30 minutes of waking. I Eat usually red meat, now incorporating more fish, and I make a shake with whey protein collagen, differnet things and coconut milk bee pollen etc etc some raw eggs. Also some raw unpasteurized honey. I finish eating by 6:30-7pm. I have 3 meals.

I take 2 showers a day and end every single one with cold water no less than one minute. Getting used to the cold is hard. I am usually in bed around 9 or 10 and up at 5:30-7:00. I ionic foot detox 5 days a week, and infrared sauna 4x a week.

The only supplements I take (which actually ehlp and make a difference) Hexaniacin 2,360mg a day, licorice extract with ashwaganda rehmannia and chinese yam, and a fish oil (Polar Power) which is cold-pressed whole sockeye salmon with the brains whch yield vitamins A,D, Omega 3, 6, 9. I Recently got the X3 Bar to do outdoor training. I have been getting sunlight but it is hard in Pittsburgh.

### Lahelada — 2018-03-18T14:57:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-hoepers-journal-looking-for-all-inputs.21059/post-237405>

Welcome doc!
You are managing the light you have pretty well already!
The light you do not have is sun. Could you consider a trip to the beach? Playa del Carmen?

From what you say about needing coffee and the BUN/Creatinine you are very possibly dehydrated. Are you drinking low fluoride water?
Have you been following the story about deuterium and deuterium depleted water here? There may be mileage in that for you. We have also talked about here that supplements may contain a lot of deuterium so they may after all not be your best friend. You might want to look into the tests offered by Cignaturehealth to get an idea what your body's deuterium levels are.

One thing that will help immensely is to do a history of the maternal side of your family. What was your mother and grandmother like,what did they suffer from if anything ?

Finally, as you said your only 29... You can lick this !

### Lahelada — 2018-03-18T15:05:59+0200

PS : I forgot. It seems that cold showers tax you and understandably so. Start with facedunks in very cold water 50-55 F . it might seem little but has surprising effects.

[https://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-easy-start-guide/](https://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-easy-start-guide/) to get you going, but really read the whole CT series when you can.

### WalterNL — 2018-03-19T09:10:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-hoepers-journal-looking-for-all-inputs.21059/post-237498>

Your history sounds very comparable to mine. Just keep incorporating more and more of the protocols. Change doesn't happen overnight. In my experience it's incremental, and every day adds up in either the right way or wrong way. Eventually you will have built a solid base and your life turns into the right direction naturally because every decision gets slightly better.

I'd drop all supplements and focus only on sun, water & magnetism. Epi-paleo helps, but at the least make all your food unprocessed. Supplements have a way of messing everything up by disturbing all the intricate feedback loops of our biology. And most of them are ultra-processed and high in deuterium. I still keep some mitochondrial optimizers at hand, but find I don't bother with them unless I go down the wrong way again. Of course this is my n=1.

### shannoncorrin — 2018-03-18T03:23:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-for-beginners.21060/post-237389>

I didn’t grow up eating seafood and I’m not fond of it. I think I can learn to tolerate salmon and maybe shrimp but the thought of oysters just doesn’t sit well. I also have no clue as to how to prepare it. What sea food is easy to tolerate and easy to prepare? Are there any good resources out there?

### Brent Patrick — 2018-03-18T03:44:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-for-beginners.21060/post-237390>

I too hated seafood before coming across Jacks work ,now I mainly eat salmon I usually have with chilli for lunch and I eat a dozen Raw Oysters 4 times per week I have them with cracked pepper and fresh squeezed lemon for Breakfast also with a cup of hot Beef Bone Broth.

Actually knowing the **WHY** regarding seafood consumption I look forward to the above knowing I am ticking the boxes and the results have been better cognitive function more energy deeper sleep to name a few.
I can only suggest that mindset plays a big part ,knowing how important DHA is I was able to overcome my negative bias towards seafood.

### Jenny S — 2018-03-18T05:12:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-for-beginners.21060/post-237392>

I used to only eat whiting that my Dad caught or any other white flesh fish & tuna. Now eat sardines, mackerel, salmon & oysters. Don't really like oysters, but try & disguise them with tamari & bit of olive oil. The red salmon has the least amount of DHA so mostly eat mackerel & sardines - both canned.

### drezy — 2018-03-21T21:58:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-for-beginners.21060/post-237683>

> [shannoncorrin said:](/goto/post?id=237680)
>
> What do you do with the canned seafood? Mix it with anything? Heat it? Put on a salad?

The more "traditional" stuff you mix in the more deuterium you'll get and the more processing the more deuterium you get. The raw oysters start to make sense in this respect.

The best I've gotten my own mom is slow cooked pulled pork (on a bun \* facepalm) and with coleslaw (OK I'm willing to negotiate). With seafood I can lightly cook oysters or fish and sprinkle salt, turmeric, and pepper and she'll eat it. She won't MAKE it for herself though \*facepalm#2.

FWIW if it was lunch time and you handed me some canned seafood I'd reach for my ever-present turmeric shaker and black pepper for various reasons. All that extra stuff you've been raised to believe that you need is just a belief.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-03-21T22:13:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-for-beginners.21060/post-237686>

The Leptin Rx just gets more right every day!
"Before now researchers believed that land mammals played a bigger role in the diets of Stone Age people.
'At the Norje Sunnansund settlement outside Sölvesborg in Sweden you can see that just over half of the protein intake has come from fish, 10 percent from seals, and around 37 percent from land mammals - such as wild boar and red deer - and scarcely three percent from plants such as mushrooms, berries and nuts......they only got 3% from plants." Imagine that. ### Brent Patrick — 2018-03-22T07:56:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-for-beginners.21060/post-237723>

> [Jan Christer said:](/goto/post?id=237712)
>
> Larger fish like tuna tend to have more mercury:
>
> [http://www.sarahwilson.com/2012/07/...rdines-and-how-to-avoid-mercury-in-your-fish/](http://www.sarahwilson.com/2012/07/why-you-should-eat-sardines-and-how-to-avoid-mercury-in-your-fish/)

Mercury is over hyped in the natural health community, the natural antidote is selenium which is high in seafood

**"The selenium compound selenide binds mercury by forming mercury selenide, which neutralizes the harmful effect of mercury. However, once that bond is made, selenium is no longer available to react with the selenoproteins that depend on it."**

[http://healthandscience.eu/index.ph...you-get-enough-us&catid=20&Itemid=374&lang=us](http://healthandscience.eu/index.php?option=com_content&view=article&id=770:selenium-protects-against-mercury-provided-you-get-enough-us&catid=20&Itemid=374&lang=us)

and this,,,

[https://www.medicinenet.com/script/main/art.asp?articlekey=186426](https://www.medicinenet.com/script/main/art.asp?articlekey=186426)

**"The theory is that mercury exposure confers toxicity because it induces oxidation in the [human body](https://www.medicinenet.com/human_body_quiz/quiz.htm), which often results in inflammation," van Wijngaarden said. "These omega 3s are more anti-inflammatory. The idea would be that they would reduce the level of inflammation in the mother, softening any effect that mercury might have on the unborn child."
**
Seafood is a complete biological package ,fish oil/ algae oil will never come close to what marine foods have to offer.

### Brent Patrick — 2018-03-23T09:20:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-for-beginners.21060/post-237812>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=237400)
>
> The only membrane in humans without DHA is the inner mitochondrial membrane. There is biophysical a reason for this.

Ok Jack I have had an itch in my pants the past four days ,so I went back through the blogs most notable TENSEGRITY 7 & 8 and I believe I may have found the answer to your statement above.

The reason there is no DHA in the inner mitochondrial membrane is because Molybdenum replaces DHA in the inner mitochondrial membrane it allows for Electron tunneling to occur ,tunneling happens only below 14 Angstroms.

Molybdenum was key in our oceans before Oxygen showed up, DHA could not be made without Oxygen. Molybdenum was the foundation element for DHA construction in the marine chain and has not changed in 600 million years to present day.

Molybdenum also functions as a cofactor for four enzymes in our mitochondria.

**"To get to DHA in the marine food chains, we needed a _bridge loan_ from molybdenum in our past**. This relationship remains cemented in our mitochondria today, that use 4 key enzymes that require molybdenum as a cofactor. In addition, the unique chemistry of molybdenum enabled the development of other enzymes in organisms allowing for the assimilation and reduction of the toxic nitrate ion by electrons. **Even today, in all eukaryotic life forms, Molybdenum enzymes are within their mitochondrial membranes. Without them mitochondria would not be able to depolarize correctly**. It would be wise to remember that your mitochondria came from a prokaryote during endosymbiosis, at this juncture in the blog. **Molybdenum is the key to handling high nitrogen and sulfate biochemistries in the circulatory system.** This should make you realize why the recent [TENSEGRITY 7](https://www.jackkruse.com/tensegrity-7-black-hole-sun-come/) blog is now more important in this evolutionary story as it continues to unfold.
**Eukaryotes need sulfates to make glucose and oxygen transport safe in blood plasma during summer and spring**. Conversely, **we use nitrogen based transport for the safe transport of glucose and oxygen in autumn and winter. Molybdenum is key in making the biochemistry of glucose and oxygen transport safe in modern eukaryotes, like humans**. This links us back to our oceanic evolutionary past. **It is a remnant of how we handled sulfur and nitrogen before to innovate solutions in our past. "JK**

# Thread: Making DDW...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-ddw.21063/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Andrea — 2018-03-18T15:29:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-ddw.21063/post-237409>

I have been asked privately about making deuterium depleted water (DDW), and to stop wasting time I thought I could as well post my thoughts here.

Light water (H2O) and heavy water (D2O) (there is also semi-heavy water, HDO, but for convenience I will stick to D2O in this post) have similar properties. At atmospheric pressure the boiling points at 100°C (211F) and 101.4°C (214.5F) respectively and the freezing points are at 0°C (32F) and 3.8°C (38.9F) respectively. The difference in these points may seem possible to be used to separate them, at least to some extent. However, the very close boiling points are challenging and the difficulty to control phase change makes separation through freezing and/or thawing all but straightforward.

I have played with another thought: The density of both light and heavy water varies with temperature. At atmospheric pressure, light water has its maximal density at around 4°C (39.2F). Heavy water at around 11.4°C (52.5F) (note: I have found values between 11.2°C and 11.6°C, and have chosen the average 11.4°C in this post for convenience, but the number may be 0.2°C or so off). This difference, the significantly different densities (<1 and 1.106 kg/m3 respectively), and dealing with one phase only ought to make it feasible to decrease the amount of heavy water in the upper part of the body of water and increase it in the bottom part.
You could have a large container of water, cool the entire body of water to 11.4°C and maintain that temperature, and let it rest for a while, so that the heavy water settles at the bottom. Through a hose, inserted from above, suck the top section of the water out into bottles or other containers for use where DDW is called upon. This could be done easily at home.

My hypothesis sounds too simple to be true. And it probably is.
One likely reason is that the amount of heavy water in normal water is infinitesimally small, and heavy water molecules don’t have a tendency to aggregate in clusters. So when you cool the entire body of water to 11.4°C, will one tiny molecule of D2O among thousands of H2O’s really drop down to the bottom? Nah, most probably it will not.

For the same reason the distillation approach will not work. When heating water in a pot on the stove, steam can be observed already at temperatures around 50°C, even though boiling doesn’t occur until 100°C. How do you convince that one tiny molecule of D2O among thousands of H2O’s, that are evaporating, to remain in liquid phase and to stay in the bulk of the water rather than joining the H2O’s in the wet steam?

Regarding freezing water... When you put your container of water in the freezer, where the temperature is around -20°C, how do you convince that one tiny molecule of D2O among thousands of H2O’s to freeze first and aggregate with others to make ice that you can remove, rather than freezing H2O’s that are closer to a colder surface or impurities? And as for the thawing method, when letting a block of ice melt a bit and throwing away the melted water, how do you make those relatively few frozen D2O molecules inside the block if ice move to the surface so that they can melt first?

Maybe there is a reason why heavy water is expensive, and production of it includes addition of substances such as NaOH, H2S...?

But could there be another, relatively simple, method to change the concentration of heavy water? I believe so. Today there are possibilities...

How do you convince that one tiny molecule of D2O among thousands of H2O’s, that are evaporating, to remain in liquid phase and to stay in the bulk of the water rather than joining the H2O’s in the wet steam?"

### WalterNL — 2018-03-19T09:17:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-ddw.21063/post-237499>

Separating hydrogen isotopes is simply not an easy process, just look at the plants that some countries use to make heavy water (light water will be a byproduct). I know it sounds very attractive to have your own DDW production, and if it were that easy, competition to Preventa and Qlarivia would have sprouted up all over the place. It's not like there is a conspiracy to keep DDW prices high. You can look over all their patents and design a better system if you'd like. Most people don't bother, because it's expensive.

The easiest way would be to live high in the mountains and/or high up north to get DDW for free. Then again, who knows what type of tech will be invented in the future...

### Andrea — 2018-03-19T15:01:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-ddw.21063/post-237519>

Where does the value 125 ppm come from? Please give me a reference to an unbiased study. And what do you mean by "chance at helping people who are otherwise healthy"?

And how do you measure the rotational speed in any specific ATPase in a specific tissue at any point? (Please don't say through a breath test, because that really doesn't make any sense.)

Dr. K. used to say that you don't need to teach a lion or a hippo how to eat. I would assume that goes for drinking too. Why should a healthy animal go buy processed, unnatural water to drink?

Personally, I want my natural deuterium! Deuterium has been around since the beginning of times and living organisms ought to have learnt how to deal with it by now. It has taken nature / evolution millions and millions of years of assembling atoms and recycling them to create what is Andrea today. I wouldn’t be I without my deuterium. I may not be perfect, but I am quite happy with what I am (despite the Dunning-Kruger warning sign).

### Andrea — 2018-03-19T15:07:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-ddw.21063/post-237521>

One thing that I have learnt from dr. K. is that substances act as double edged swords. The same key can open the door to heaven and the gates of hell. Context matters. And I find it hard to believe that a living organism would take or make substances in order to damage or kill itself. Even cancer is, in a way, an attempt of survival rather than suicide.

Cholesterol is often accused of causing cardiovascular disease and should be kept low, but then again, cholesterol is needed to repair cell damage, and also to make vitamin D. Aggregation of amyloid-beta in the brain is said to be the cause of Alzheimer’s disease, but it has been shown that it is antimicrobial and protective. And so on. Fighting either of these substances is like shooting the firefighters that try to save your home from burning down - because firefighters always turn up where there is a fire.

Why should deuterium be any different? DDW has been shown to be inhibitory for tumors, but it has also been shown that deuterium can prevent lipid peroxidation and cell death caused by misfolding and aggregation of a-Syn in Parkinson’s disease. And there is more. It is just not on the menu in this restaurant.

I have yet to see studies that show that nnEMF or other environmental factors make humans aggregate deuterium in their tissues, but if that indeed is the case, then perhaps it is not a way to commit suicide, but rather a protective mode?

# Thread: Nathan Australia's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathan-australias-optimal-journal.21066/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Duck — 2018-03-19T12:22:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathan-australias-optimal-journal.21066/post-237505>

My name is Nathan and I have always lived in Melbourne, Australia. Upon a private consultation with Jack in July 2017, I moved to Cairns in January 2018.

A little background into my Health and Journey:

**January 1992**: I was diagnosed with Bilateral Retinoblastoma, as a baby, at 8 months of age. I received Radiotherapy treatment for this. I have approx... 10% patchy camera vision in both eyes with badly scared retinas. I am classified as Legally Blind.

**2004 – 2006:** I underwent 2 Separate Operations on my teeth due to crowded mouth, removing 8 adult teeth around the back of the mouth.

**December 2011:** By the time I was 19 I had developed quite dense Cataracts in both eyes. In 2011, I underwent Cataract Surgery in my left eye only and they placed an untinted Perspex Hoya Intraocular lens implant. I still have my cataract in my right eye.

**December 2015**: I was diagnosed with Testicular Cancer and an enlarged lymph node in my retroperitoneal space. I underwent a single orchiectomy and 5 rounds of BEP Chemotherapy.

After this experience in 2015/2016 I realised my mitochondria were trashed and I quit my job at The ANZ Bank, 12 Floors up in the air, in a transition metal tower in the middle of Downtown Melbourne and began living a more natural life totally devoted to my health, ‘Pedal to the Metal’. I became a Blue Blocking Maverick, turned my bedroom into a faraday cage, ate Epi-Paleo, undertook months of CT during the Melbourne winter and have seen nearly every sunrise since.

Upon a consultation with Jack in July 2017, he strongly suggested I move up north to the Australian Tropics. I began packing and moved up to Cairns permanently 6 months later. I now drink Qlarivia (DDW) water mixed with Evian Spring Water or Voss Water and eat a great deal of seafood, coconuts, eggs and pork in addition to all I have mentioned above.

I proudly call myself a Jack Kruse Star Fish as I have already helped and will continue to help my family, friends and colleagues in my community to make big shifts in their lives towards slowing down their Heteroplasmy.

**Goals:** Although, I am far from out of the woods, it is my goal to heal my Scared Retinas with the sun and restore my RPE. I aim to heal my abdominal scaring from my orchiectomy to allow the electrical signalling to work over that area again and energise my organs again to make them stronger than ever to remedy all the damage 5 rounds of BEP Chemotherapy did. In Jack’s 2017 Vermont talk, when he mentioned how the Intraocular lens implant blocks UV frequencies reaching the RPE, I was heartbroken, but I still believe that despite this I can find a way to heal my RPE.

I am excited to share my journey with such an amazing group of individuals and welcome any comments or suggestions from Jack and or any member. I am an extremely motivated, passionate young man and take everything in my stride, so don’t hold back if there’s anything that comes to mind.

**Paternal Haplogroup:**

R-Z278

**Maternal Haplotype:**

J1

**Blood Type:**

O+

I'm also interested to hear what health improvements you've seen so far?

> I am excited to share my journey with such an amazing group of individuals and welcome any comments or suggestions from Jack and or any member. I am an extremely motivated, passionate young man and take everything in my stride, so don’t hold back if there’s anything that comes to mind.
>
>
> **Paternal Haplogroup:**
>
> R-Z278
>
> **Maternal Haplotype:**
>
> J1
>
> **Blood Type:**
>
> O+

### Alper — 2018-03-20T18:29:08+0200

### Jude — 2018-03-21T00:53:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathan-australias-optimal-journal.21066/post-237636>

Welcome and hats off to your dedication, well done You are a great inspiration for us all!

### Duck — 2018-04-06T06:38:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathan-australias-optimal-journal.21066/post-238820>

> [Earth Monkey said:](/goto/post?id=237570)
>
> Hi Nathan, Respect for the total life turnaround!
> I spent a month in Cairns last winter, was thinking about doing it again and just wondering where you get your seafood and pork from there?
> I'm also interested to hear what health improvements you've seen so far?

Seafood:
Ocean World Seafood Market - 379 Sheridan St, Cairns North
Captain Cook Seafoods - 351 Sheridan St, Cairns North
These two usually have all the best and freshest seafood in Cairns that I have found. Oysters are a little pricy, $25 per dozen, but the rest is affordable. Although this doesn't stop me from buying them every time I go for a swim at the pool accross the road from these 2 places!

Pork:
Edge Hill Butchery - 136 Collins Ave, Edge Hill
They source most of their meat from Tasmania. They also have a large selection of Grass Fed meats.

Health Improvements:
1\. I'm eating much less than I was living in Melbourne. Most days seafood omlette for breakfast and Fish/Pork/Lamb/Beef with veggies or bone broth for dinner. I don't feel the need to snak besides the occasional local coconut, because I can get away with it up here.
2\. More toned muscle mass and lean physique
3\. Better quality sun absorbtion: The sun feels more nurrishing here too, rather than a 'lazer beam' down south, it's a much slower and nicer cok throughout the day.
these were jsut the first 3 that came to mind, but I'll be doing labs at my 6 month mark and report back with scientific results then.

My Biohack is:
For the first 3 months I'm using 165ml of Qlarivia and 835ml of water. So I only use 1 x 500ml bottle per day.
For the second 3 months I'll use 300ml of Qlarivia and 700ml of water. Then compare the difference and see which one is Optimal for me.

This was my hypothesis based on my situation. If you are curring it differently, I'd be interested to hear what is Optimal for you.

# Thread: Help to understand why
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-to-understand-why.21067/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### healthy2015 — 2018-03-19T14:04:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-to-understand-why.21067/post-237513>

Hi Everyone

I wonder if anyone can help shed anylight of what is causing so many problems for my family and pets at our detached home in the UK which is nowhere near an airport or power lines or cell phone tower.

We have recently lost 2 of our beloved pets to bone cancer ages 11 years old each this despite the fact they would spend a minimum of 6+ hours outside virtually everyday plus longer during the spring/summer months as I am at home everday.

We changed there diet to consist of mussels (they didnt like oysters) tinned sardines and mackerel, tumeric, black pepper, 1 raw egg egg plus various meats and fat and only fed them once a day to try to drive down hetroplasmy.

We consistently check the garden, house and sleeping areas with the tri meter and coronet meter and the readings have come back as what is considered the 'norm' these days from a respected forum member. No wifi is used and mobiles are swithched off no later than 6pm and not turned on until at least 7.30am the next morning and are at least 5metes+ minimum away from them at all times. They drink evian water only. Candles only after sunset. Breakers swithched off to the bedroom also.

We also used the sperti on each of them for 10 minutes most days to top up the poor uv enviroment we live in.
Plus have a uva bulb on every day.

All of our dogs have had arthritis and other issues and i admit for the first half of their lives we had phones by our beds and on all night near them, tv on late , walked early with streetlights on before sunrise before we found the jack kruse forum. Would the damage done to our poor pets in the early years mean that despite the massive changes we implemented for the second half of their lifes, we could not undo the horrible damage already done???? And if so why did the cancer only develop so late?????

Im trying to understand how we've got it so wrong????????

My partner is also suffering from mutiple health issues (hospitalised with a liver infection this year, damaged liver from taking antibiotics, low T) but we have planned to move to the canary islands later this year but for our poor pets it is already to late and im so distraught i couldnt do more for them and need to understand where i got things so so wrong

I would really apprecate if anyone can help

Many thanks, Emma

### Jack Kruse — 2018-03-19T17:17:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-to-understand-why.21067/post-237536>

I think it is simple.......you don't need to understand with these results you need to act.

I am at a loss to understand why this is so hard to comprehend.

### healthy2015 — 2018-03-19T20:00:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-to-understand-why.21067/post-237545>

Move @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)???????

We are going to move this year as stated in my post above so i completly comprehend what needs to be done.....???????

Im trying to understand why this has happened to my family and pets despite all the changes
implemented yet those around us who live like shit seem to do just fine and dandy???????

On another note there is a small lifeboat launch station approx half a mile away that isnt
used that often but is still used could this be it???????????????

Or as i have already asked the damage done in earlier years and living in the uk made it impossible to turn things around??????????

### Mayuri — 2018-03-19T23:01:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-to-understand-why.21067/post-237556>

The other issue is the water in the U.K.
The male fish in rivers and streams have been turning female for _decades._
They do try to clean the water with the implementation of processes like 13 times filtering the drinking water but they cannot get the estrogen out of the water.
Cancer rates are so, so very high there.
This article is from 2009 and it's only gotten worse.
[https://www.theguardian.com/environment/2012/jun/02/water-system-toxic-contraceptive-pill](https://www.theguardian.com/environment/2012/jun/02/water-system-toxic-contraceptive-pill)
Alternatively, dependent on where you are located in the U.K. we've got this other problem.
Article dated 2003
[https://www.theguardian.com/uk/2003/nov/30/greenpolitics.health](https://www.theguardian.com/uk/2003/nov/30/greenpolitics.health)

Last thing left is low test and libido which I have been agressivley Cting to offset symptoms. 3 weeks ago one of my more peculiar symptoms popped back up. ### Jack Kruse — 2018-04-11T00:22:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/celtic-sea-salt-vs-redmond-real-salt.21073/post-239183>

Salt is going to be important in a 5G world and not too many people know why. The way sea salt is produced in the Guerande and the Isle de Re in Brittany, France is a wonderful thing to watch. Man and nature in harmony. Nothing but wind and sun are used to evaporate the sea water. Nothing but wood or occasionally plastic tools are used as metal reacts with the salt. This is a totally sustainable way of making a living - how fantastic! This guy is so lucky to work in this environment because as nature gives him the bounty he is also getting a wealth of health as he does it. Even the growth and harvesting of salt follows circadian rules in sunlight. [**https://www.youtube.com/watch?time\_continue=330&v=N0bwVun02bM**](https://www.youtube.com/watch?time_continue=330&v=N0bwVun02bM)

**French sea salt from the renown Guérande region of France helps sleep in a big way.** [**https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/1258120367585648**](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/1258120367585648)

### Tfreeman — 2020-05-22T23:11:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/celtic-sea-salt-vs-redmond-real-salt.21073/post-289751>

Question about salt: As someone with POTS and autonomic dysfunction, I strive to get at least 5G a day of iodized sea salt. I use Hainz brand. I usually put it in 4 ounces of water and chug it before my hikes. Is this wrong? I can sprinkle it on food but I can't put that much in one meal, so it'd be hard to measure. I'm not drinking in my drinking water all day, just in a few ounces, but I could still be going about it wrong. Thoughts? I'm seeing in Jack's redox RX:

"Avoid any exogenous salts in your drinking water if you can. This is not an axiomatic rule. Why? **Salts.** In addition to metals, salts can also conduct electricity. In cells we do not want a salt disturbing semiconducting flow of electrons or protons. This creates loss of efficiency for energy transfers because it creates a _smaller exclusion zone_ in water in a cell. In salt electric conduction, there are no free electrons moving, so the conductivity depends on the specific ions used in the salt. When a salt is in water, it is either melting or dissolving, so that the ions of the salt are free to move to conduct the current. Their atomic sizes however limit coherent conduction with 100{a7b724a0454d92c70890dedf5ec22a026af4df067c7b55aa6009b4d34d5da3c6} efficiency that is seen in a semiconductor. K+ is the key element in life’s equation with water to create a semiconductor that has the same properties we see in a topologic insulators."

# Thread: Yummy Uyghur skewers
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yummy-uyghur-skewers.21076/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### drezy — 2018-03-22T20:46:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yummy-uyghur-skewers.21076/post-237764>

Every spring I start remembering eating the interesting [Uyghur](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Uyghurs) ethnic food when I was in China's capitol and it was yummy. Since it was being served in the Capitol it was one ethnic group's take on another ethnic group's food.

I whipped up some of these on a trial run and will be making them for dinner with the below edits. You may want to tone down the red pepper flakes to whatever your spicy sensibility level:

[https://www.seriouseats.com/recipes/2015/05/spicy-lamb-skewers-chinese-street-food-recipe.html](https://www.seriouseats.com/recipes/2015/05/spicy-lamb-skewers-chinese-street-food-recipe.html)

Edits to the recipe:
Fatty Pork shoulder substituted for Lamb (it fits right in)

Added 1 t powdered Fenugreek to dry rub
Added 2 t turmeric to dry rub

Social implications:
I have this [cheap tabletop grill](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B06XSJ7C7P/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o02_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1) we all sit and talk and tend to our own skewers. I like this so much more than having one cook running around like an idiot(aka me usually). There is something about having a little fire near you and a swirl of cooking food smells while we all wait that seems to encourage more discussion and just a plain old happier family.

### caroline — 2018-03-25T05:25:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yummy-uyghur-skewers.21076/post-237918>

Looks so good - thanks Dan.

You sit this table top grill on your table? seems like a stupid question I know ..........but I would worry about harming our table......

### drezy — 2018-03-25T16:02:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yummy-uyghur-skewers.21076/post-237930>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=237918)
>
> Looks so good - thanks Dan.
>
> You sit this table top grill on your table? seems like a stupid question I know ..........but I would worry about harming our table......

FWIW I think the recipe mentioned by @[John Saf](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20790/) is more optimal in cooking technique:

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/#post-237367](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/#post-237367)

It's just once in a while in spring I just like to reproduce one of the best street foods I ever had.

The grill I have sits 5"(12+cm) above the table and I placed a small oven/smoker rack wrapped in foil right under the grill. It's seems to reflect the heat away from the table well. If it ever proves insufficient I'll get some metal plating. There is something nice about cooking together in the evening with a little hot infrared bathing everyones face as they cook. I can feel any tension in my own face get totally relaxed after a few minutes.

Maybe when putting my best face forward to my family it's a infrared heated face that's best...
