### Jenelle — 2019-09-25T23:57:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-278904>

An act of kindness ~ just doing something out of the goodness of your heart ~ can seem like such a simple thing to you ... you may go on about your day, and not even think about it ever again.

I'm here today to tell you this:

Reaching out to someone in their time of need -- _no matter how trivial or superficial that need may seem to you_ _at the time_ \-- **can** be a life-changing moment for that person ... reaching far greater, having much larger and longer-lasting effects than you could have ever imagined.

I was reminded today of this video, which was part of my first online class with WGU. The things that were shared and that resonated with me in that _very first class_ made the cost of this entire degree worth it to me.

I like the version at this link WAY better than youtube ... the youtube version seems to be sped up for some reason, and it doesn't give you time to really let his story sink in.

[https://hls.ted.com/talks/1355.m3u8](https://hls.ted.com/talks/1355.m3u8)

### Jenelle — 2019-09-26T02:43:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-278915>

-   **We’re looking for both experienced tree fellers and those wanting to learn the trade of felling trees around Utility Right-of-Way transmission lines.**
-   We have steady, year-round career opportunities serving our utility clients throughout the Midwest (MI, IN, OH) and Southeast (WV, VA) regions of the U.S.
-   Learn how to safely climb a tree and operate heavy equipment. We own some of the most high-mechanized equipment in the industry and we want to teach you how to operate this cool equipment!
-   For those of you who want to see the country, we’ll pay you per diem to travel with us!

\-----

I'm gonna say I just _confused the shit out of indeed_ by switching from searching for RN jobs to looking at jobs like this one.

### Jenelle — 2019-09-26T18:28:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-278950>

I'm supposed to be writing an APA paper for school. This has been weighing on me for a couple of weeks now.

It's a Community Health class. We spend a bunch of time "visiting" a simulation city and gathering details. Then we're supposed to write this huge paper about all of their problems, zero in on one problem, and come up with a plan to fix it.

I want to write. **I love to write!** But I don't want to write this stupid fucking paper about this stupid pretend city.

My mind is so many other places.

I signed up for Patreon again yesterday and read the relationship blogs.

I'm reading The Vital Question, a little bit at a time.

I want to be outside trimming the bushes along the back row of our property -- because if I don't get them down to an acceptable level, the power company is going to come _one of these days_ and remove all of them. There goes the little bit of privacy I have here in my backyard.

I keep thinking, _when I get this paper done, when I get this class done, when I get this degree done...
_
Bullshit. It's all bullshit at this point.

I'm now an RN, I have tremendous earning **potential** that I did not have before, and I chose a specialty that is absolutely _dying_ for RNs.

But I want to climb trees.

I fucking love to use my chainsaw.

I want to push-mow yards.

And I want to shovel snow. Since (for now) I'm choosing to be stuck in a place where we do have to deal with snow.

What it all boils down to is: I want my time back. Every "free" moment right now is not really free ... and I hate that.

Rant over. I'm gonna go work on this stupid fucking paper.

I FEELS YA......

You value your time... sometimes, we have to Spend Time Now to Get Time Later. It is a trade-off.

Your Earning Potential is huge. Well done! Seriously, fuck what anyone says, Finances are MAJOR. Because Money can buy you so much freedom. Money gives you power and control. I love what it can do, and I love having it. _But, the Time used to get it can never be refunded..._

Just make sure, you 100%, believe in that trade-off. Or else, no matter the result you will feel short-changed....

### Jenelle — 2019-09-26T19:42:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-278960>

This must be what my 90-year-old grandmother meant when she said she “threw a chain” on her chainsaw.

You see, all I needed to do was type that post above “out loud” to realize how ridiculous it sounded.

This is my life. I make my own choices. I want to chainsaw, I’m gonna chainsaw ~ and yes, it was more important to me in that moment than anything else.

You see ... the school work is still there in the background, waiting for me.

But first — I satisfied a need that I can only describe as primal — to move, to work, to lift heavy things. Out in nature. I made a mess. I cleaned it up. I threw a chain. Now I have to do something else. Lol. I don’t know what to do with that.

Back to grandma. She’s now 92, and she is completely gone. It is horrifying. This woman who was my rock and the PICTURE of strength ... still here, still in “good health” ... but fucking GONE.

It is one of the most surreal experiences I have ever had to mourn someone while they are still alive. Yet, that is what I’m doing. It’s slow ... and it’s painful.

So ~ Grandma ~ I did some chainsawing today for you. You don’t remember telling me about that just a couple of short years ago ... but I sure do.

There have been so many great conversations here lately ... so many things swirling through my mind each day.

The relationship blogs ... I read #4 yesterday and man, it was good. I need to go through and read it again and take some notes. Lots of inspiration there.

I am married to my best friend. No doubt about that.

Thinking about the circle of 6 ... that one would take me longer. I’ve whittled down, weeded out the people I can’t relate to, the ones who suck energy or make me feel a negative response ... so for me, it would be more about filling up that circle. If that makes sense.

There were just a lot of things that resonated with me. So much wisdom and truth.

It takes a lot of confidence to be the one who stands out as “different” ...

There is an internal mission, a drive in me, that I do not see in most people at work.

I see defeat. Complacency. And laziness. To be quite honest.

I’m a leader, even though I may not officially hold the title of a manager. Leadership and management are two completely different concepts, anyway ... many do not distinguish between the two ~ but I do.

You can be a leader in any walk of life.

I have / have had “management” above me who are shitty leaders.

What [@Sean Waters](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19464/) said yesterday REALLY resonated with me ... why do I care? Why do I get fired up / care about the details? Because I am passionate about what I do.

YES. If it doesn’t spark passion in you ... or even worse, makes you feel dead inside ... wtf are you doing?

YES..... Love this shit. You are literally speaking my mind here.

That quote yesterday meant ALL ASPECTS of Life too, not just a job or a career... I think we should feel Lit Up/ Firey in every part of life... **THIS IS LIVING... Eat with Passion, Fuck with Passion, Make Friends with Passion, Travel with Passion, Read/ Watch, Learn, Work, Plan, Exercise, Farm, Clean, Shower, Journal...... everything feel like you do.**

I feel and see the same Living Cadavers as you do. They don't have a single ounce of Fire in their blood for anything they do. They plod along and don't know where the fuck they are. Smite them fuckers down and walk forward.

I don't like meditating. I don't like shutting down my Fire. I don't want to think like they do... plant eating idiots with no Libido and Macbooks telling me I need to look into a new podcast about mindfulness and finding inner peace... Get Fucked.

They don't know the first thing about being alive. It ain't about suppressing the state of chaos inside of us... it is about letting that run wild.

I don't see the Passion we have as an issue, or a need for us to slow down, I have thought about it a lot and no I don't think I want to spend time meditating this.

**I want to be on Fire** in my life, I don't want to entertain the opposite. No matter if it is too strong for others or potentially dangerous for me. Stay out of my way if it is too much for you.

The Sun, and Nature, and Animals... they are all Extreme. And they do not try to understand their desire to thrive and kill, they just do it.

**What's that hedge looking like Jenelle?....... I've heard it needs tearing apart with a chainsaw and some Imperial British aggression**

### Phosphene — 2019-10-02T15:44:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-279384>

Ok here you go! Not Toad, but I like what I’ve seen so far of this woman’s work so here is her interpretation of Frog.

My abs were already sore from class and you gave me a mini morning laugh workout through my journal. Thanks again!

[https://www.universeofsymbolism.com/frog-symbolism.html](https://www.universeofsymbolism.com/frog-symbolism.html)

\[edit\] Oh no!! Ignore her bit on ‘Frog Tatoo Ideas’.

Were the toads \*living in my uterus\*

OR

Was I \*birthing the toads\* ?

Hmm, an important distinction, I think.

While I would like to say I was the loving mother, smiling serenely and bringing toads into the world to go forth and do great things ...

It was more like: WTF, why are there toads coming out of my vagina ... I was horrified ... and doing a thorough check to make sure they were ALL out of there, after the last one came out. (You know, because I was looking up my vagina with a flashlight, in a way that we can’t actually do to ourselves without a speculum).

I did. Up until recently.

I _really_ didn't want to do Biochemistry ... I had to dig deep, and devote a lot of time to it. But I ended up LOVING IT and I was really glad I stuck with it.

These last 6 classes I had left ... nothing but papers. Papers, papers, papers ... much of it bullshit I don't even believe or remotely care about. It was getting to the point of downright painful.

Meanwhile, my kids are growing ... changing ... making plans for college, etc ... and here I am, with this GIANT monkey on my back, overshadowing everything. Who the fuck put this monkey here? And what purpose is he actually going to serve?

### Jenelle — 2019-10-05T15:41:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-279633>

**That Old Pair of Shoes
**
_(a story by Jenelle, that isn't really about shoes at all)

\---_

I have an old pair of shoes ...

They aren't really that old.

It was my first pair of shoes for my first nursing job.

I thought they were a great pair of shoes ... until my toes started hurting.

I bought a new pair of shoes ~ and WOW! Now, _those_ are a great pair of shoes.

I realized when I got these new shoes, that I don't even have to use insoles in them. My back doesn't hurt, no matter how long I'm on my feet. My toes never hurt. I do untie them when I get in my car ... loosen them up for the drive home. But all day long -- up until that point -- I don't even think about my shoes.

Sometimes, I still look at that old pair of shoes, and I think, "Man, those shoes are so cool! I wish I could still wear them."

Then I remember: There's a reason I stopped wearing that old pair of shoes. And I've tried those old shoes on since I bought my new shoes ... thought I could just wear them at home for a day, and see how they feel. NOPE. Those old shoes had to come right back off.

I can hold them in my hands, turn them over, and see the places where the bottoms are worn ... a sign of every hustle-bustle, stressed-out moment I spent, walking MILES and MILES around that building, at my first job. And I can smile ~ and love those shoes, for what they once were to me. I can keep them as a reminder of where I've been, and what I've done.

But then I will put them back down, and walk away. In my new shoes.

Sometimes I wonder when my new shoes will become my old shoes. What will the next pair look like? And I keep an eye out for them.

But for today ~ I'm really happy in my new shoes. And that is good enough for me.

### Jenelle — 2019-10-06T12:40:59+0300

Wishing you all the best in your new shoes.

My husband recently encouraged me to try out a new brand. He got some after hearing about them from a guy at work ~ and he loves them.

I tried his on, and quickly realized that \*while they are great for him\* ... they would not serve me and my purposes well. The tread, and the sole in general, are too thick.

So he wears his shoes. And I wear mine. And as long as we are both comfortable in our new shoes ~ it works.

### Jenelle — 2019-10-08T15:18:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-279829>

I don’t know ... I also took my family to a German restaurant/tavern, and that was a fun new experience. Their menu was a newspaper! The phones were (mostly) put away, and everybody did the word find activity inside. And we discussed the glossary of German terms. And now I can tell you what schnitzel means.

Of course ... after we shared the potato “pancake” appetizer, loaded with pieces of bacon and sour cream etc (which was new to all of us) ... the server laughed at us when we all ordered bunless burgers and fries.

There was room to dance right next to our table. I don’t know if you’d call it a dance floor ~ but I wanted to dance. No takers. Something tells me there are a few folks here who would have danced with me.

### Jenelle — 2019-10-08T15:40:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-279830>

Of course there was the immediate “not sure how that would work with my schedule” <— {not a cop-out ... his job changes every week, and HE HAS NO SCHEDULE.}

But this glorious — and sometimes frustrating — man who’d do just about anything for me said yes ~ he’d take salsa classes. Come on, now. Picture that guy doing salsa. That’s some real love right there!

### 5G Canary — 2019-10-09T00:14:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-279865>

You have a beautiful family Jenelle!

That’s funny I asked my husband about the Salsa dancing too. I also used to love the kitchen dancing with my girls. Now that I am so photosensitive... I love dancing in the moonlight. Outside with shoes off...just feels right!

I was reflecting on your shoe story... I miss the days of one pair of shoes. I unfortunately am a different size every hour and actually have three pairs of shoes depending on my inflammation level. Looking forward to having one pair again.... or maybe the universe just wants me to stay barefoot!

### Jenelle — 2019-10-09T15:16:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-279887>

Yesterday I spent some rare time marveling at the beauty of nature at an inside location.

I’ve always had such mixed feelings about zoos and aquariums. Yes, it’s cool that we get to see these all of these species that we would probably have no other way of seeing. And I LOVE learning about them. It honestly blows my mind ... and I felt like a 40-year-old woman with a “childlike curiosity” several times ... and I thought, when do people lose touch with that?

I didn’t see any other grown women spending extended amounts of time squatting down to stare at a tank in wonder. Maybe they were ... and I just didn’t see them.

But I don’t think so.

I thought of [@Phosphene](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19648/) and The Vagina Frogologues when I saw these poison dart frogs. If you had been next to me, I would have turned to you and said, “Oh shit, guess what I’ll be dreaming about tonight?!”  ...And we would have had a good, hearty abdominal workout together.

These male seahorses made me think of [@drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/) ~ our self-described “manny” ... and I thought about how many men there are out there who do not fully understand their importance as a father. Yes, your children need you to “provide” for them. They also NEED YOU. They need you to be present ... and they need you to fill some roles that \*for whatever reason\* the mother cannot.

And I thought of [@Sean Waters](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19464/) and [@5G Canary](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/22671/) while I danced in the pirate section to the blasting Yellow Submarine song ... with my husband ... who said, “did they start selling wine here and I didn’t know it?”

Nope. I haven’t had a drop of alcohol this entire time ~ and I don’t have any desire to. This is me high on life, dear. Get used to it.

There are no pictures of that moment. Some pictures can only be captured and stored in our minds. And some of the best moments would be disrupted by stopping to get a phone out to take a picture.

Don’t tell big pharma.

### Jenelle — 2019-10-10T03:37:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-279940>

[@Phosphene](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19648/)

Oh, hell ~ I don’t care!

The only thing bothering me about this thread tonight is that I hope my male seahorse thoughts didn’t come out wrong.

I had a dad who worked all the wrong shifts, and lots of overtime. And I married a man who works for the railroad.

I thought to myself while watching those seahorses ... how fucking cool it is that we can see the importance of the father’s presence and involvement, even in some other species.

I stared at the penguins for a long time, too. The kids and I talked about how the male will stand still and hold the egg on his feet while the female will go out to get food.

We also learned that penguins poop every 5-10 minutes ... and that they can and DO shoot their poop at each other when angry, up to a distance of 4 feet. That has nothing to do with my concern that [@drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/) might have taken my comment the wrong way. But it’s still an interesting fact worth sharing.

Don’t piss off a penguin, folks. Just don’t do it!

### Jenelle — 2019-10-10T03:43:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-279941>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=279939)
>
> The bear's expression is great. It looks like a mix between "What?" along with "Wait, do you have any of that bacon stuff I steal from inexperienced campers?"

I don’t think I can share videos here ... but the video right after I took that picture tells so much more of the story.

You can hear the dog, barking at the bear, one kid yelling at the dog to stop barking, another kid telling my husband to throw Cheetos toward the bear, and see my husband waving Cheetos around in the air ... which he did NOT actually feed to the bear.

### caroline — 2019-10-10T10:57:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-279953>

This thread is turning remarkable ....

You are such a different person Jenelle than when you first arrived here...but that is what it is all about isn’t it...

### Jenelle — 2019-10-10T15:16:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-279975>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=279953)
>
> This thread is turning remarkable ....
>
> You are such a different person Jenelle than when you first arrived here...but that is what it is all about isn’t it...

Thank you, [@caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) ... and thanks to ALL who are here and have commented. I appreciate each and every one of you.

I was a very different person when I \*first\* came to this forum in 2016. I could elaborate on that ~ and maybe someday I will. But for now I’ll just say I’m very aware of it ... and the multitude of reasons.

I was aware of it when I returned and started this new journal. And I’ve grown and changed even more since then.

What those of us know, who have been here for years ... is that this group and what you will DISCOVER here is completely unique from any other website on the internet.

\*Anyone showing up who wants (or needs) to be spoon-fed\* is either going to feel:

A) like they see all of this food around them ... but they are starving to death! “Why won’t anybody FEED me?!”

or

B) like they are choking and drowning because the bites are coming in faster than they can chew.

I think there really isn’t a way to properly articulate the change that must occur INSIDE oneself ... it is not something that can be easily put into words. And the journey is long, and different for each of us.

I spent a lot of time on a nursing forum, while I wasn’t active here. Gathering information ... learning as much as I could about what it is like to be a nurse. I don’t go there very much anymore ~ because the overall vibe THAT I GET from the site is pretty negative. It’s not a “feel-good” kind of place {spoiler alert: most nurses DON’T feel good}.

One important thing I did take away from there, however ~ is this:

Get comfortable being uncomfortable.

### MITpowered26 — 2019-10-10T15:20:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-279976>

> [Jenelle said:](/goto/post?id=279975)
>
> Thank you, [@caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/)
>
> Get comfortable being uncomfortable.

.. and then go comfortably numb again by accident. and learn to get uncomfortable again.. and then back and forth .. again and again.. forever and ever. **YES.!**

### Jenelle — 2019-10-10T15:41:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-279977>

[@MITpowered26](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/23000/)

I want you to know that your presence here is changing lives ... even when you are not aware of it. You WILL NOT always see the results of your actions. Continue to be the best you you can be ... follow your heart ... and put yourself out there anyway.

The video you shared of Sinek (someone I had never heard of) resonated with me deeply.

I shared it with my oldest ... 18 ... a high school senior ... getting ready to spread his wings and jump out of the nest. Into the real world of which Sinek speaks.

I will be sharing it with my other two sons, 12 and 14. I haven’t had the right moment yet.

I shared it with my husband on the porch of our cabin two days ago.

He woke up yesterday, came and gave me a big morning hug ... and he said, “I greeted my spouse before I checked my phone.”

I shared the video because it was relevant to what we deal with on a daily basis with our kids. WHO KNEW that it was going to speak to HIM about himself, as well.

Thank you.

Thank you [@Jenelle](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/18778/) ..

This post just fucked me up... big time.

Can I get a bit emo again (like i love to do? haha) . I have to share something about me.. that is about YOU.

As you may know by now, I've uncovered that I have a gigantic fucking ego, and a voracious appetite for attention seeking that only pales in comparison to that of the stomach of a world champion of hot dog eating.

It's like each and every one of you is on my fucking hit list... that i'm sniping with my words. Check. Check. Check. A fucking laundry list.

I can even sense, (maybe my imagination).. that people hesitate, or perhaps are more carefull... to like, respond.. to my journal... knowing what they know of me. .. its like they are managing my ego or something.. I DONT KNOW.. but i take as CARE. And I love it. Could be total delusion but whatever, it helps me.. cope with life yknow?

Anways, back to you. You've intrigued me as someone on my list.. because up until now. I haven't received any "satisfactory" validation from you. And that really realy fucking bothered my fucking ego.. and then seeing you grow and getting love from others.. bothered my ego even more. It's shameful..but thats why I need to talk about it. It's been far too long I've quelled ugliness within me... and this helps me...heal.

So ya, theres that. haha.

Im' actually suprised anyone watched the Sinek vid. Its really long. But I love it.

Peace (and too much info). haha

### Christine_L — 2019-10-10T17:30:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-279986>

Jenelle I love the zoo and the little aquariums here and there. My mom always stops and looks. "Come look at this!" Doesnt care about how old she is or how old I am. She loves learning and \*seeing\*. Glad she's stubborn and made me look.

### Jenelle — 2019-10-11T03:00:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-280067>

> [Christine\_L said:](/goto/post?id=279986)
>
> Jenelle I love the zoo and the little aquariums here and there. My mom always stops and looks. "Come look at this!" Doesnt care about how old she is or how old I am. She loves learning and \*seeing\*. Glad she's stubborn and made me look.

My line of thought took me off in a different direction, and I never finished about zoos and aquariums.

I find the creatures fascinating ~ and I am glad to get to see them and learn about them. At the same time ... I feel sorry for them. I don’t care how much they try to recreate their natural habitat ~ it’s still not the same as being truly wild and free in nature.

I’m happy for you that you have a mom who shares a love of learning.

I saw toddlers in strollers with tablets and phones ... and that brings up too much for me to put into words right now.

### Jenelle — 2019-10-11T04:18:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-280071>

As this trip nears the end ~ and we return to reality ...

I’d like to remember the early morning walks we took. Mostly just me, my husband, and of course our beloved dog ~ Rocket.

Our cabin is partway up a mountain. So we walked to the top, all the way down to the bottom, and partway up to our cabin again.

It was rejuvenating. Refreshing. And completely free.

We saw wild turkeys. I got to see them fly. It was beautiful.

We saw plants, and berries, and pods I’ve never seen before.

Each day, we saw Persistence.

We saw this gorgeous ... whatever it is.

But what I saw, that was perhaps most beautiful to me - is that, with all of the other daily bullshit stripped away - we are still very much connected.

We didn’t talk about work, or the kids, like we do on our neighborhood walks at home.

We talked about our future. And it wasn’t just dreaming. The foundation is being laid for a plan that can one day be executed.

Until then ... I will remember the importance of traveling. Seeing the world. Taking a break from it all. And getting back in touch with who we really are. Together.

I fucking love this place.

[@MITpowered26](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/23000/)

There is a lot to digest in this post.

I know it bothers you that I did not respond to it right away. You put yourself out there ... you look to get something back. You look. You look again. You’re like, what the fuck?! What is it with this woman?!

I’m not laughing at you. I’m letting you know that I understand how it made you feel.

BUT.

Sometimes, I will choose to stay silent ~ rather than rush a response.

I care about people ... and I think about things. More deeply than you know.

The short answer to your post is that it’s not about ME at all.

The long answer is still coming. If you actually want it.

[\[URL="https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/avoiding-alan-impossible.24351/#post-280304"\]From this conversation](<http://[URL="https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/avoiding-alan-impossible.24351/#post-280304"]Avoiding ALAN impossible[/URL]>)\[/URL\]

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=280304)
>
> There is a fine line between challenging yourself and overwhelming yourself. Changing paradigms is not for the weak of heart or mind. This type of transformation takes hold of you. Invades you. Soon it owns you. You want to be free of them, but you never will be. A very slow movement on the right way is better than overwhelming speed on the wrong path and this is why you enjoy the chaos of being overwhelmed. You must have the change you seek

_**Changing paradigms is not for the weak of heart or mind.**
_
\---

There are some things in life that you cannot un-see.

Once they have been revealed to you ... there is no choice but to move forward with this new knowledge. It's now a part of you. You carry it with you everywhere you go ~ and on some level, it affects everything you do.

Is it inconvenient?

Hell, yes, it can be.

But would you have it any other way?

### Jenelle — 2019-10-16T02:19:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-280523>

After up-and-quitting my BSN program in the middle of a class... and with only 6 classes left...

I have now applied for a part-time job I discovered ~ close to home, with GREAT flexibility in hours, **the ability to work from home sometimes**, and be here when my kids get on the bus (AKA not having to get up at 4:00 in the morning), and be here when they get home from school.

It has NOTHING to do with nursing whatsoever -- and it's making HALF the money I'm making now.

And I'm pretty fucking excited about this new opportunity. Jacked!

I think my family may think I've completely lost it ... so I had to share my excitement where people will actually understand.

(No, I"m not going to quit being a nurse. I just realized my closet is actually pretty full of hats.)

### Jenelle — 2019-10-16T02:35:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-280527>

>
> I get it bro.

I knew you would.  Thanks, man!

I didn’t mean to just say I applied. I’ve gone a lot further in the process. Talked to this lady for an hour on my way home today. In-person restaurant meeting on Thursday.

The best part is that — no matter the outcome — I’m enjoying the process. If it’s a good fit, it will work out. If not, still a good experience.

> [Jenelle said:](/goto/post?id=280659)
>
> They are behind my house right now with their chainsaws & trucks. My privacy is gone. Good thing I’m leaving for the day. More thoughts on this later ...

False alarm. They ARE in our neighborhood ~ and it's coming. They were right on the other side of the row of bushes, discussing things.

But I did not come home yesterday to a stripped back yard.

We have another trip planned this weekend ... leaving this morning. So I **won't** be out there beating them to it.

I'm just going to be honest (and negative, for a minute, which I don't like to do) and say this makes me feel a tad bit pissed off at the _lack of understanding_ / _lack of give-a-shit_ / _or whatever it is_ that I am dealing with in a house with two full-grown men + one who's almost there + one who's capable of doing all of the things a kid can do to help.

You all understand the importance of my backyard sanctuary. Why has this not been important to anyone else in my family?

I think I'm more pissed off at myself than at anyone else. Because I probably should have put some Miracle-Gro on my balls awhile ago, and made everyone get off their asses and help me.

Off to google the correct usage of that product now ... everybody have a great day!

Yes [@drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/) since I’m already in the hall of shame for today with my early-morning-still-very-dark posting ... I’m going for #1 trophy by posting after dark, as well.

### Jenelle — 2019-10-21T15:31:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-280873>

So our trip this weekend was our first Airbnb experience ~ thanks to all who’ve talked about it here.

I think I might be an Airbnb addict now.

On Saturday ~ Cedar Point was (possibly?) the most packed it has ever been, due to the killer deal they offered on annual passes for the rest of this season and ALL of next year, as well.

We left our rental house — 5 minutes away from the entrance to CP — and in 1.5 hours, we drove 4 blocks. When my husband finally decided to turn around, park the truck, and WALK IN with the younger kids ... I said, I’ll just stay at the house. The older kids had gotten there earlier in the day & parked, no problem. So they could all ride home together and no one would have to walk home late at night in the dark. But there wasn’t going to be room for me.

BESIDES ... I had enjoyed myself Friday night, when it wasn’t so packed ... and I had NO desire to be in that crowd on Saturday. It got so crazy that they shut down the roads and didn’t allow anyone to enter for several hours. Ridiculous.

### Jenelle — 2019-10-21T15:51:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-280874>

This quiet time alone at the rental house allowed me to accomplish a couple of big things.

1) I found a kick-ass “pool house / guest house” on 25 acres ... not far from our home, where we can have the open house when our son graduates in June.

I messaged back and forth with the lady (on Airbnb, of course) and she is cool with everything I was picturing.

THIS PLACE IS BEAUTIFUL ... and now that stress I’ve had looming in the back of my mind, “where are we going to have it, what are we going to do if it rains” ~ etc etc ~ is GONE.

I was so excited on Sunday morning when I told everyone, I was like a kid at Christmas. Seriously. And ~ this place has a diving board ~ so that’s ALL my husband needs to know about it. Lol.

\*We are never going to want to leave.\*

2) I really liked the couch at the rental house, so I asked the guy about it. Put together my Big Lots coupons + a good sale going on ... ordered a nice big sectional for less than $600 TOTAL.

This was a much-needed purchase. I won’t bore everyone with the details — the point is — you need to have ROOM for everyone if you actually want your kids & their friends to hang out in the same room with you. Lol.

So my husband and I picked it up last night, and we are rearranging the house to accommodate it. It’s good. Good things are happening.

### Phosphene — 2019-10-21T15:53:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-280875>

We used to own some Cedar Point stock—probably should’ve held onto it. People will jut want to be more and more amused as we march in our current direction. (Though I do love a good roller coaster.)

So you had a day all alone in someone else’s clean house??? Bliss right?

### Phosphene — 2019-10-21T15:55:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-280876>

Posted too soon! Of course it was bliss...

We still have the same forest green leather sofa, loveseat, chair, ottoman set for the past 20 years. Getting time for something new but why? They’re still fine—just rough around the edges.

Hubs likes to remind me that it’s a LOVEseat.

### Jenelle — 2019-10-21T16:14:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-280879>

[@Phosphene](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19648/)  yes it was good to have the time alone ... in a clean, uncluttered space, where I didn't have a million things staring at me that needed to be done.

But it was WEIRD ~ not even having the dog with me. We ended up _(after renting a pet-friendly house on airbnb, lol)_ leaving him at home w/ middle son who hates scary things AND roller coasters ... middle son stayed the night with the neighbors across the street, but was able to go back and forth and take care of the dog.

It was weird.

And I **really** would have liked to go to the beach and watch the sunset and get pics ~ but I didn't want to go alone in a huge truck, on tiny streets, in a place I'm not familiar with. Besides -- the traffic. Did I mention the traffic?

I didn't ride any of the roller coasters on Friday night when I was there. I think I must have gotten that all out of my system when I was younger. I was really content just to be along, take pics of the kids ... watch the people ... and I got that ONE nice sunset picture.

Watching the people was, you know, actually sort of depressing. LOTS of alcohol going on at Halloweekends ... fine and dandy ... but there was clearly some who did not know when to stop. And who takes a newborn? I mean, seriously! It was freaking COLD.

### Jenelle — 2019-10-24T22:50:07+0300

Just got the official email HIRING me for the new position I'd posted about.
**
Wow** ... I'm so grateful and excited for this opportunity. It feels like a good fit.

### Jenelle — 2019-10-25T00:34:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-281215>

### Jenelle — 2019-10-25T01:01:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-281216>

Ok ~ I might have to post my deets in chunks. Because, kids.  And I think a lot about what I want to say.

First off — after applying, I was told to send a “short statement” about why I would be the “perfect” one for the job.

Well. I sent back a LONG statement — with bullet points — and told her {paraphrased, but close} “I cannot tell you that I am the perfect candidate for the position. And I’m not going to regurgitate the information that is already on my resume. But what I do want to tell you is this:”

And it took me a long time. Lol. Because I had done my research about her company before applying ... and because I put a lot of thought into my words. Especially when it’s something that is important to me, or something I am passionate about.

Well. She loved it.

She told me she was “blown away” ... and that everyone else was responding with buzz words, like “detail-oriented” etc. She said, “it was just so BOLD and like, out there!”

—

And that is because I am authentic. I am me, through and through ... take it or leave it, love me or leave me ... I cannot STAND fake, and I cannot pretend to be someone I’m not.

### Jenelle — 2019-10-25T02:02:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-281220>

So we arranged a phone call on my way home from work one day.

We talked for close to an hour.

And I was honest ... just really upfront about myself, and my job, what it’s like, what I do, what I value ... my strengths AND my weaknesses.

We talked about my husband’s back problems ... the catalyst that led me to nursing school. The missed work, the financial problems ~ all of it. Because you cannot accurately describe who you are today, without acknowledging where you’ve been.

So I put myself out there. And I slept well that night ... as I do every night ... knowing, at the end of the day, that I was the best me I could be. And what happens, happens.

### Phosphene — 2019-10-25T02:11:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-281222>

But the shoes! Which shoes??

### Jenelle — 2019-10-25T03:31:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-281227>

> [Phosphene said:](/goto/post?id=281222)
>
> But the shoes! Which shoes??

LMAO. Shoes in the morning!

### Jenelle — 2019-10-25T14:06:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-281238>

I think I didn’t just jump straight to the shoes, because I’m still processing the whole thing ... and writing it out here helps with that.

AND I know that people here, in \*this\* community, will understand and support me on this journey ... in a way that possibly no one else IRL will.

I made a strategic decision to become an RN. I went for the big bucks. And I quite purposefully dove into an area of nursing where I knew I would be in high demand — AND where I would have the most earning potential, straight out of school.

So I feel like everyone around me (my family) looks at me and sees $ signs now. They don’t see what I really do each day ~ and they can’t possibly relate to it. They don’t see the sacrifices I make, and the toll it takes on me.

I can say some things here ~ and know that others are sitting there on the other end, smiling and nodding, because \*they get it\*. Whereas people (some people) IRL would think I’ve lost my fucking mind.

### Phosphene — 2019-10-25T15:08:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-281240>

Yep I get it. The restaurant work I’m doing now earns about a third of my ‘potential’ and this is after several years of being little more than a vagabond leech in the eyes of others, while healing my mostly invisible ailments. I did have some odd jobs throuout but of course nothing that brought a SALARY and BENEFITS and SECURITY like the old days. I caught a lot of heat for it at first, but now that everyone is noticing the changes in me they accept it. And frankly these days I couldn’t care less if they didn’t. I got first hand experience of how content I could be living on very little at the ashram and other service exchanges. Once you do that it’s just like swallowing the red pill!

Of course I’m very lucky to have supportive family and friends. I never forget that. But I also don’t push hubs to earn more (although he could). Being content with what I have has been a huge life lesson through all of this.

Didn’t know about your husband's back trouble—I’m sure that was super stressful all around. Is he doing better now?

### Jenelle — 2019-10-25T15:25:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-281244>

This woman has created her own, successful, marketing company ... and zeroed in on a very specific niche in the marketplace. She recognized a need and an opportunity, and she went for it. I DIG THAT.

Her business has grown to a point where she needs an Executive Assistant / Office Manager. That is how the job was advertised.

So we met at Starbucks, and we talked for 3 hours. Really hit it off. We talked about all kinds of things ... getting to know each other, talking about what we each are picturing, and how this would work.

\- The office is in her home. In the country.

\- It’s close to where I live (vs the job I am driving an hour for now ... into the big city).

\- Hours are flexible. 20 hours per week ~ weekdays only.

\- She never wants to start before 9 am. We’ll be done by 4 pm. And if I need to structure that differently to accommodate picking up a kid from school (or whatever) ... that’s pretty much my call.

\- She understands that I am going to keep one foot in the nursing world, and that I work PRN (as needed) so my schedule will vary. And she’s cool with that.

\- There are times when she will be out of town, and I will work from home. If I am sick or have a sick child at home, she encouraged me to work from home on those days (if I can and want to) as well.

\- I talked to her about using my own computer with f.lux and/or installing it on my work computer. Yep, she wants me to be comfortable working. Whatever we need to do.

\- Full license to go outside and work if I want to. Wherever I feel I will be most productive.

\- We talked about the overstimulation from artificial lighting & how it drains you. She described her office as “a cave”. Lol. I haven’t been there yet ... so we’ll see!

\- She wants to grow her business, and she has a vision to expand internationally. She pictures this position to eventually become full-time.

\- Because this is a brand-new position \*we are creating\* ... she wants and VALUES my input.

\- We talked a lot about keeping communication open, and not letting things “get weird”. When you are working this closely, and one-on-one, that is VITAL.

I could go on. Lol. I’ll go ahead and post for now.

### Phosphene — 2019-10-25T15:40:43+0300

### Jenelle — 2019-10-25T17:44:55+0300

### Jenelle — 2019-10-25T17:58:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-281256>

> [Phosphene said:](/goto/post?id=281240)
>
> Didn’t know about your husband's back trouble—I’m sure that was super stressful all around. Is he doing better now?

After trying everything we could to avoid surgery ... seeing a chiropractor whom we love, but who looked at his MRI scan and said, "I can't fix this, he needs surgery" ... eventually having the surgery ~ then having a spinal fluid leak and constant, untreatable headache \*for weeks\* as a result of the surgery ... a surgeon who would not admit to any error, but who gladly put us up in a hotel and paid for the blood patch to fix it ... then it NOT being fixed, and having to have a **second** blood patch procedure to fix it for real ... and then a LONG healing process, after the surgeon's promise of: _"6 weeks, you'll be jumping out of airplanes, dodging bullets" ...
_
He's better.

It will never be the same. He will never feel like he's in his 20's or 30's again. He has to be careful.

But ~ this is life. And he chooses to remain in a career which is probably a very large part of what caused his problems to begin with. So. You know.

We grow, and we learn, and we move on.

### caroline — 2019-10-26T04:26:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-281276>

yes indeed .....grow and learn and move forward.

How will we know what it takes unless we get out there and beat the bushes.

Our life, our choices......we can't ever forget that.

Someone at the beach yesterday asked us how we come to live in this magic place. They are just visiting for a few days.

They asked us " so you have family here?" nope

" did you used to come often for your holidays?" nope

"are your family close by?" nope ......actually 1000 K away.

"did you have friends here?" nope, nope, nope

We acted on an impulse to drive up here...and our instincts took over. We bought a house about 60 seconds after looking at it.

We are meant to be here.......

### Inger — 2019-11-02T16:54:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-281699>

Love your new work Jenelle
I could not work as a nurse, even though I do went to nursing school 23 years ago. If I worked as a nurse, I would do like Drezy...LOL - drag the patients out in the sun, take off their clothes - and so I would be fired...

I work as something I have not even education at! Waitressing. I love it! Because I work a lot outside too, and I love the feeling and the people and everything. And I earn at least as much as a nurse would.
I do not care one bit if it is not something "special" or something that gives me a status. Ha! This is my life, this is your life  Let us do what makes us happy - what we really enjoy doing
and who knows what we will do tomorrow? I have no idea! Maybe something totally different? You can never know  But we are free to change, to try out and live, learn and love

### Saichi — 2019-11-04T03:54:04+0200

### caroline — 2019-11-05T03:16:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-281816>

I was standing in the ocean this morning, talking to a man we see pretty much every morning. We talked about how all the early morning folks at the beach/ocean are so much healthier . He told me about a doctor on the sunshine coast who hands out prescriptions for early morning sunshine to his patients ...and tells them to get out side and enjoy!

We are often speechless when we see new families arrive on the beach, obviously on vacation. They are absolutely pure white! How can you live in this climate and be pure white??????? That can only mean that Mom and Dad and the kids never go outside to do anything at all.

We generally find we have no impact on our friends and family and neighbours with our lifestyle.....they think we are nuts and generally ridicule us.

We had a great discussion this morning with our friend about how good early morning sunshine is for us and how medicine has made no inroads into breast cancer and prostrate cancer etc. He also thought wearing sunscreen was harmful, and to be really healthy we have to be outdoors as much as we can.

### caroline — 2019-11-05T04:26:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-281820>

I did tell our friend to "get his junk out in the sun" he didn't blink an eyelash!

### Jenelle — 2019-11-05T14:00:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-281830>

I started my new job yesterday, and it was good. Really good.

Since the time changed, especially ~ I was able to sit and enjoy sunrise for awhile while kids were getting ready for school ... and give them hugs and see them off to school.

Then I drove to my new workplace ... a very nice farmhouse, about 20 minutes out \*in the country\* on 20 acres.

I was promptly greeted by two beautiful Irish setters, who welcomed me fully by washing my glasses for me with their (noses? tongues?) Lol ... I’m not sure, it all happened so quickly.

Perhaps I sound a tad superstitious when I say ~ don’t want to say too much at this point ~ “don’t want to jinx it” ... or perhaps that’s just a sign to you all that I’ve found something that could really, really mean a lot to me.

### Jenelle — 2019-11-11T19:36:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-282114>

I have not regretted my decision to quit school -- not at all -- _not for a second_.

I'm thankful to all who encourage me each day to follow my heart ~ whether it is talking to me directly, or just by sharing your own stories here.

I'm reading books now _because I want to, WHEN I want to_ ... and I've been working my way through one by Gregory Berns called How Dogs Love Us: A Neuroscientist and His Adopted Dog Decode the Canine Brain.

This caught my attention because {I have a dog and} the kids and I watched a documentary several years ago, which was **excellent** \-- very thought-provoking -- and I'm pretty sure we didn't even have a dog at the time. I think it was Nova: Dogs Decoded.

All of this to say ~ we've added another member to our family. This is Winnie ~ which means a lot of really cool things, if you look it up, but most importantly: _first-born daughter_.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

We picked her up Saturday night, then I had to work on Sunday while she settled in with the rest of the family.

They spent a lot of time outside with her, and got some really good pics and videos.

What does Rocket think? Well ... it's a process. Lol. I think they're going to be good friends.

### drezy — 2019-11-11T21:00:19+0200

Rocket's face says: "So... When does this other dog go home?"

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=281276)

>

>

>
> Someone at the beach yesterday asked us how we come to live in this magic place. They are just visiting for a few days.
>
> They asked us " so you have family here?" nope
>
> " did you used to come often for your holidays?" nope
>
> "are your family close by?" nope ......actually 1000 K away.
>
> "did you have friends here?" nope, nope, nope
>

>
> We are meant to be here.......

This is my experience here in Florida too. "Why are you HERE?!" They act like it's hell or something... lol This is where I have been placed after being open to the change and I embrace it.

### LieselK — 2019-11-12T16:23:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-282150>

An awesome new job AND a puppy?! All wonderful! In reading through your recent posts I can see your boldness and strength shining through. It's really inspiring!

### caroline — 2019-11-13T03:13:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-282181>

we all need boldness and strength and joy to move forward ....and follow our intuition and instincts.

We all have so much to look forward to......we are a very lucky bunch of people, but then, we followed our intuition and landed here.

We are all amazing in our own special ways.

### Jenelle — 2019-11-16T21:23:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-282388>

Winnie had her first snow on Monday ... and I'm here posting about it on Saturday ... Lol. It's been a busy (and awesome) week!

I recently was reminded _the hard way_ not to put all of my eggs in one basket. In this case, it was losing all of the pictures on my phone from the past couple of years. Damn you, phones and technology ... _damn you._ It was ~ and continues to be ~ a hard lesson, indeed.

So I'm trying to make sure I at least preserve my favorite pictures, in multiple ways now. I even ordered some good-old PRINTS the other day ... it has been \*way\* too long since I'd done that. And they are incredibly cheap (or even free) if you can find the right deals.

I might even find the time to put them in an album!

### Jenelle — 2019-11-24T14:51:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-282726>

I think this pretty much sums it up.

### Phosphene — 2019-11-24T15:07:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-282728>

Awwwww...

Friendly creatures make everything better. We need them more than ever now to show us the way back.

What the heck are those creatures behind the furry ones? Friendly?

### Jenelle — 2019-11-24T17:09:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-282737>

Remember I got a good deal on that new couch? Then we had to cover the whole thing in an assortment of blankets. She likes to chew and scratch. She’s like a kitten at this point. LOL. So that is a guardians of the galaxy blanket.

Speaking of guardians of the galaxy, if you haven’t seen the movie, at least listen to the soundtrack. It has some really good ones on there!

### Jenelle — 2020-03-31T16:03:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-288186>

Emerging From Darkness ... again. 4 months have gone by since I last posted here ~ that's interesting.

I do know exactly what occurred at that time in my life when I stopped posting. [@Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/) talks about the build-up of trauma, and how it affects us ~ even when we are not aware of it. Even when we refuse to acknowledge it. This is after I recently described on Sean's journal the "dam" I've created in my mind ... holding things back ... holding things back ...

It's a reality that I could die soon. And that's not overly dramatic. Even without this virus circulating ~ it's a **reality** that all of us - any of us - could die soon. Today, I choose not to - at any point - curl up on my couch in a ball of depression.

I watched 7 minutes of the video the other day - the guy going on and on (and on) about "fuck you" - fuck the virus, fuck this, fuck that, fuck all of it. I really couldn't believe that it went on for another 11 minutes after I stopped watching ... I got the message loud and clear after just a few minutes. Lol. He's so angry. So, so angry. There's an anger in me, yes ~ but there is something else, much larger. Today, I choose to focus on that.

### Phosphene — 2020-03-31T19:38:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-288192>

I’ve been off the forum for a few months as well. Combination of factors but I do still care very much about members of this community.

Been thinking of you and your selfless and now treacherous vocation. I know all about The Dam. Stay safe!

### caroline — 2020-04-01T03:31:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/post-288209>

Thinking of all of us..... be safe and continue doing what you do best.

# Thread: Ladies seafood and menopause......and the leptin receptor
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-seafood-and-menopause-and-the-leptin-receptor.21285/>
**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2018-05-05T01:45:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-seafood-and-menopause-and-the-leptin-receptor.21285/post-240525>

Oily fish seem to delay menopause, refined rice, and pasta advance menopause. DHA is found to be highest in the central retinal pathways connecting the sun to retina and retina to hypothalamus and pituitary where all hormones are made. So when some idiot tells you not to eat fish they just told you you should ignore their advice. More proof that the Vermont 2017 youtube video is real. [http://jech.bmj.com/content/early/2018/04/10/jech-2017-209887](http://jech.bmj.com/content/early/2018/04/10/jech-2017-209887)

### ZoeSanderson — 2020-07-20T05:26:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-seafood-and-menopause-and-the-leptin-receptor.21285/post-291091>

Wow, I heard it on TV show, but I didn't even think about it being true. The funny thing is that I just love to eat fish and this is very bad news for me. In any case, I do my best to avoid the rapid onset of menopause. I really love having sex and I do not want to lose sensitivity and sex drive after menopause ... I already talked about this with my doctor and he said that I can get tested regularly in order to monitor the level of estrogen and take action in case of a decrease! I use tests from [https://www.myhealthconfirm.com/product/menopause-plus/](https://www.myhealthconfirm.com/product/menopause-plus/) and they are the best on the market!

### drezy — 2018-05-14T15:06:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-might-one-mitohack-the-power-grid.21288/post-241159>

> [Neil Taylor said:](/goto/post?id=241155)
>
> I read this article but others seem to suggest UVA is a bell curve like UVB throughout the day e.g. [http://www.hko.gov.hk/m/article\_e.htm?title=ele\_00122](http://www.hko.gov.hk/m/article_e.htm?title=ele_00122)
>
> [http://www.hko.gov.hk/m/article\_e.htm?title=ele\_00122](http://www.hko.gov.hk/m/article_e.htm?title=ele_00122)

It might be worth asking the folks over at UK AIR. They have nice daily UV index graphs at the link below.

Pros:
Their sensors may be pretty close to you with sites in London, Leeds, Swansea, and many others

Cons:
For the public data and graphing they are unfortunately not separating out UVA and UVB. If their sensors are doing the erythemal modeling then this is not the answer you're looking for, but....

Possibilities:
They may have the data but think it's over the heads of the average Joe Pint (assuming that's the British equivalent of Joe Six-pack here in the States). So you might want to ask them. It seems hard to believe that the sensors do all the erythemal calculations. Boffins, a term that just never caught on here, love to calculate and change models regularly.

[https://uk-air.defra.gov.uk/data/uv-index-graphs](https://uk-air.defra.gov.uk/data/uv-index-graphs)

Good luck

### JanSz — 2018-05-14T16:20:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-might-one-mitohack-the-power-grid.21288/post-241167>

> [Neil Taylor said:](/goto/post?id=241040)
>
> From the article "AM sun when the UVA IRA transition occurs." roughly what angle is the sun when this transition occurs?

So far both charts are dealing with UVA and UVB.
Lets go back to the original question.
**UVA IRA transition
**
When we have Dawn or Dusk?
It is the time when we see the light but we do not see sun (directly).
I would say that the moment of transition is seen best when the Dawn ends,
at the beach on East Coast,
when we see the first direct sun ray.

Opposite for Dusk (Left coast).
Dusk starts when there is still light but sun is below horizon.

............
So the answer to question:
" roughly what angle is the sun when this transition occurs?
which came from reading this (written by [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) ):

\=======

**It is the moment of sun rise.**
.............

Normally we are exposing our selves to sunlight for longer than a fleeting moment.
So, I think, that Jack meant that it is good to see morning light before we can see sun.
Specially for those suffering from EHS.
..............

### WalterNL — 2018-05-15T16:07:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-might-one-mitohack-the-power-grid.21288/post-241230>

I stumbled upon this paper about the importance of IR-A in the AM, quite interesting: [https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1011134415300713](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1011134415300713)

Given that IR has the longest wavelength of light that passes through the ozone layer, I assume it's present right after sunrise (haven't found a confirmation of this though). The ratio of UVA / IR-A will then slowly change as UVA shows up within an hour or so.

> I think it may possibly help to define sunlight and sunset, first.
> I was trying to do that here:
> [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...ne-mitohack-the-power-grid.21288/#post-241167](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/how-might-one-mitohack-the-power-grid.21288/#post-241167)

I agree it's best to have clarification from Dr. Kruse.

I'm only thinking out loud. IR-A has longer waves than visible light. UV-A has shorter waves than visible light. Given that they need to travel a longer distance at sunrise/sunset, the shorter wavelengths scatter to a higher degree than the longer wavelength radiation. Sunrise appears red and orange because they have longer wavelengths. So however long the transition is for a certain zipcode and season, IR should hit Earth's surface first, then visible (red, orange, yellow, green, blue, violet), then UV.

So it looks like the
Transition when we see IRA only and UVA is still absent
is (definitely) when we see red sun.
And for little bit longer, minutes, when sun gets brighter.

Not sure how to classify the time before we see first ray of sun's disk.
But that light, likely also affects our pineal gland.

### WalterNL — 2018-05-16T10:15:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-might-one-mitohack-the-power-grid.21288/post-241325>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=241298)
>
>
>
> So it looks like the
> Transition when we see IRA only and UVA is still absent
> is (definitely) when we see red sun.
> And for little bit longer, minutes, when sun gets brighter.
>
> Not sure how to classify the time before we see first ray of sun's disk.
> But that light, likely also affects our pineal gland.
>
> /

The odd thing is that we can see sunlight before it breaks the horizon. The actual sunrise is also before the sun breaks the horizon due to atmospheric refraction, where the rays don't follow a straight path.

There's also something interesting here: [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blue\_hour](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blue_hour)
Apparently there is a small time window before sunrise, where the sky appears blue because those short wavelengths scatter in the atmosphere, while long red rays go through the atmosphere into space. So there's a definite relevance of the angle of sunlight that make the difference whether certain rays reach Earth's surface or not.

# Thread: Effect of pomegranate extract on vagina, skeleton, metabolic and endocrine profiles
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/effect-of-pomegranate-extract-on-vagina-skeleton-metabolic-and-endocrine-profiles.21289/>

### Scompy — 2018-05-06T06:16:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/effect-of-pomegranate-extract-on-vagina-skeleton-metabolic-and-endocrine-profiles.21289/post-240606>

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/29603520](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/29603520)

**CONCLUSION:**
In rats fed with pomegranate extract, estradiol levels increased and tibial bone cortex thickness and vaginal epithelium thickness also increased. Pomegranate itself or its formulation extracts may be a support or an alternative to the main treatment modalities in the preservation of bone density and the treatment of vaginal epithelial atrophy.

Interesting - pomegranates are a polyphenol which have a benzene ring - aromatic - they donate hydrogen - I wonder if it is deuterium depleted since it's gone through photosynthesis or is the tree storing its deuterium in the fruit - so like ascorbic acid, beta carotene, alpha-tocopherol (vitamin e), since the benzene ring is a photon trap - it said something about a pi electron cloud in there - this is why this stuff is ok to eat in the sun but not in blue light - they absorb light - obviously there are vast gaps in my knowledge base here, but I think that's why the latest FB post on vitamin E said it works in the sunlight to help eyesight, but is a fail in artificial light...

[https://www.mdpi.com/1420-3049/14/6/2202/pdf](https://www.google.com/url?sa=t&rct=j&q=&esrc=s&source=web&cd=16&ved=2ahUKEwiGlMfuk_XeAhVFYKwKHV9LAE4QFjAPegQIERAC&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mdpi.com%2F1420-3049%2F14%2F6%2F2202%2Fpdf&usg=AOvVaw2bE4nigojvTfXc7MKfAjYo)

Hey, did you know they had organic LED lights? While attempting to fathom rings and the Stokes shift I came across this lovely treatise...

[http://depts.washington.edu/cmditr/modules/oled/light\_emitting\_electrochemical\_processes.html](http://depts.washington.edu/cmditr/modules/oled/light_emitting_electrochemical_processes.html)

### drezy — 2018-11-29T02:53:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/effect-of-pomegranate-extract-on-vagina-skeleton-metabolic-and-endocrine-profiles.21289/post-256887>

> [Penny said:](/goto/post?id=256886)

>

The engineering me is a little impressed with them.

They are not grass fed FWIW.

My wife went to school with the owner a Chinese OLED manufacturing company. He's both the chillest and the smokiest(cigarette) person I've met in the last decade.

### Penny — 2018-11-29T21:43:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/effect-of-pomegranate-extract-on-vagina-skeleton-metabolic-and-endocrine-profiles.21289/post-256984>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=256887)

> The engineering me is a little impressed with them.
>
> They are not grass fed FWIW.
>
> My wife went to school with the owner a Chinese OLED manufacturing company. He's both the chillest and the smokiest(cigarette) person I've met in the last decade.

So, the Stokes shift means that when any wave hits an atom that absorbs light then emits it (absorption/emmisivity), the light that is then emitted is less than the light captured... so say vitamin A blasts vitamin B12, does that mean B12 then emits light when it's supposed to keep it? What does this mean?

Of course, today I am still reeling from that chromaphore mitochondria respiratory proteins being destroyed by vitamin A thing... Christ, just when you don't think it can get any worse...

### drezy — 2018-11-30T02:28:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/effect-of-pomegranate-extract-on-vagina-skeleton-metabolic-and-endocrine-profiles.21289/post-257012>

> [Penny said:](/goto/post?id=256984)
>
> So, the Stokes shift means that when any wave hits an atom that absorbs light then emits it (absorption/emmisivity), the light that is then emitted is less than the light captured... so say vitamin A blasts vitamin B12, does that mean B12 then emits light when it's supposed to keep it? What does this mean?
>
> Of course, today I am still reeling from that chromaphore mitochondria respiratory proteins being destroyed by vitamin A thing... Christ, just when you don't think it can get any worse...

I encourage everyone to get the basics of [stokes shifts](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stokes_shift) as well as the [Planck–Einstein relation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Planck–Einstein_relation).

It's there where It's unavoidable that something funny is going on.

# Thread: Menstruation & The Lunar Cycle (Light)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/menstruation-the-lunar-cycle-light.21290/>
**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ScottishEmma — 2018-05-06T08:52:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/menstruation-the-lunar-cycle-light.21290/post-240609>

I know this has been mentioned somewhere before but I'm trying to wrap my head around the light story here...

As I understand it, correct me if I'm wrong, females ovulate with the full moon and bleed on the new moon.

I know for me, this is my current cycle but I have been known to bleed and ovulate at different times throughout the months or even switch to bleed on the full. (My son was born on a full moon)

But as I understood it, the preference for mating was full moon breeding and new moon bleeding.

Anyhow, I read something online yesterday - certainly not scientific - that said that the advent of electricity threw this whole system into dissarray as women were receiving different light signals.

We are meant to pay attention to the increasing blue light at the full moon to signal our bodies, but the light at night has fucked it all up.

My question is, really, how does the altering blue light of the moon affect female biology? Is there a link anyone has where I can find out more about this?

In the online world where I hang out there are a lot of women wanting to 'sync' with the moon. I'm sure switching off light at night would help with this - as well as grounding and looking at the moon when it's out - but I don't want to start spouting that without something legit to fall back on...

Thanks

### ScottishEmma — 2018-05-10T11:03:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/menstruation-the-lunar-cycle-light.21290/post-240934>

I found this: [http://journals.sagepub.com/doi/pdf/10.1177/1099800407303337](http://journals.sagepub.com/doi/pdf/10.1177/1099800407303337)

Now interested whether light plays a role in the onset of menopause....

### ScottishEmma — 2018-05-10T11:05:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/menstruation-the-lunar-cycle-light.21290/post-240935>

"Researchers have found evidence of the effects of season on the activity of the pituitary–ovarian axis and the pineal gland. Kauppila, Kivela, Pakarinen, and Vakkuri (1987) reported an inverse seasonal relationship between melatonin and estradiol secretion observed in 32 Finnish women. The dark season (i.e., winter) was characterized by prolonged and increased melatonin secretion and decreased ovarian and androgenic activities. To further evaluate this association, Kivela, Kauppila, Ylostalo, Vakkuri, and Leppaluoto (1988) measured daytime and nighttime concentrations of serum melatonin, follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH), LH, and prolactin by radioimmunoassay on various days of the menstrual cycle during summer (average daylight 22 hr) and winter (average daylight 5 hr) in healthy women (N = 12) from northern Finland. Kivela and colleagues suggest that in winter, the high levels of melatonin in the follicular phase have an inhibitory effect on the serum LH levels. In summer, the melatonin levels are lower and perhaps less inhibitory on the secretion of LH."

So in Winter we shouldn't be copulating? Or rather, nature doesn't accommodate it so readily?

### ScottishEmma — 2018-05-10T11:07:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/menstruation-the-lunar-cycle-light.21290/post-240936>

> [ScottishEmma said:](/goto/post?id=240935)
>
> "Researchers have found evidence of the effects of season on the activity of the pituitary–ovarian axis and the pineal gland. Kauppila, Kivela, Pakarinen, and Vakkuri (1987) reported an inverse seasonal relationship between melatonin and estradiol secretion observed in 32 Finnish women. The dark season (i.e., winter) was characterized by prolonged and increased melatonin secretion and decreased ovarian and androgenic activities. To further evaluate this association, Kivela, Kauppila, Ylostalo, Vakkuri, and Leppaluoto (1988) measured daytime and nighttime concentrations of serum melatonin, follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH), LH, and prolactin by radioimmunoassay on various days of the menstrual cycle during summer (average daylight 22 hr) and winter (average daylight 5 hr) in healthy women (N = 12) from northern Finland. Kivela and colleagues suggest that in winter, the high levels of melatonin in the follicular phase have an inhibitory effect on the serum LH levels. In summer, the melatonin levels are lower and perhaps less inhibitory on the secretion of LH."
>
>
> So in Winter we shouldn't be copulating? Or rather, nature doesn't accommodate it so readily?

Nope, it's saying darkness helped infertile women to conceive. I need to read in to this a lot more. So fascinating.

### ScottishEmma — 2018-05-10T11:08:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/menstruation-the-lunar-cycle-light.21290/post-240937>

"Taken together, these studies suggest that ambient light has effects on the activity of the pituitary– ovarian axis and the pineal gland, but the threshold is unknown. Melatonin secretion is likely altered in amplitude and duration. However, it is unknown to what degree these changes affect ovulatory functioning."

"Many menstruating women experience psychological or somatic symptoms during the luteal phase of the menstrual cycle. Treatments such as bright-light therapy, which may act to increase brain serotonin levels and production in addition to suppressing melatonin secretion, may represent alternatives to selective serotonin reuptake inhibitors, oral contraceptive pills, or a myriad of other therapies. "

### Sue-UK — 2018-05-11T18:34:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/menstruation-the-lunar-cycle-light.21290/post-241039>

Hi Emma

I found this on the light frequencies of full moon [http://www.olino.org/us/articles/2015/10/05/spectrum-of-moon-light](http://www.olino.org/us/articles/2015/10/05/spectrum-of-moon-light). After sunset, the yellow frequencies drop right down, so its interesting the ratio of yellow in a full moon. If human females rely on the moon to sync their reproductive cycle because its compressed into a month, with a 2 hour hibernation period every night... as well as the blue/melatonin link, I think the ratio of yellow in artificial light could potentially disrupt the moon transition signals, by simulating a full moon chronically.  Moon phase evening glasses anyone?

### DianGisnbergMD — 2018-07-02T16:06:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/menstruation-the-lunar-cycle-light.21290/post-244753>

The COMT enzyme controls the off load of both estrogen and the balance of your nerurotransmitters. In the luteal phase you have high hormone surges and this enzyme is stressed. If you have a SNP in the enzyme more issues can be possible if your circadian rhythms are off. Remember menses are controlled by the central nervous system Supra Chiasmatic Nucleus - it tells the ovaries what to do. Keep the cycle in sync - watch the diet- know your COMT- and the mood menses balance will follow.

The amount of oxygen is a function of how much UV light the skin and eye SENSE from the environment.

BH4 is part of the methylation pathways for neurotransmitters, like dopamine. COMT pathways are quite important for those with sleep disorders (bad autophagy and apoptosis) and low DHEA levels. People forget autophagy and apoptosis are the ONLY two self regulatory change programs for mitochondria.. DNA has a massively more complex and higher fidelity system. When sleep is poor it means so is autophagy and apoptosis. AM sunlight is what ruins sleep because this is how melatonin is made and melatonin is what controls the two mito programs via its ability to regulate mtDNA.

BH4 is needed to synthesize serotonin, dopamine, conversion of phenylalanine to tyrosine and language-related function (autism connection). The Vermont 2017 and 2018 talks really get into these aromatic amino acids and the QT series goes next level on this topic.

The A1298C mutation in the MTHFR gene may impact levels of BH4 when you are (-,-) and live in a shit environment for light.

Remember BH4 is a WATER soluble B like vitamin so exogenous estrogens deplete BH4 too!!! the water it needs it DDW from the matrix made by what? Solar exposure. That is how redox is built.

Vitamin B6 is required for the production of serotonin and melatonin. QT series alert again. Serotonin gets converted to melatonin in the pineal gland in darkness, which is why a B6 deficiency MAY lead to sleep problems when you subtract sunlight.

Simultaneously IL −6 rises in the brain to destroy melatonin and DHEA signaling when the melanopsin system is altered to lower Vitamin A. Why? You cannot make melatonin or DHEA without Vitamin A because of the blue light hazard.

Here the B vitamins of the MTHFR pathways overlap with COMT pathways because of altered light we allow. Sleep problems in these women are often diagnosed and treated with Rx drugs, when a B6 supplement is raely effective when the AM sun is missing and the real cause of the problem. This is the biggest mistake I see in functional and allopathic medicine in women. Tyrosine, another aromatic amino acids is a precursor for the production of dopamine and norepinephrine, which are involved in a vast array of brain functions, including motivation, stress, cognition, and arousal.................all mass equivalence issues....................ALL have to be programmed by what to work? SUN.

That is what the QT series SAYS.

Read the damn blogs.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-05-06T21:17:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-are-using-light-bulbs-with-kids-here-is-a-reminder.21291/post-240651>

The most painful thing is losing yourself in the process of trying to help someone too much and forgetting that you are special too. You are good for no one unless you are good for you. Make sure you change all light bulbs frequently if you are using them because it cut back on the flicker effect. If you really like yourself keep all fake light off of your eyes and skin because of the melanopsin/Vitamin cycles that control the circadian mechanism in your cells. [https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/flicker-effect-light-jack-kruse/?published=t](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/flicker-effect-light-jack-kruse/?published=t)
